Description: Asham Meads is a tranquil nature reserve near Shabbington. It comprises two water meadows beside the River Thame, featuring diverse grassland, ancient hedgerows, and a small woodland. The site supports a wide range of flora and fauna.

Name: Ashberry
Description: Ashberry Nature Reserve is a small woodland, home to ancient trees, wildflowers, and a variety of birds and mammals. Located near Rievaulx Abbey, it features a stream and diverse flora, supporting a rich ecosystem. The site is managed for conservation.

Name: Ashculm Turbary
Description: Ashculm Turbary is a small, species-rich culm grassland site in Devon, managed by the Devon Wildlife Trust. It features wet rush pasture and ancient oak pollards, supporting a diverse range of flora and fauna, including rare butterflies. The reserve is an important example of culm grassland habitat.

Name: Ashes Pasture (Orchid Capital)
Description: Ashes Pasture is a Yorkshire Wildlife Trust nature reserve in the Yorkshire Dales. The site features diverse calcareous grassland, limestone pavement, and supports various orchid species including early purple and common spotted. It is home to many wildflowers and insects.

Name: Ashlawn Cutting
Description: Ashlawn Cutting is a Site of Special Scientific Interest (SSSI) near Rugby, Warwickshire. This former railway cutting features rich grassland, supporting diverse wildflowers like pyramidal and bee orchids. It is also home to various invertebrates, including brown argus and marbled white butterflies.

Name: Ashleworth Ham
Description: Ashleworth Ham is a large floodplain grassland by the River Severn. It supports diverse wetland birds, especially in winter when floods occur. Lapwing and snipe are present. Traditional hay meadows and wet pastures are managed for wildlife.

Name: Ashley Wood
Description: Ashley Wood is an ancient woodland and hazel coppice in Dorset, part of a wider network of nature reserves. Featuring oak, ash, and field maple, it supports dormice and diverse bird species. Paths allow visitors to explore this ecologically important site.

Name: Ashridge Estate (Ancient Trees & Chilterns Heath)
Description: From rolling chalk hills to ancient woodlands, the Ashridge Estate boasts a diverse landscape and a rich history. Covering 5,000 acres, it supports varied wildlife, including fallow deer, and features significant ancient trees. Visitors explore diverse habitats.

Name: Ashridge Estate (Nature)
Description: Ashridge Estate encompasses 5,000 acres of woodland, chalk downland, and open commons within the Chiltern Hills. This expansive site in Hertfordshire provides habitats for diverse wildlife, including a deer herd. Visitors can access numerous trails for walking, cycling, and horse riding.

Name: Ashtead Common
Description: Ashtead Common is a 200-hectare ancient woodland in Surrey, managed by the City of London Corporation. A National Nature Reserve and SSSI, it features historic wood pasture, veteran trees, and diverse wildlife, offering public walking trails.

Name: Ashtead Rye Meadows Wetlands
Description: Ashtead Rye Meadows Wetlands is a flood-plain grassland and wetland habitat situated on the River Mole in Surrey. The reserve supports a variety of wetland plants and bird species. Managed by Surrey Wildlife Trust, it offers accessible public footpaths for visitors.

Name: Ashton's Meadow
Description: Ashton's Meadow is a valuable wet grassland and fen habitat in the Erewash Valley, supporting a diverse range of plant species and invertebrates. It forms part of a larger network of local wildlife sites managed for biodiversity.

Name: Ashton's Meadow (Wildflower Hay Meadow)
Description: Ashton's Meadow is a species-rich hay meadow near Bingham, Nottinghamshire. It supports green-winged orchids, cowslips, and adder’s tongue fern. Managed by the Nottinghamshire Wildlife Trust, the site is mown for hay then grazed, attracting diverse insects.

Name: Ashwell Quarry and Quarry Springs
Description: Ashwell Quarry is a former chalk quarry, now a geological SSSI, featuring grassland and woodland habitats. Quarry Springs is a spring-fed wet woodland with a pond and stream, supporting diverse plant and invertebrate life. These Hertfordshire sites provide wildlife-rich environments.

Name: Astley Moss
Description: Astley Moss is a restored lowland raised peat bog, part of the Manchester Mosses Special Area of Conservation. It supports rare bog flora and fauna, including cottongrass and sphagnum mosses, with ongoing conservation efforts.

Name: Astonbury Wood
Description: Astonbury Wood features ancient hornbeam and oak woodland with a hazel understorey, alongside meadows and ponds. Key species include bluebells, wood anemones, primroses, and wild garlic. The site supports diverse birds and invertebrates, forming a mosaic of habitats.

Name: Attenborough Nature Reserve (Flagship Wetland)
Description: Attenborough Nature Reserve is a Site of Special Scientific Interest near Nottingham. It covers 360 acres of lakes, wet grassland, and scrub, created from former gravel pits. The reserve is home to over 250 species of birds and a diverse range of other wildlife. It features a modern visitor centre with facilities for learning about local ecology and conservation efforts.

Name: Attingham Park
Description: Attingham Park is an elegant Regency mansion and historic parkland near Shrewsbury, Shropshire. The estate features a deer park, walled garden, and an impressive collection of art and furniture. Visitors can explore the mansion, grounds, and play areas, offering a rich historical experience.

Name: Attingham Park (Nature Recovery Project)
Description: Attingham Park implements a nature recovery project, restoring natural processes across its Shropshire estate. Initiatives include rewilding areas, enhancing biodiversity, and creating diverse habitats for wildlife. The project focuses on long-term ecological resilience.

Name: Aubrey Buxton Nature Reserve
Description: Aubrey Buxton Nature Reserve features a mosaic of ancient woodland, heathland, and acid grassland. The diverse habitats support a variety of wildlife, including nightingales and adders, offering tranquil paths for visitors.

Name: Aughrim Quarry (Heather Transplant Pilot)
Description: Aughrim Quarry, near Belfast, is a former quarry site where a heather transplant pilot project was undertaken in 2017. Volunteers helped clear scrub to restore habitats. This project, part of a larger Landscape Partnership scheme, aimed to re-establish native heather in the area.

Name: Aughton Woods
Description: Aughton Woods is an ancient woodland, wet woodland, and reedbed mosaic by the River Lune, supporting diverse wildlife like otters and kingfishers. Part of the Lune Estuary SSSI, it provides a vital habitat link.

Name: Augill Pasture (Lead Mill Meadows)
Description: Augill Pasture, also known as Lead Mill Meadows, is a traditional hay meadow and unimproved pasture in the Augill Beck valley. It supports diverse meadow flowers like globe-flower and lady's-mantle, and is a designated Coronation Meadow. The site includes ancient semi-natural woodland.

Name: Ausewell Wood (Dartmoor)
Description: Ausewell Wood is a large ancient woodland in the Dart Valley, bordering Dartmoor National Park. It features diverse habitats including oak and conifer woodland, open heathland, and riverside meadows, hosting rare wildlife.

Name: Aushaw Moss (Upland Peatland)
Description: Aushaw Moss is an upland peatland restoration site in the South Pennines, managed by Lancashire Wildlife Trust. It supports diverse wildlife. Conservation efforts focus on re-establishing sphagnum moss and improving water quality. Public access is limited.

Name: Avebury (Neolithic Landscape & Grassland)
Description: Avebury is a Neolithic henge monument and stone circle complex in Wiltshire, featuring large standing stones, ancient earthworks, and processional avenues. This archaeological landscape showcases early human settlement and rituals, managed by the National Trust.

Name: Aversley Wood
Description: Aversley Wood is an ancient woodland in Cambridgeshire, managed by the Woodland Trust. It features oak, ash, and hazel coppice, supporting diverse ground flora. Public footpaths provide access. Parking is available.

Name: Avon Woods (Bristol Fringe)
Description: Avon Woods, near Bristol, features a blend of ancient woodland and more recent plantings, forming part of the city's green fringe. Visitors can explore various walking paths and observe local wildlife. Mature oak and ash trees are prominent throughout the site.

Name: Axmouth to Lyme Regis Undercliffs NNR
Description: A unique coastal landslip wilderness in Dorset and East Devon, part of the Jurassic Coast World Heritage Site. Rich in diverse flora and fauna, the reserve features a dynamic landscape of ancient woodlands, scrub, and grasslands formed by historic geological movements.

Name: Aykley Wood (Durham County Council)
Description: Aykley Wood is an ancient semi-natural woodland and local nature reserve in Durham. It features a significant number of mature oak trees, providing an important green space and habitat for wildlife within the city.

Name: Ayr Gorge Woodlands
Description: Ayr Gorge Woodlands is a diverse nature reserve in Ayrshire, Scotland. It features ancient woodlands, river cliffs, and a meandering section of the River Ayr. The site supports varied wildlife and offers walking trails.

Name: Baal Hill Wood
Description: Baal Hill Wood is a County Durham nature reserve managed by Durham Wildlife Trust. It comprises ancient semi-natural woodland, conifer plantations, and a pond. The site supports roe deer, woodpeckers, and diverse invertebrates. Public footpaths cross the reserve.

Name: Babbacombe & Oddicombe Cliffs
Description: Babbacombe and Oddicombe Cliffs form a scenic stretch of coastline in South Devon. Public footpaths offer expansive views over Lyme Bay. The National Trust maintains these steep coastal habitats for wildlife and public access.

Name: Babbington Meadows (East Mendip Restoration)
Description: Babbington Meadows is a species-rich grassland reserve featuring a stream, diverse wildflowers, and insect life on carboniferous limestone. It is one of two reserves forming the East Mendip Restoration Area, working to connect fragmented habitats. Grazing maintains its meadow habitat.

Name: Babcary Meadows
Description: Babcary Meadows is a Somerset Wildlife Trust nature reserve located between Babcary and Foddington. It features two fields of unimproved neutral grassland, rich in wildflowers and various grasses. The site supports diverse insect life, including bees and butterflies, and attracts birds like linnets. Management includes an annual hay cut and grazing.

Name: Babcary Meadows (Somerset Wildlife Trust)
Description: Babcary Meadows is a nature reserve in Somerset, managed by Somerset Wildlife Trust. The site features flower-rich meadows, ancient hedgerows, and a small pond, supporting diverse plant and insect life.

Name: Backstone Bank
Description: Backstone Bank nature reserve features ancient woodlands, wildflower-rich grasslands, and limestone cliffs. Located in County Durham, it supports diverse plant and animal species including rare orchids and woodland birds. The site offers walking trails and panoramic views of Weardale.

Name: Backwarden
Description: Backwarden is a tranquil woodland in the Dengie Peninsula, supporting diverse bird species like woodpeckers and nuthatches. Mature oak, ash, and field maple trees provide habitat. Maintained walking trails offer public access for wildlife observation.

Name: Bacombe Hill (Chalk Grassland)
Description: Bacombe Hill, part of a SSSI, features chalk grassland with marbled white and dark green fritillary butterflies. Green woodpeckers and badgers inhabit the area. Wildflowers like chalk milkwort also thrive.

Name: Baddesley Clinton (Forest of Arden Core)
Description: Baddesley Clinton is a moated manor house, home to the Ferrers family for 500 years. The site features priest holes, a walled garden, and ancient woodland walks, offering insights into its rich history and architecture.

Name: Baddesley Common & Bentley Park Wood
Description: Baddesley Common and Bentley Park Wood features ancient woodland, species-rich grassland, ponds, and hedgerows. It provides habitats for diverse flora and fauna, including rare plants and insects, within Warwickshire. Managed by Warwickshire Wildlife Trust.

Name: Badgeworth Buttercup Reserve (The Genetic Ark)
Description: Badgeworth Buttercup Reserve is a small Gloucestershire Wildlife Trust site. It protects the endangered crested cow-wheat, one of the UK's rarest wildflowers. The site is actively managed to support biodiversity.

Name: Baggy Point (Croyde)
Description: Baggy Point, a headland in North Devon, offers dramatic clifftop walks and panoramic sea views along the South West Coast Path. It supports diverse marine birds and coastal flora; grey seals are often visible. The site is managed for conservation.

Name: Bagmere
Description: Bagmere nature reserve in Cheshire is a significant glacial lake and wetland. It features open water, fenland, and wet woodland habitats, supporting rare plants and diverse wildlife including wintering wildfowl and numerous dragonfly species.

Name: Bagmoor Common (SSSI)
Description: Bagmoor Common is a Site of Special Scientific Interest in Surrey, England. It features a valley mire and wet heathland, supporting rare plants such as white sedge and bog asphodel. The common is also home to various bird species, deer, and dragonflies, with a notable pond enhancing its biodiversity.

Name: Bailey Einon (Ancient River Ithon Wood)
Description: Bailey Einon is a mature, semi-natural woodland alongside the River Ithon in Powys, Wales. It features a mix of ancient and younger trees, boasting a rich ground flora including wild garlic, bluebells, and wood anemones. The site supports various bird species.

Name: Baker's Fen (Wicken Fen)
Description: Wicken Fen National Nature Reserve, including Baker's Fen, is a precious wetland habitat in Cambridgeshire. It supports diverse wildlife, from rare insects to birds, and is an active site for conservation research and species reintroduction.

Name: Baker's Pit
Description: Baker's Pit is a former clay pit with diverse habitats including heathland, acidic grassland, scrub, mire, and woodland. The site supports rare plants, fungi, insects, and birds. Ponds and open water areas provide habitat for dragonflies and amphibians.

Name: Bakethin Reservoir
Description: Bakethin Reservoir, in Kielder Forest, Northumberland, is a shallow, narrow water body formed with Kielder Water. Its margins feature marshy areas and bog woodland, supporting otters, red squirrels, and diverse wildfowl. A Northumberland Wildlife Trust reserve.

Name: Balloo Wetland & Woodland
Description: Balloo Wetland, near Bangor, is a nature reserve featuring a mosaic of habitats, including ponds, reedbeds, wet grassland, and woodland. It supports a variety of wildlife, such as otters, kingfishers, and various invertebrates. A boardwalk offers visitor access.

Name: Balls Wood
Description: Balls Wood is an ancient woodland near Hertford Heath, featuring mature oak, hornbeam, and hazel. The diverse site includes ponds, streams, and glades, supporting fungi, invertebrates, and birds like great spotted woodpeckers. Public footpaths provide access.

Name: Ballyaghagan Nature Reserve (Wildflower Gem)
Description: Ballyaghagan Nature Reserve protects ancient woodland and wildflower meadows near Belfast. It preserves one of the area's few remaining ancient woodland fragments, featuring notable wildflowers in spring and early summer. It is home to diverse bird and invertebrate species.

Name: Ballynahone Bog (Lowland Raised Bog)
Description: Ballynahone Bog is a significant lowland raised bog in Northern Ireland. It supports rare plants like bog-rosemary and great sundew, along with diverse insect life. The site forms part of the Moyola River catchment area, providing crucial habitat for various wildlife species.

Name: Balmacara Estate (Coille Mhor Oakwood)
Description: Balmacara Estate is a crofting estate near Kyle of Lochalsh, Scotland, on Loch Alsh. The diverse landscape features coastal areas, lochs, moorland, and Coille Mhòr, an ancient Atlantic oak forest. The estate is actively managed for conservation and sustainable crofting.

Name: Balnaguard Glen
Description: Balnaguard Glen is a woodland reserve in Perthshire, Scotland, known for its ancient oak and hazel trees. It provides habitat for red squirrels, wood warblers, and other woodland birds. Visitors can follow waymarked trails through the diverse flora and fauna.

Name: Bamff Wildland (Beaver Pioneer)
Description: Bamff Wildland is a rewilding project in Perthshire, Scotland. It focuses on the reintroduction and monitoring of European beavers, observing their transformative impact on the landscape. The estate actively supports biodiversity and habitat restoration.

Name: Ban-y-Gor
Description: Ban-y-Gor is a small woodland nature reserve near Beachley, overlooking the River Severn. It features ancient woodland, limestone cliffs, and grassland rich in wildflowers. The site supports various bird species, insects, and rare plants, forming part of the Wye Valley Area of Outstanding Natural Beauty.

Name: Bankhead Moss
Description: Bankhead Moss is a small lowland raised bog in North East Scotland, designated a Site of Special Scientific Interest. It hosts various sphagnum mosses, dragonflies, damselflies, and different bird species. The site is a key example of this habitat.

Name: Bardney Limewoods NNR
Description: Bardney Limewoods NNR is a complex of ancient woodlands between Lincoln and Horncastle in Lincolnshire. It preserves the largest remaining small-leaved lime woods in Britain, rich in biodiversity. The reserve offers habitats for various flora and fauna, including rare invertebrates.

Name: Bareland Street (Flower-Rich Levels)
Description: Bareland Street nature reserve in Torfaen features two flower-rich meadows, hosting species like yellow rattle, ragged robin, and northern marsh orchid. Traditional methods manage these habitats for wildlife.

Name: Barford Wood and Meadows
Description: Barford Wood and Meadows is a diverse nature reserve near Cambridge, featuring ancient woodland, wildflower meadows, and a lake. It supports various wildlife, including butterflies, birds, and badgers. Managed for biodiversity and visitor enjoyment.

Name: Barkbooth Lot
Description: Barkbooth Lot is an upland pasture within the Lake District National Park, part of a Site of Special Scientific Interest (SSSI). It features species-rich grassland and a stream, providing habitats for rare butterflies and birds. Grazing by cattle maintains its ecological value.

Name: Barkway Chalk Pit
Description: Barkway Chalk Pit is a former chalk quarry in north Hertfordshire. The site features chalk grassland on a steep south-facing slope, supporting wildflowers such as bee orchid and common spotted orchid. It is home to butterflies like the grizzled skipper and brown argus.

Name: Barlow Common
Description: Barlow Common Nature Reserve, near Selby, transformed from a railway goods yard and ash tip. It now features diverse habitats including wetlands, ponds, and woodland. The site supports a variety of birds, amphibians, and insects. Ponds host newts and dragonflies, whilst the woodland edge provides shelter.

Name: Barn Elms Wetland Centre SSSI
Description: Barn Elms Wetland Centre SSSI, located in London, is a nationally important site for wildlife. It encompasses a variety of wetland habitats and supports diverse bird species and other wildlife in an urban environment. It is managed to conserve its ecological value.

Name: Barnes Meadow
Description: Barnes Meadow is a riverside wildflower meadow by the Great Ouse, managed to protect water voles and diverse plant species. The site features a rich array of flora and supports various invertebrates, with a public footpath offering access.

Name: Barnsley Canal
Description: Barnsley Canal Nature Reserve protects a section of the historic canal, opened in 1799, near Stairfoot, south of Barnsley. It features reedbed, marsh, scrub, and woodland habitats. The reserve supports water voles, kingfishers, dragonflies, and damselflies.

Name: Barnwell Meadows (Cambridge PPF)
Description: Barnwell Meadows is a traditional hay meadow in the heart of Cambridge, managed by Cambridge Past, Present & Future. It supports diverse wildflowers, grasses, and insects, offering a glimpse into the area's agricultural past. Visitors enjoy peaceful walks and observe wildlife.

Name: Baron's Haugh
Description: Baron's Haugh is an RSPB reserve in Motherwell, North Lanarkshire, Scotland. It features a diverse wetland habitat alongside the River Clyde, attracting numerous waterfowl, waders, and other bird species throughout the year. Visitors can enjoy several trails and hides.

Name: Barossa
Description: Barossa is a 40-hectare nature reserve near Camberley in Surrey, featuring heathland and woodland habitats. It supports diverse wildlife, including protected ground-nesting birds like nightjar and woodlark, and various rare insects. The site is a key part of the Thames Basin Heaths Special Protection Area.

Name: Barossa (MOD Dry Training Area)
Description: Barossa, a large heathland nature reserve near Camberley, Surrey, is managed by Surrey Wildlife Trust. The site, an MOD Dry Training Area, features dry lowland heath, acid grassland, and mire, supporting rare species like sand lizards and Dartford warblers.

Name: Barrington Hill (Meadow)
Description: Barrington Court estate in Somerset features diverse landscapes, including extensive meadows that support a range of local wildlife. These grasslands are managed to enhance biodiversity, providing habitats for insects and ground-nesting birds within the historic grounds.

Name: Barrow Haven Reedbed
Description: Barrow Haven Reedbed is a declining brackish reedbed habitat in the UK, vital for birds like reed warblers, sedge warblers, and bitterns. It's also home to various insects and otters. An observation screen provides excellent birdwatching opportunities near the river.

Name: Barrow Haven Reedbeds (Humber Estuary)
Description: Barrow Haven Reedbeds is a freshwater reedbed adjacent to the Humber Estuary. It provides habitat for reed warblers, bearded tits, and other wetland birds, featuring a circular trail and a viewing screen.

Name: Barrow Wake
Description: Barrow Wake is a nature reserve on the Cotswold scarp, offering panoramic views across the Severn Vale. The site features flower-rich limestone grassland, scrub, and woodland habitats. It is managed to conserve its rich butterfly, moth, and plant populations, supporting significant biodiversity.

Name: Bartinney
Description: Bartinney Nature Reserve in West Cornwall forms part of the Penwith Moors SSSI and West Penwith ESA. Managed by Cornwall Wildlife Trust, the site protects rare plants and birds, restoring heathland and traditional farming practices.

Name: Barton Broad and Marshes
Description: Barton Broad, the second-largest Norfolk Broad, is part of the Broads National Park. It features extensive reedbed and carr woodland habitats, supporting rare plants, insects, and otter. A wheelchair-accessible boardwalk allows visitors to explore the unique wetland environment.

Name: Barton Gravel Pit
Description: Barton Gravel Pit, a restored former gravel pit in Bedfordshire, features open water, reedbeds, grassland, and woodland. The site supports diverse wildlife, including wading birds, dragonflies, and rare plants. It is actively managed for conservation.

Name: Barton Meadows
Description: Barton Meadows is a 38-hectare nature reserve north of Winchester. Opened in 2018, it features newly planted woodland, restored chalk grassland, and a wetland scrape. This former arable site supports species like brown hairstreak butterflies and barn owls, offering accessible wildlife habitats.

Name: Barton Pool (Small Wetland adjacent to Attenborough)
Description: Barton Pool is a small wetland reserve adjacent to Attenborough Nature Reserve in Nottinghamshire. The site comprises reedbeds, open water, and scrub, supporting diverse birdlife including warblers and ducks. It offers a tranquil habitat managed for biodiversity.

Name: Barton's Bank
Description: Barton's Bank is a small ancient oak woodland nature reserve on the edge of the Wyre Forest, near Cleobury Mortimer. Historically part of the Kinlet Estate, it hosts a wide variety of woodland flowers and provides habitat for birds, bats, and invertebrates. Shropshire Wildlife Trust manages the site for biodiversity.

Name: Barton's Pan
Description: Barton Broad, the second largest Norfolk Broad, is a crucial wildlife site managed by the Norfolk Wildlife Trust. Visitors can explore the 163-hectare reserve, including fen and woodlands, via accessible boardwalks. The site features rich biodiversity, supporting various species. Conservation efforts are ongoing.

Name: Bartonsham Meadows (Hereford City)
Description: Bartonsham Meadows is a 23.9-hectare nature reserve outside Hereford city centre. Located on the River Wye's southern bank, it comprises flood meadows managed for nature recovery and flood management. The site offers walking routes and river access.

Name: Bassenthwaite Lake NNR
Description: Bassenthwaite Lake NNR is a shallow lake in the Lake District National Park, Cumbria. It hosts diverse habitats like reedbeds, woodlands, and open water, supporting a variety of wildlife including rare fish and otters.

Name: Basted Mill
Description: Basted Mill Nature Reserve is a Site of Special Scientific Interest near Borough Green, Kent. It features diverse wetland habitats including reedbeds, ponds, and wet woodland. The site supports a variety of wildlife, notably water voles and dragonflies. Public access paths are available.

Name: Baston Fen
Description: Baston Fen is a 70-hectare nature reserve in Lincolnshire, featuring fen, wet woodland, and unimproved grassland habitats. It supports diverse insect populations and breeding birds. A boardwalk and trails provide visitor access.

Name: Bath Skyline (Woodland & Rare Grassland)
Description: The Bath Skyline, a six-mile National Trust route, overlooks Bath. It features ancient woodlands, Iron Age hill forts, hidden valleys, and wildflower meadows, supporting diverse wildlife habitats.

Name: Bathampton Meadow
Description: Bathampton Meadow is a tranquil floodplain meadow by the River Avon, near Bath, managed by Avon Wildlife Trust. It hosts diverse wildflowers like snake's-head fritillary and marsh marigold, along with various invertebrates and birds. Traditional hay cutting and grazing are used for biodiversity.

Name: Bathampton Meadows
Description: Bathampton Meadows is a riverside flood plain near Bath, offering tranquil green space and city skyline views. Popular for walking and cycling, the meadows support diverse wildlife, including birds and wildflowers, and are managed for conservation.

Name: Bawdeswell Heath
Description: Bawdeswell Heath, near Dereham, Norfolk, is a medieval heathland fragment. It features wet and dry heath, acid grassland, and woodland. Diverse flora and fauna thrive, including common lizards and adders. Site management maintains its character.

Name: Bawsinch and Duddingston
Description: Bawsinch and Duddingston Loch is a Scottish Wildlife Trust reserve in Edinburgh. The site features a freshwater loch and surrounding wetlands, attracting various waterfowl and bird species. Habitats include reedbeds, scrub, and woodland. The reserve offers walking trails and bird hides.

Name: Bay Meadows (The Marlborough Link)
Description: Bay Meadows is a Wiltshire Wildlife Trust nature reserve near Marlborough. This site features wet grassland and ancient hedgerows, supporting diverse wetland flora and fauna, including water voles. A circular walking route is accessible for visitors.

Name: Bay Pond
Description: Bay Pond is a significant wetland habitat in Surrey, featuring a large pond, fen, and wet grassland. It supports diverse wildlife, including waterfowl, dragonflies, and water voles. A public footpath encircles the reserve.

Name: Beacon Hill
Description: Beacon Hill is a small nature reserve in north Kent, situated on the edge of the urban area of Vigo. It features chalk grassland with orchids and a variety of invertebrates.

Name: Beacon Hill (Heathland Stronghold)
Description: Beacon Hill is a lowland heathland nature reserve in Monmouthshire, Wales. It protects diverse habitats, including dry and wet heath and acidic grassland. The site supports varied wildlife and offers public footpaths for exploration.

Name: Beacon Hill Conservation Park
Description: Beacon Hill is a tranquil site, encompassing wildflower meadows, woodlands, and a pond. It offers habitat for diverse wildlife, including various bird species and invertebrates. The park provides public access for quiet recreation and nature observation.

Name: Beacon Hill Country Park (Charnwood Geology)
Description: Beacon Hill Country Park in Leicestershire features ancient 600-million-year-old rocks and panoramic views across Charnwood Forest. The park contains mature woodlands, heathland, wildflower meadows, walking trails, and reconstructed Bronze Age log houses.

Name: Beaconwood and the Winsel
Description: Beaconwood and The Winsel spans 24.18 hectares, featuring a mix of mature and regenerating broadleaf trees. The site provides various walking trails and supports diverse wildlife habitats. It includes remnants of ancient woodland and offers a natural environment.

Name: Beales Meadows
Description: Beales Meadows is a Cornwall Wildlife Trust nature reserve, featuring traditional hay meadows, ancient woodland, and borders the River Lynher. The site supports diverse wildflowers, dormice, and otters. Grazing helps maintain its rich biodiversity for conservation.

Name: Beam Parklands
Description: Beam Parklands is a large wetland area in the London Borough of Barking and Dagenham, created as part of the Thames Gateway regeneration. It features open water, reedbeds, and grasslands, providing important habitat for wildlife and a green space for local communities. The River Beam flows through the site.

Name: Beane Marsh
Description: Beane Marsh is a tranquil nature reserve in Hertford, located on the River Beane flood plain. It comprises wet pasture, willow scrub, and ditches, providing habitat for wetland birds and diverse invertebrates like dragonflies and damselflies.

Name: Bearley & Snitterfield Meadows
Description: Bearley & Snitterfield Meadows comprise two wildflower meadows in south Warwickshire. Bearley, formerly parkland, supports colourful plants and insects. Snitterfield is an undisturbed hay meadow, hosting green-winged orchids and oxeye daisies.

Name: Beck Bottom
Description: Beck Bottom is an ancient semi-natural woodland near Guisborough, featuring mature oak, ash, and birch trees. A dense undergrowth of hazel and hawthorn supports wildflowers like bluebell and wild garlic. Beck Bottom Beck, a small stream, flows through the reserve, hosting aquatic invertebrates.

Name: Beckenham Place Park (Ravensbourne)
Description: Beckenham Place Park in South London hosts the River Ravensbourne, a site of significant river restoration work. Thames21, in partnership with local groups, improves habitats and naturalises the river course, with ongoing volunteer tasks contributing to its ecological recovery.

Name: Beddington Farmlands
Description: Beddington Farmlands, a nature reserve in south London, is built on a former landfill site. Managed for biodiversity since the 1980s, it provides a crucial habitat for various wildlife, particularly birds. It serves as a significant area for ecological restoration.

Name: Bedford Purlieus (National Nature Reserve)
Description: Bedford Purlieus is one of the largest ancient woodlands in lowland England. It is home to a rich diversity of trees, plants and invertebrates, including over 40 species of breeding birds. The site is a National Nature Reserve, managed for conservation and research.

Name: Bedfords Park
Description: Bedfords Park, a 215-acre deer park and nature reserve near Romford, is managed by Essex Wildlife Trust. It features woodlands, grasslands, and ponds, supporting diverse wildlife. The park offers walking trails, a visitor centre, and views across London.

Name: Bedfords Park (Havering)
Description: Bedfords Park is a nature reserve located in Havering, offering panoramic views across London. It features woodlands, ponds, and meadows, supporting a variety of wildlife including red deer. The site has a visitor centre and promotes environmental education.

Name: Bedgebury National Pinetum and Forest
Description: Home to a world-leading collection of conifers, the National Pinetum is a centre for research and conservation. The wider forest offers walking, cycling, and family activities, supporting biodiversity and public engagement.

Name: Bedgebury Pinetum (East District boundary)
Description: Bedgebury National Pinetum and Forest, located in Kent, is a renowned arboretum housing a world-leading collection of conifers and other trees. It offers extensive walking and cycling trails, alongside a popular adventure play area.

Name: Bedgebury Pinetum (Forestry England/KWT interface)
Description: Bedgebury National Pinetum and Forest is a world-leading conifer collection and ancient woodland in the High Weald AONB, supporting diverse wildlife. It serves as a living laboratory for conservation and research, managed by Forestry England and Kent Wildlife Trust.

Name: Beechenhurst - Forest of Dean
Description: Beechenhurst Lodge is a popular visitor centre within the Forest of Dean, Gloucestershire. It offers a café, adventure play, sculpture trail, and easy access walks. Visitors can find information and explore Go Ape Tree Top Adventure.

Name: Beechwoods
Description: Beechwoods is a small ancient woodland south of Cambridge, noted for its fine mature beech trees. The reserve is home to varied fungi species and supports birds such as tawny owl and nuthatch. Spring sees wildflowers like bluebells and wood anemones.

Name: Begwary Brook
Description: Begwary Brook is a tributary of the Great Ouse, flowing through arable farmland. The reserve features two wet grassland areas, restored in partnership with the Environment Agency. The brook itself is a natural habitat, managed with cattle grazing to maintain species-rich grassland, supporting breeding waders and aquatic invertebrates.

Name: Beinn Eighe & Torridon NNR
Description: Torridon estate in north-west Scotland features mountains, ancient Caledonian pine forests, and sea lochs. It encompasses the Beinn Eighe National Nature Reserve and peaks like Beinn Alligin and Liathach, offering a diverse wilderness habitat.

Name: Beinn Eighe NNR
Description: Beinn Eighe NNR, established 1951 in Wester Ross, Scottish Highlands, is the UK's first National Nature Reserve. This 10,000-hectare site features ancient Caledonian pinewood, mountain habitats and diverse wildlife.

Name: Belhus Chase
Description: Belhus Chase is a 20.37-hectare site near Aveley in Essex, within the Thames Chase Community Forest. The woodland features mature native broadleaves and younger plantings. It offers walking routes through diverse habitats, providing a green space for wildlife and visitors.

Name: Bell Crag Flow (Border Mires)
Description: Bell Crag Flow is a raised peat bog forming part of the Border Mires complex. It features bog pools, hare's-tail cotton grass, and heather. The site supports sphagnum mosses and insect life, contributing to a diverse wetland ecosystem.

Name: Bellever Moor and Meadows
Description: Bellever Moor and Meadows is a Dartmoor nature reserve, managed for its diverse habitats including mire, wet heath, and riverine woodland. It supports significant wildlife such as otters, dormice, and marsh fritillary butterflies.

Name: Bellfield's Pasture
Description: Bellfield's Pasture is a small nature reserve in Kirkheaton, near Huddersfield. The sloping pasture, leading to the River Colne, features diverse grasses and wildflowers, attracting insects and butterflies. Ancient hedgerows border the reserve, providing bird habitats.

Name: Belmaduthy Dam
Description: Belmaduthy Dam is an artificial loch on the Black Isle, north of Inverness. The reserve protects woodlands along its south bank, featuring birch and pine, attracting diverse waterfowl and wading birds. It is managed by the Scottish Wildlife Trust.

Name: Belsize Wood (Camden Green Gym)
Description: Belsize Wood Nature Reserve is managed by Camden Green Gym volunteers. They undertake woodland management, coppicing, path clearance, and litter picking. The site offers a peaceful retreat and habitat for wildlife in London.

Name: Beltingham River Gravels (South Tyne)
Description: Beltingham River Gravels is a nature reserve along the River South Tyne, near Haltwhistle. It features open shingle and gravel banks, providing habitat for species like little ringed plover. The site includes a riverside wood and supports various butterflies and dragonflies.

Name: Belton Estate (Ancient Deer Park)
Description: Belton Estate, a National Trust property in Lincolnshire, features a Grade I listed country house surrounded by a 1,300-acre deer park. The estate includes formal gardens, ancient woodland, and parkland. Visitors can explore varied landscapes and observe wildlife.

Name: Belvide Reservoir (Bird Reserve)
Description: Belvide Reservoir is a Site of Special Scientific Interest in South Staffordshire. Managed as a private bird reserve, it focuses on wildlife conservation and bird observation. The site hosts a bird ringing station, recording over 200 species annually. Access requires a permit.

Name: Bemersyde Moss
Description: Bemersyde Moss is a rare lowland raised bog and Site of Special Scientific Interest in the Scottish Borders. It features peatland, acid grassland, and woodland, supporting sphagnum mosses, heather, diverse invertebrates and birds. Managed for biodiversity.

Name: Ben Lawers NNR (Arctic-Alpine Flora)
Description: Ben Lawers National Nature Reserve in Perthshire, a site of international importance for arctic-alpine flora. Seven Munros offer challenging walks with dramatic views. The reserve is managed for nature conservation and protects a unique plant community.

Name: Ben Lomond (Conservation Project)
Description: Ben Lomond is Scotland's most southerly Munro, offering stunning views across Loch Lomond & The Trossachs National Park. The National Trust for Scotland manages conservation efforts, focusing on path maintenance, erosion control, and supporting native wildlife.

Name: Beningbrough (The Wilderness Project)
Description: Beningbrough Hall is a Georgian mansion near York, managed by the National Trust. It features extensive gardens, including a walled garden and a dedicated Wilderness Project area for nature exploration and play. The hall houses artworks from the National Portrait Gallery.

Name: Benington Lordship (managed boundary)
Description: Shaw's Corner, in Ayot St Lawrence, Hertfordshire, was home to playwright George Bernard Shaw for over 40 years. This Arts and Crafts house and garden are preserved, offering insight into his daily life and work. Visitors explore preserved rooms, see his writing shed, and enjoy the tranquil grounds.

Name: Benmore (Argyll Forest Park)
Description: Benmore, within Argyll Forest Park, features an impressive avenue of towering Californian giant redwoods. This botanical garden near Dunoon showcases a wide variety of trees and shrubs, including a unique Chilean rainforest glade, offering diverse plant collections.

Name: Bennett's Patch and White's Paddock
Description: Bennett's Patch and White's Paddock are two former arable fields near Upton Cheyney. Restored to flower-rich grasslands, they host various butterflies, bees, and birds. Managed by Avon Wildlife Trust, they offer walking trails and scenic views of the surrounding countryside.

Name: Benscliffe Wood (Ancient Woodland)
Description: Benscliffe Wood is an ancient woodland, a Site of Special Scientific Interest in Leicestershire. It features mature oak, ash, and birch trees with a rich understorey of wildflowers, ferns, and diverse plant communities. This important habitat supports a variety of birds and mammals, preserving a key part of the region's natural heritage.

Name: Benshaw Moor (Rede Valley Mosaic)
Description: Benshaw Moor is a 372-hectare Northumberland Wildlife Trust nature reserve in the Rede Valley. The upland site features blanket bog and wet heath. It supports important wading birds, including curlew and snipe, and diverse flora.

Name: Bentinck Banks
Description: Bentinck Banks is a 13-hectare former colliery spoil tip near Kirkby-in-Ashfield, Nottinghamshire. It features diverse habitats including grassland, scrub, and woodland, supporting butterflies and common lizards. Public access is available.

Name: Bentley Community Woodland (Doncaster)
Description: Bentley Community Woodland is a former colliery site in Doncaster, transformed into a green space with native trees, shrubs, ponds, and wildflower meadows. It enhances local biodiversity and is managed with community volunteer involvement.

Name: Berneray (North Uist)
Description: RSPB Balranald is a nature reserve on North Uist, Outer Hebrides. It features vast machair, sandy beaches, and rocky shorelines. The reserve supports numerous wading birds, corn crakes, and seabirds, especially from spring to summer. A visitor centre, car park, and toilets are available.

Name: Bernwood Forest (Oxfordshire/Bucks)
Description: Bernwood Forest, a former royal hunting ground, consists of various woodlands across Oxfordshire and Buckinghamshire. It provides diverse habitats for rare butterflies and wildlife. Visitors can enjoy walking, cycling, and horse riding on established trails, with public access maintained for recreation.

Name: Bernwood Meadows
Description: Bernwood Meadows is a traditionally managed hay meadow, rich in wildflowers, with diverse grasses and herbs. The site, situated in the Bernwood Forest, supports various insect life, including butterflies like the brown argus. A small stream flows through the meadow.

Name: Berry Head (Seabird Colony)
Description: Berry Head is an iconic headland and National Nature Reserve, home to the UK's most southerly guillemot colony. Features include a Napoleonic fort and spectacular views across Torbay. It offers opportunities for wildlife spotting.

Name: Besthorpe (Mons Pool)
Description: Besthorpe Nature Reserve, near Newark, features diverse habitats including sand and gravel workings, ponds, woodland, and grasslands. It is home to various bird species, invertebrates, and flora. Mons Pool is a prominent feature.

Name: Besthorpe Nature Reserve
Description: Besthorpe Nature Reserve is a former sand and gravel quarry by the River Trent, near Newark-on-Trent. It features diverse habitats like wetlands, grasslands, and woodland, supporting many bird species, invertebrates, and flora. The site offers peaceful trails.

Name: Betchcott Hills
Description: Betchcott Hills is a 112-hectare nature reserve near Church Stretton, Shropshire. It features grassland, heathland, and woodland habitats, supporting common lizards, slow worms, and various bird species. Public access is permitted for walking.

Name: Betchworth Quarry
Description: Betchworth Quarry is a former chalk quarry in Surrey, now a nature reserve. It features exposed chalk cliffs, an underground tunnel, and diverse habitats. The site supports varied flora and fauna, including rare orchids and butterflies.

Name: Betchworth Quarry and Lime Kilns
Description: Betchworth Quarry is a 20-hectare former chalk quarry and lime kilns, now a nature reserve. It supports diverse habitats like grassland and woodland, providing homes for bats, wildflowers, and insects on its exposed chalk faces.

Name: Bevercotes Wood (Sherwood Community Woodland)
Description: Bevercotes Wood is a Sherwood Community Woodland near Newark, transforming former arable land into a diverse habitat. It offers surfaced paths for walking, cycling, and horse riding, alongside quiet areas for reflection, providing space for people and wildlife.

Name: Bevere Fish Pass (Severn Connectivity)
Description: Bevere Fish Pass is a significant fish pass near Worcester on the River Severn. It allows migratory fish, such as shad and salmon, to bypass Bevere Weir and access upstream spawning grounds. The pass is a key part of the Unlocking the Severn project.

Name: Bicester Wetland Reserve
Description: Bicester Wetland Reserve, a former MOD site, offers a mosaic of habitats including ponds, scrapes, and reedbed. It supports diverse wildlife, notably wading birds like lapwing and curlew, and various invertebrates. The reserve aims to increase biodiversity within its varied landscapes.

Name: Bickham Wood
Description: Bickham Wood is a diverse woodland in the Quantock Hills, managed for conservation. It supports various tree species including oak and ash, showcasing ancient woodland features. The reserve features a rich ground flora and diverse insect life.

Name: Biddulph Grange Garden (Victorian Stumpery)
Description: Biddulph Grange Garden in Staffordshire features a Victorian stumpery, a unique garden design utilising upturned tree roots and stumps for dramatic effect. The site also includes various themed gardens, reflecting global horticultural styles from the 19th century.

Name: Big Pool Wood (Reedbed & Fen)
Description: Big Pool Wood is a small wetland reserve in North Wales, featuring a reedbed and fen. It supports rare plants, invertebrates, and water birds. A boardwalk offers views across the reedbed, while a circular path goes through ancient woodland. Summer brings butterflies and dragonflies.

Name: Big Waters (Urban Wetland Stronghold)
Description: Big Waters is a Northumberland Wildlife Trust nature reserve north of Newcastle upon Tyne. It features a large lake, woodlands, and reedbeds, supporting diverse birdlife including overwintering wildfowl and migrating waders. This urban wetland stronghold offers peaceful wildlife watching opportunities.

Name: Bigbury-on-Sea & Burgh Island Coast
Description: Bigbury-on-Sea, a coastal village in South Devon, offers sandy beaches, rock pools, and views of Burgh Island. The island is accessible via a tidal causeway or sea tractor, featuring an Art Deco hotel. The area is popular for watersports, walks, and wildlife observation.

Name: Birch Moss Covert
Description: Birch Moss Covert is a small woodland reserve in the Weaver Valley, Cheshire, managed by Cheshire Wildlife Trust with Brereton Parish Council. It features native trees such as oak, ash, and hazel. The site supports woodland birds, small mammals, and diverse understorey vegetation, contributing to local biodiversity.

Name: Birch Wood
Description: Birch Wood is an ancient woodland nature reserve in Yorkshire, managed by Yorkshire Wildlife Trust. It features oak, ash, and hazel trees, providing habitat for various birds like woodpeckers and treecreepers. Spring brings a display of bluebells and wood anemones across the forest floor. Visitors can explore paths through the woods.

Name: Birches Farm
Description: Birches Farm is a 24-hectare Herefordshire reserve featuring unimproved grasslands and traditional orchards. It supports diverse wildflowers and insects, including the brown hairstreak butterfly, alongside ancient hedgerows and veteran trees.

Name: Birdbrook Road Nature Reserve
Description: Birdbrook Road Nature Reserve is a small urban woodland and grassland in the London Borough of Lewisham. It features a mosaic of habitats, including mature trees, scrub, and open grassy areas, supporting various local wildlife species.

Name: Birkrigg Common (managed interface)
Description: Birkrigg Common, a limestone grassland, provides panoramic views of Morecambe Bay and the Furness Peninsula. A Site of Special Scientific Interest, it hosts diverse flora, fauna, and ancient cairns. Managed for conservation and public access.

Name: Birling Gap and the Seven Sisters
Description: Birling Gap and the Seven Sisters form a dramatic coastal landscape of chalk cliffs and shingle beach. Rapid erosion reveals new fossils. The area provides habitat for diverse wildlife and offers popular cliff-top walks.

Name: Bishop Middleham Quarry
Description: Bishop Middleham Quarry, a Durham Wildlife Trust reserve, features limestone grassland and ancient woodland. It supports wildflowers and butterflies such as dark-red helleborine and dingy skipper, and is a designated Site of Special Scientific Interest (SSSI).

Name: Bishop Monkton Railway Cutting
Description: Bishop Monkton Railway Cutting Nature Reserve is a disused railway cutting near Ripon, North Yorkshire. The site features species-rich grassland, ancient woodland, and marshy areas, supporting diverse wildlife including orchids, butterflies, and amphibians.

Name: Bishop Wood
Description: Bishop Wood, a diverse woodland in North Yorkshire, features mature oak, birch, and hazel trees, alongside conifers. It supports roe deer, woodpeckers, and various butterfly species. This ancient site provides important habitats for fungi and invertebrates.

Name: Bishop Wood (Selby - managed blocks)
Description: Bishop Wood is a large ancient woodland in North Yorkshire, featuring diverse broadleaf and conifer tree species and significant wildlife habitats. It offers walking trails and is known for its rich ground flora. Historically managed for timber, the site provides a tranquil environment for visitors.

Name: Bishop's Wood
Description: Bishop's Wood is a 33.7-hectare ancient woodland near Selby, North Yorkshire. The site features a diverse mix of tree species and supports rich wildlife, including ground flora. It offers walking trails and is part of a wider landscape.

Name: Bishopswood Meadows
Description: Bishopswood Meadows, near Taunton, Somerset, is a species-rich nature reserve. It has diverse habitats, including ancient woodland and damp grasslands. Somerset Wildlife Trust manages the site, supporting wildflowers, insects, and various wildlife. Public access is available.

Name: Bisley & Pyrford Commons
Description: Bisley & Pyrford Commons is a varied nature reserve in Surrey, comprising heathland, woodland, and ponds. It supports diverse wildlife, including reptiles, birds, and insects. The site offers visitors several walking trails.

Name: Bisley & West End Commons and Reidon Hill
Description: Bisley & West End Commons and Reidon Hill forms part of the Thames Basin Heaths Special Protection Area. This important heathland site hosts all three native reptile species and diverse insects. Habitats include dry and wet heathland, acid grassland, and woodland.

Name: Bissoe Valley
Description: Bissoe Valley Nature Reserve is situated within the Cornish Mining World Heritage Site. It encompasses diverse habitats including wetlands, heathland, and acid grassland along the Bissoe River valley, supporting various flora and fauna.

Name: Black Bourn Valley
Description: Black Bourn Valley is a mosaic of habitats, including wildflower meadows, wet woodlands and riverine fen. The reserve is located along the Black Bourn river. It supports a variety of wildlife, from otters to dragonflies.

Name: Black Devon Wetlands
Description: Black Devon Wetlands is a key wildlife reserve near the Firth of Forth in Scotland. It features a mosaic of wetland habitats, including reedbeds, lagoons, and wet grassland. It provides a vital stopping point for migrating birds and supports many resident species. Visitors can observe diverse birdlife from hides.

Name: Black Lake (Delamere)
Description: Black Lake, near Delamere Forest, Cheshire, is a nature reserve with a rare floating bog habitat. This glacial relict supports diverse invertebrates, like dragonflies, and carnivorous plants. It holds Site of Special Scientific Interest status.

Name: Black Moss Covert
Description: Black Moss Covert is a small deciduous woodland south of Whaley Bridge, bordering the Goyt Way and Peak Forest Canal. It supports diverse wildlife, including common spotted orchids and broad-leaved helleborines, and is managed by Cheshire Wildlife Trust.

Name: Black Mountain Quarry (Biodiversity Transition)
Description: The Black Mountain Quarry Biodiversity Transition project enhances biodiversity through habitat management, grassland restoration, scrub control, tree planting, and pond creation. This former quarry within the Belfast Hills offers a mosaic of habitats for various wildlife species.

Name: Black Rocks (Gritstone Outcrop & Woods)
Description: Black Rocks is a prominent gritstone outcrop near Cromford, Derbyshire. It offers panoramic views over the Derwent Valley. Adjacent woodlands provide walking, cycling, and horse riding trails. The site is popular for rock climbing.

Name: Blackadon
Description: Blackadon is a nature reserve in the South Hams, Devon. Its ancient woodland, damp meadows, and river valley support diverse wildlife. Features include oak woodland, the River Avon, and habitats for rare butterflies and numerous bird species.

Name: Blackadon (Webburn Valley & Tor)
Description: Blackadon is an ancient woodland and pasture reserve within the Webburn Valley, featuring mature oaks, a diverse ground flora including bluebells, and a rich array of bird and insect life. The site is actively managed for wildlife conservation.

Name: Blackburn Flow (Cumbrian Borderlands)
Description: Blackburn Flow is a significant peatland restoration project located on the Cumbria/Northumberland border. A key Great North Bog site, it aims to rewet 450 hectares of peatland to reduce carbon emissions and enhance wildlife habitats.

Name: Blackcap
Description: Blackcap offers spectacular views over the South Downs National Park and chalk grasslands near Lewes. This site features ancient trees and diverse flora and fauna, characteristic of a chalk downland environment.

Name: Blackhall Rocks (Heritage Coast)
Description: Blackhall Rocks is a coastal nature reserve in County Durham, known for its limestone cliffs, intertidal rock pools, and unique magnesian limestone grassland. It supports diverse wildlife, including seabirds, wildflowers, and rare invertebrates, and offers panoramic views of the North Sea coastline.

Name: Blackleach Country Park (Salford)
Description: Blackleach Country Park is a Green Flag park in Salford, Greater Manchester. It features a large reservoir, woodland, and grassland, providing habitat for diverse wildlife including waterfowl and woodland birds. Visitors enjoy walking, fishing, and birdwatching.

Name: Blackmoor Copse (Ancient Woodland)
Description: Blackmoor Copse is a 115-acre ancient woodland in Wiltshire. It features oak, ash, hazel, and field maple, supporting varied wildlife like dormice and badgers. Spring brings vibrant bluebells. Managed by Wiltshire Wildlife Trust for conservation.

Name: Blackness Banks
Description: Blackness Banks is a Gloucestershire Wildlife Trust nature reserve featuring species-rich grassland, ancient woodland, and hedgerows. It provides habitats for diverse wildflowers, insects, and bird species. The site is actively managed for biodiversity conservation.

Name: Blackpool Sands & Strete Gate Coast
Description: This stretch of the South Devon coast features Strete Gate and Blackpool Sands, a shingle beach. The area, part of Start Bay, is known for its diverse geology, coastal habitats, and scenic views. The National Trust cares for segments of the coastline, providing access for visitors.

Name: Blagrove Common
Description: Blagrove Common is a small nature reserve managed by Hertfordshire Wildlife Trust. It features grassland, scrub, and wet woodland habitats. The site supports diverse flora and fauna, providing valuable ecological corridors for wildlife.

Name: Blair-nam-Faoileag NNR (Flow Country)
Description: Blair-nam-Faoileag National Nature Reserve protects a vast area of blanket bog, forming part of the internationally important Flow Country of Caithness and Sutherland. This peatland landscape supports diverse wildlife.

Name: Blakehill Farm (Airfield to Grassland)
Description: Blakehill Farm, a former WWII airfield in Wiltshire, is a 250-hectare nature reserve transformed into species-rich grassland, wetlands, and arable margins. It supports diverse wildlife, including skylarks, lapwings, invertebrates, and wildflowers, maintained through conservation grazing.

Name: Blakeney National Nature Reserve
Description: Blakeney National Nature Reserve in Norfolk features Blakeney Point, home to England's largest grey seal colony. It serves as a vital nesting ground for various bird species and holds international importance for wildlife. Visitors can explore the shingle beach.

Name: Blakey Topping (North York Moors)
Description: Blakey Topping is a distinctive conical hill in the North York Moors National Park. This landmark offers panoramic views across the Pickering valley and features unique geological formations, including sandstone outcrops. It forms part of a wider landscape managed for its natural beauty and heritage.

Name: Blashford Lakes
Description: Blashford Lakes Nature Reserve, near Ringwood, spans 150 hectares of lakes, woodland, and grassland, formed from former gravel pits. Managed by Hampshire and Isle of Wight Wildlife Trust, it is a key site for resident and migratory birds, offering hides and walking trails.

Name: Blashford Lakes Nature Reserve (New Forest Edge)
Description: Blashford Lakes Nature Reserve is a key wintering site for thousands of wildfowl and other water birds. Managed by Hampshire & Isle of Wight Wildlife Trust, it encompasses former gravel pits, River Avon floodplain, woodlands and grasslands, supporting a diverse range of wildlife throughout the year.

Name: Blaxhall Common
Description: Blaxhall Common is a Suffolk SSSI featuring dry and wet heath, fen, and woodland habitats. It supports specialist heathland birds like nightjar and woodlark, alongside adders and silver-studded blue butterflies. Public access is available.

Name: Blaxhall Common (Sandlings Heath)
Description: Blaxhall Common, a Sandlings Heath fragment, lies within the Suffolk Coast and Heaths AONB. This reserve features dry heathland, acid grassland, and scattered trees. Notable species include nightjar, woodlark, adders, and green hairstreak butterflies. Managed to conserve rare heathland habitat.

Name: Blickling Estate (950-acre Woodland & Veteran Trees)
Description: Blickling Estate in Norfolk features a Jacobean house, historic gardens, and extensive parkland with ancient trees. Managed by the National Trust, it offers woodland walks, lakeside paths, and diverse natural habitats near Aylsham.

Name: Blindley Heath
Description: Blindley Heath is a lowland wet grassland and heathland site located in Surrey, south-east England. It supports diverse flora and fauna, including rare invertebrates and ground-nesting birds. The site is managed for conservation, with habitat restoration ongoing.

Name: Blodwel Rocks
Description: Blodwel Rocks is a Shropshire nature reserve on a former quarry site. It features limestone grassland, ancient woodland, and scrub. The reserve supports diverse wildflowers, butterflies, and invertebrates, offering views of the Shropshire hills.

Name: Bloody Oaks Quarry
Description: Bloody Oaks Quarry, a former limestone quarry in Rutland, is now a Leicestershire and Rutland Wildlife Trust nature reserve. It features unimproved limestone grassland, a priority habitat rich in wildflowers such as the bee orchid. Diverse butterflies and insects thrive, with scrub and trees providing valuable bird habitats.

Name: Blow's Downs
Description: Blow's Downs is a 64.9-hectare biological Site of Special Scientific Interest (SSSI) in Dunstable, Bedfordshire. Managed by the Wildlife Trust BCN, this chalk grassland, scrub, and woodland site hosts rare plants like the Pasque flower and diverse butterflies, including Chalkhill Blue. It offers panoramic views.

Name: Bloxholm Wood
Description: Bloxholm Wood is an ancient woodland, primarily composed of oak and ash, with a rich ground flora including bluebells and wood anemones. It hosts diverse birdlife, such as nuthatch and great spotted woodpecker, and various woodland insects. The reserve is managed for biodiversity.

Name: Blue House Farm
Description: Blue House Farm is a coastal farm reserve on the River Blackwater, featuring grazing marsh, mudflats, and saltmarsh. It is vital for wintering wildfowl and waders, including Brent Geese and Pintail. Water levels are controlled via ditches and sluices for wildlife habitats. Footpath access is available.

Name: Bluebank Pools (Chesterfield Canal)
Description: Bluebank Pools, on the Chesterfield Canal near Staveley, is a former subsidence flash. It now thrives as a vibrant wetland, teeming with diverse wildlife and offering a significant habitat for various species.

Name: Blythe Alive (River Blythe SSSI)
Description: Blythe Alive focuses on restoring the River Blythe SSSI and its surrounding habitats. The project enhances biodiversity and climate change resilience by removing weirs, reconnecting the river with its floodplain, and creating new wetlands and ponds. It also involves community engagement.

Name: Bo'mains Meadow
Description: Bo'mains Meadow is a small, species-rich grassland located near Linlithgow, West Lothian. It features a diverse range of wildflowers, including common spotted orchid and cowslip, supporting various insect species.

Name: Boathouse Field (Derwentwater)
Description: Boathouse Field is a riverside meadow beside Derwentwater near Keswick. This site forms part of a larger network of wetland habitats. Grazing maintains species-rich grassland, supporting diverse flora including rare tubular water-dropwort and water voles.

Name: Boddington Meadow
Description: Boddington Meadow is a traditionally-managed floodplain meadow beside the River Ise in Northamptonshire. It supports diverse wildflowers and grasses, providing habitat for insects and birds. The site is managed to enhance its biodiversity for wildlife.

Name: Bodenham Lake
Description: Bodenham Lake is a significant reserve in Herefordshire, important for wintering wildfowl and various water birds. It offers a large hide for birdwatching. Herefordshire Wildlife Trust manages the site, focusing on habitat conservation and visitor engagement.

Name: Bodnant Garden (Estate & Ancient Wood)
Description: Bodnant Garden is a National Trust property near Tal-y-bont, Conwy, Wales. It features formal gardens, grand terraces, wooded glades, and an arboretum with champion trees. The garden offers stunning views of the Conwy Valley and Snowdonia.

Name: Bog Meadows (Urban Wildlife Hub)
Description: Bog Meadows, situated off the Falls Road in West Belfast, is a vital urban nature reserve. It offers a mosaic of reedbeds, meadows, ponds, and woodland, providing sanctuary for diverse wildlife including water rail, snipe, and various dragonflies.

Name: Boilton Nab Redscar and Tunbrook Woods
Description: Boilton Nab, Redscar, and Tunbrook Woods form a mosaic of woodland, grassland, and river habitats along the River Ribble. The site features ancient woodland, open grassland, and riverside paths, supporting diverse wildlife and industrial heritage.

Name: Bolam Lake (Managed Woodland)
Description: Bolam Lake Country Park, located between Belsay and Whalton, offers extensive footpaths, lakeside walks, and open grasslands. The site supports diverse wildlife, including red squirrels and birds, across its managed woodlands and meadows, providing recreational and nature study.

Name: Bolberry Down (South Devon Coast)
Description: Bolberry Down offers dramatic coastal views near Salcombe. This area features rare wildflowers, birds, and insects, making it a valuable habitat. Visitors can walk the South West Coast Path and enjoy the rugged coastline.

Name: Bold Forest Park (St Helens)
Description: Bold Forest Park in St Helens is a significant regeneration project transforming 450 hectares of former industrial land into new woodlands, wetlands, and grasslands. It offers walking, cycling, and horse riding opportunities and is part of the larger Mersey Forest initiative.

Name: Bolderwood (New Forest Core)
Description: Bolderwood, located in the New Forest, features ancient woodlands and walking trails. Visitors can observe fallow deer from a purpose-built viewing platform at the deer sanctuary. The area offers various routes through mature trees and picnic facilities.

Name: Bolt Head to Bolt Tail
Description: This dramatic stretch of South Devon coastline between Bolt Head and Bolt Tail offers spectacular views and diverse wildlife. Walk the South West Coast Path, exploring jagged cliffs and secluded coves rich in maritime history.

Name: Boltby Forest (Cleveland Hills)
Description: Boltby Forest, within the Cleveland Hills, features extensive walking and cycling trails. This mixed woodland supports diverse wildlife and offers views across the Vale of Mowbray. The forest comprises ancient woodlands and conifer plantations for visitors to explore.

Name: Bolton Percy Station
Description: Bolton Percy Station Nature Reserve, near Appleton Roebuck, preserves grassland and scrub habitats on a former railway line. This site supports a variety of wildflowers, butterflies, and moths, acting as a crucial wildlife corridor.

Name: Bolton-on-Swale Lake
Description: Bolton-on-Swale Lake, a former sand and gravel quarry near the River Swale, is now a wetland nature reserve. It supports overwintering wildfowl and breeding waders. Reedbeds and open water habitats provide diverse wildlife. The site is managed for conservation.

Name: Bonny Grove
Description: Bonny Grove is a small woodland in Middlesbrough, managed by Tees Valley Wildlife Trust. It provides a vital green space for both wildlife and people, featuring mature trees and a diverse understory of plant life, offering a quiet natural retreat.

Name: Bonny Wood
Description: Bonny Wood is a woodland with diverse habitats, including ancient woodland, newly planted areas, and rides. It is home to various tree species like oak, ash, hazel, and field maple, supporting a range of wildlife, including birds, butterflies, and dormice. The site features ponds and streams.

Name: Bonny Wood (Ancient Coppice)
Description: Bonny Wood is an ancient coppice wood and County Wildlife Site in Suffolk. It features diverse tree species like hornbeam, ash, and hazel, with a rich ground flora including bluebells and wood anemone. Traditional coppicing manages the site, supporting various bird species.

Name: Bonsai Bank
Description: Bonsai Bank is a small nature reserve managed by Kent Wildlife Trust, featuring veteran oak, ash, sweet chestnut, and hornbeam trees. It supports a diverse fungi community and various bird species. Its sloping terrain contributes to a rich ground flora.

Name: Boon's Copse
Description: Boon's Copse is an ancient, species-rich woodland near Woolavington, predominantly hazel coppice with oak standards. It provides a habitat for birds and dormice, with a diverse woodland floor supporting bluebells, primroses, and wild garlic.

Name: Booton Common
Description: Booton Common is a 13-hectare site in Norfolk, managed by Norfolk Wildlife Trust. It features diverse habitats including damp grassland, woodland, and a pond, supporting a variety of wildflowers, insects, and bird species. Visitors can explore marked trails.

Name: Border Mires (The 30x30 Peat Arks)
Description: The Border Mires are a vast complex of peatlands located within Northumberland National Park. Active restoration enhances biodiversity, improves water quality, and sequesters carbon. This site forms a key part of the wider peatland recovery programme.

Name: Borough Hill (Daventry Heathland)
Description: Borough Hill, a Local Nature Reserve near Daventry, comprises heathland, grassland, and scrub. It supports rare plants and diverse butterflies. The site includes an Iron Age hillfort and Roman villa remains, with views across Northamptonshire.

Name: Borrowdale - Johnny Wood (Atlantic Oak)
Description: Johnny Wood in Borrowdale, Lake District, features ancient Atlantic oak woodland. A designated Site of Special Scientific Interest, it supports diverse plant and animal life. The wood lies on the banks of the River Derwent.

Name: Borrowdale Rainforest Project (NT/NE Partnership)
Description: The Borrowdale Rainforest Project, a National Trust initiative, aims to restore and expand temperate rainforests across the Borrowdale valley in the Lake District. It focuses on regenerating ancient woodlands and enhancing biodiversity.

Name: Bossington Beach (Holnicote Estate)
Description: Bossington Beach, part of the Holnicote Estate in Somerset, is a shingle beach forming a natural barrier with saltmarshes. It offers views across the Bristol Channel and is a starting point for coastal walks.

Name: Bosvenning Common
Description: Bosvenning Common is a small nature reserve near Merrymeet, Liskeard, managed by Cornwall Wildlife Trust. It protects a fragment of wet heathland and bog, featuring cross-leaved heath and bog asphodel. The site supports diverse invertebrates and amphibians within a rare habitat.

Name: Bough Beech
Description: Bough Beech reservoir and surrounding woodland in west Kent is a Site of Special Scientific Interest. Managed by Kent Wildlife Trust, it offers important habitats for wildfowl, waders, and gulls, with bird hides available for observation.

Name: Bouldnor Forest
Description: Bouldnor Forest is an ancient woodland on the western Solent coast near Yarmouth, Isle of Wight. It features diverse habitats including saltmarsh, reedbeds, meadows, and mature broadleaf woodland, supporting a range of wildlife.

Name: Bouldnor Forest Nature Reserve (Isle of Wight)
Description: Bouldnor Forest is a coastal woodland and heathland mosaic on the Isle of Wight. It supports raptors, reptiles such as slow worms and adders, and rare invertebrates. The reserve offers views across the Solent and forms part of the Hamstead Heritage Coast.

Name: Boulmer Steel (Intertidal Rock Habitat)
Description: Boulmer Steel is an intertidal rock habitat situated on the Northumberland coast. The site is actively monitored by Northumberland Wildlife Trust for the presence of marine non-native species, contributing to regional biodiversity conservation efforts.

Name: Boundary Wood (Sherwood Edge)
Description: Boundary Wood is a newly established woodland site near Mansfield, Nottinghamshire, managed by Forestry England. It features a mix of broadleaf and conifer trees with accessible paths, contributing to local biodiversity and community green space.

Name: Bourne Mill (Wetland interface)
Description: Bourne Mill is a restored 16th-century watermill in Colchester, Essex. The site features a historic building, a large pond, and surrounding wetlands. It supports diverse wildlife, including kingfishers and dragonflies.

Name: Bourne Wood
Description: Bourne Wood, near Farnham in Surrey, is an ancient woodland on the Greensand Ridge. Managed by Forestry England, it offers walking paths through mature trees and open heathland, popular with locals and dog walkers. It serves as a haven for diverse wildlife.

Name: Bourne Woods (Kesteven Forest)
Description: Bourne Wood is a tranquil oak wood near Bourne, South Lincolnshire, offering walking, cycling, and horse riding trails. It features diverse habitats, including ancient woodland, open grassland, and ponds, supporting varied wildlife.

Name: Bovey Heathfield
Description: Bovey Heathfield, near Newton Abbot, is a surviving fragment of the extensive Bovey Basin heathlands. This important nature reserve is one of Devon Wildlife Trust's most significant sites for invertebrates, hosting many rare and protected species, including the nationally rare heath tiger beetle and the silver-studded blue butterfly.

Name: Bovey Heathfield (Lowland Gorse & Nightjar)
Description: Bovey Heathfield is a restored lowland heath near Bovey Tracey, Devon. It features dry and wet heathland, mire, and acid grassland habitats. The reserve supports wildlife including nightjars, Dartford warblers, and various invertebrates. It is actively managed for conservation.

Name: Bovey Valley Woods
Description: Bovey Valley Woods, within Dartmoor National Park, is an ancient woodland patchwork along the River Bovey. Managed by The Woodland Trust, it provides critical habitat for dormice, otters, and rare bats, featuring significant geological formations.

Name: Bovingdon Flood Meadow
Description: Bovingdon Flood Meadow is a wetland site by the River Brain near Bocking. It supports diverse wildlife, including kingfishers and watervoles, within its mature trees, scrub, sedges, and wildflowers. The meadow functions as a natural flood plain.

Name: Bow Creek Ecology Park
Description: Bow Creek Ecology Park is a wildlife haven in East London, at the confluence of Bow Creek and the River Thames. It features diverse wetland and brownfield habitats, supporting birds, insects, and plants, with peaceful walking routes and viewing platforms.

Name: Bowber Head Farm (Upland Hay Meadows)
Description: Bowber Head Farm is a 30-hectare nature reserve near Kirkby Stephen, Westmorland Dales. It features species-rich upland hay meadows, traditional barns, and diverse habitats supporting rare flora like globeflower and wood crane's-bill.

Name: Bowdown Woods
Description: Bowdown Woods, near Thatcham, Berkshire, is a diverse nature reserve. It comprises ancient woodland, heathland, and acid grassland habitats. Managed by BBOWT, the site supports various wildlife, including rare fungi. Public footpaths are present for access.

Name: Bowesfield
Description: Bowesfield, near Stockton-on-Tees, is an internationally important wetland nature reserve. Part of the Teesmouth and Cleveland Coast SSSI, this former quarry on the River Tees' west bank supports diverse bird species, otters, water voles, and rare plants.

Name: Bowness on Solway
Description: Bowness-on-Solway is a coastal nature reserve at the western end of Hadrian's Wall in Cumbria. It comprises saltmarsh, mudflats, and sand dunes, supporting diverse bird species like oystercatcher, curlew, and redshank. It is an important feeding and roosting site for migratory birds.

Name: Box Hill
Description: Box Hill is a prominent North Downs summit in Surrey, England, offering panoramic views across the Surrey Hills. Managed by the National Trust, it features woodlands, chalk grasslands, and walking trails.

Name: Box Meadows (Minchinhampton Common Link)
Description: Box Meadows is a Gloucestershire Wildlife Trust reserve near Minchinhampton Common. It features species-rich unimproved grassland, ancient hedgerows, and is grazed by cattle. The site provides a vital wildlife corridor in the Stroud Valleys.

Name: Box Wood
Description: Box Wood is an ancient woodland near Painswick, Gloucestershire, dominated by mature beech trees. Located on the limestone scarp, it offers views across the Severn Vale. Hazel and ash are present in the understory alongside varied flora.

Name: Boxford Water Meadows
Description: Boxford Water Meadows is a diverse wetland reserve in Berkshire, featuring flower-rich meadows, reedbeds, and wet woodland. The River Lambourn flows through the site, supporting water voles, otters, kingfishers, and dragonflies.

Name: Brackfield Wood (Foyle Valley)
Description: Brackfield Wood is part of the Faughan Valley, home to native tree species, wildlife, and a network of footpaths. The site offers views of the River Faughan and is popular for walking and nature observation. It contributes to the Faughan Valley Landscape Partnership.

Name: Bradfield Woods
Description: Bradfield Woods is a significant ancient coppice woodland in Suffolk, traditionally managed for over 700 years. This National Nature Reserve features hornbeam and hazel coppice, glades, and rides, supporting a rich diversity of flora and fauna. It provides a vital habitat for numerous species.

Name: Bradfield Woods (Ancient Coppice NNR)
Description: Bradfield Woods is an ancient woodland National Nature Reserve in Suffolk, preserving continuous tree cover since the 11th century. Actively managed using traditional coppicing, the site supports diverse habitats for dormice, rare butterflies, and ancient woodland flora. Public footpaths allow access.

Name: Bradlaugh Fields
Description: Bradlaugh Fields is a 60-hectare nature reserve in Northampton. It features ancient ridge and furrow grassland, wildflower meadows, and mature hedgerows. Habitats support diverse bird species, insects, and small mammals. Public footpaths allow access for recreation.

Name: Bradley Canal (Restoration & Habitat Link)
Description: This project restores a disused canal stretch in Bilston, West Midlands, creating a vital 'green corridor' linking existing green spaces. It aims to improve access to green and blue spaces and provide new walking and cycling routes.

Name: Bradwell Shell Bank
Description: Bradwell Shell Bank is a coastal nature reserve on the Blackwater Estuary, Essex. It features a shingle beach and saltmarsh, providing habitat for various bird species including avocets, ringed plovers, and terns. The site is managed for wildlife protection.

Name: Brae Pasture
Description: Brae Pasture nature reserve, near Grassington, occupies a steep south-west facing slope. It features species-rich grassland, limestone pavement, and scrub. Diverse flora, including orchids, and various insects thrive here. Management maintains habitat diversity.

Name: Braeburn Park
Description: Braeburn Park is a 27-hectare nature reserve in Crayford, southeast London. It features woodlands, grasslands, and a lake, supporting diverse wildlife. The site provides habitats for species such as kestrels and amphibians. Footpaths offer views across the River Cray valley.

Name: Braithwaite Moss
Description: Braithwaite Moss is a small wetland nature reserve in North Yorkshire, managed by Yorkshire Wildlife Trust. The site protects diverse plant life across fen and wet grassland habitats, supporting rare and interesting species. It acts as a local biodiversity hotspot.

Name: Bramley Bank
Description: Bramley Bank is a local nature reserve with ancient woodland, meadow, and a pond in South Croydon. It supports diverse wildlife, including nuthatches, kestrels, and various invertebrates. Footpaths provide access through the site for visitors.

Name: Brampton Wood
Description: Brampton Wood is a 130-hectare ancient woodland in Cambridgeshire, once part of the Royal Forest of Huntingdonshire. Managed for biodiversity, it features diverse tree species, wildflowers, and wildlife habitats for public enjoyment.

Name: Bramshill Forest (Heathy Woodland)
Description: Bramshill Forest in Hampshire features ancient woodland, conifer plantations, and heathland. It provides habitat for rare species like the Dartford warbler, nightjar, and sand lizard. The forest offers various walking trails for visitors to explore its diverse landscapes.

Name: Brancaster Beach
Description: Brancaster Beach, on the Norfolk coast, is a vast National Trust sandy expanse popular for walking and birdwatching. The SS Vina shipwreck is visible at low tide. This site protects diverse coastal wildlife and offers expansive views.

Name: Brancaster Staithe
