The site provides habitats for diverse wildlife, including wildflowers, butterflies, and birds.

Name: Long Wood (Cheddar Complex)
Description: Long Wood is a key ancient woodland within the Cheddar Complex SSSI. It showcases diverse tree species, including small-leaved lime, alongside a rich ground flora featuring bluebells, primroses, and wild garlic. The site supports significant wildlife, including dormice, bats, and various rare bird species.

Name: Longford River (Bushy Park)
Description: The Longford River is a man-made watercourse, constructed in the 1630s to supply water to Hampton Court Palace. It flows through Bushy Park, feeding ponds and water gardens before reaching the palace grounds. It supports diverse wildlife and habitats.

Name: Longhaven Cliffs
Description: Longhaven Cliffs is a nationally important coastal reserve in Aberdeenshire, Scotland, featuring spectacular rocky cliffs and coastal heath. It supports vital breeding seabird colonies, including kittiwakes, guillemots, and razorbills. Peregrines, fulmars, and mainland Britain's northernmost puffin colony are present.

Name: Longnd (Stretton Gap)
Description: Stretton Gap is a Shropshire Wildlife Trust nature reserve located on the eastern slopes of the Long Mynd, near Church Stretton. This species-rich grassland supports a variety of invertebrates, birds, and wildflowers, maintained through grazing by Longhorn cattle.

Name: Longshaw Estate (Peak District Moors & Padley Gorge)
Description: Longshaw Estate, located within the Peak District, encompasses diverse moorland, woodland, and parkland environments. It features historic Longshaw Lodge and is a popular destination for walking, nature observation, and family activities. Padley Gorge is a notable feature.

Name: Longspring Wood
Description: Longspring Wood is an ancient woodland in Hertfordshire, featuring mature oak, hornbeam, and hazel coppice. Diverse ground flora includes bluebells and wood anemones, supporting various bird and insect species. Managed by Hertfordshire Wildlife Trust.

Name: Longworth Clough (Upland Stream & Wood)
Description: Longworth Clough is a tranquil reserve, featuring broadleaved woodland and an upland stream habitat. It supports diverse flora and fauna, including kingfishers and dippers along the stream, and various woodland birds and invertebrates. Ancient trees enhance biodiversity.

Name: Looe Island (Marine & Seabird Ark)
Description: Looe Island, also known as St George's Island, is Cornwall Wildlife Trust's sole marine nature reserve, located one mile off Looe. It serves as a vital marine and seabird sanctuary, supporting diverse wildlife. The island is designated a Site of Special Scientific Interest (SSSI).

Name: Lotts Grassland (Limestone Heath Rarity)
Description: Lotts Grassland, near Shepton Mallet, Somerset, is a small nature reserve featuring rare calcareous grassland and neutral grassland. It supports diverse wildflowers and invertebrates, including adder's-tongue fern and common blue butterfly. The site protects a unique limestone heath habitat, offering a glimpse into a threatened ecosystem.

Name: Lough Foyle (Estuary Giants)
Description: Lough Foyle is a large, shallow estuary on the border of Northern Ireland, internationally important for wintering wildfowl and wading birds. It provides vital feeding and roosting grounds for species like whooper swans and light-bellied brent geese.

Name: Lough Foyle (Native Oyster Recovery)
Description: Lough Foyle, Northern Ireland, hosts a native oyster recovery project. It reintroduces oysters to restore populations, enhance marine biodiversity, and improve water quality within the lough's ecosystem, working with local communities.

Name: Loughborough Big Meadow
Description: Loughborough Big Meadow is a riverside grazing meadow in Leicestershire, rich in wildflowers and insects. The reserve supports various wetland birds, including snipe and curlew, and is important for breeding waders. Part of the Loughborough Living Landscape project, it is managed for biodiversity.

Name: Lound Lakes
Description: Lound Lakes is a 100-hectare Suffolk Wildlife Trust nature reserve near Lowestoft. It features lakes, wet grassland, woodlands, and reedbeds, supporting diverse wildlife. The site offers accessible walking trails for visitors.

Name: Lound Lakes (Coastal Freshwater)
Description: Lound Lakes is a 130-hectare nature reserve in north-east Suffolk, featuring freshwater lakes, reedbeds, and wet grassland. It supports diverse wildlife, including kingfishers, otters, and various waterfowl, important for migrating birds and local biodiversity.

Name: Love Your River Cole (LYRiC)
Description: The Love Your River Cole project improves a 2.5km stretch of the River Cole in Birmingham's Shard End and Stechford areas. It focuses on enhancing river access, boosting biodiversity, and offering community engagement opportunities. The project is managed by the Tame Valley Wetlands Landscape Partnership.

Name: Low Carr
Description: Low Carr Nature Reserve, near Doncaster, features a mosaic of wildflower-rich grasslands, wetlands, and open water. This site supports diverse wildlife, including rare plants and various bird species. It is actively managed by Yorkshire Wildlife Trust for habitat conservation.

Name: Low Wood
Description: Low Wood is a 5.6-hectare ancient broadleaved woodland and grassland site. Situated on the magnesium limestone ridge overlooking the Vale of York, it hosts nationally important plant species and diverse wildlife. The reserve offers varied habitats for nature.

Name: Lower Brook Meadows (River Anton)
Description: Lower Brook Meadows, a nature reserve near Andover, features water meadows, unimproved grassland, and woodland along the River Anton. It supports diverse wildlife including otters, water shrews, kingfishers, dragonflies, and damselflies. Traditional management practices maintain these vital habitats.

Name: Lower Common (Verwood)
Description: Lower Common near Verwood, Dorset, is a 30-hectare nature reserve. It features mixed woodland, coniferous plantations, and wet and dry heathland. The site supports nightjar, woodcock, sand lizard, and smooth snake. Grazing ponies maintain habitats.

Name: Lower Derwent Valley NNR (Whistling Swan Hub)
Description: The Lower Derwent Valley NNR is a vital wetland in Yorkshire, designated for its importance to migratory birds and diverse aquatic habitats. It features extensive floodplains, wet grasslands, and riverine environments, supporting rare species and providing a crucial wintering ground for wildfowl.

Name: Lower East Prawle
Description: Lower East Prawle is a coastal nature reserve near Start Point in South Devon. It features species-rich grassland, ancient hedgerows, and coastal scrub. The site supports rare plants like fen orchid and various invertebrates. It is managed for biodiversity.

Name: Lower Lewdon
Description: Lower Lewdon is a mosaic of habitats in the Tamar Valley, comprising wet woodland, species-rich grassland, and riverine habitats along the River Lynher. Managed to support a diverse range of wildlife, including otter, dormouse, and various bird species.

Name: Lower Lough Erne Islands (Curlew Hub)
Description: Lower Lough Erne Islands, located in County Fermanagh, Northern Ireland, comprise numerous small islands and the mainland site of Hare Island. This site is a stronghold for breeding waders, particularly curlews. Conservation efforts focus on managing grazing and scrub to maintain suitable habitats.

Name: Lower Marshes
Description: Lower Marshes is a Herefordshire Wildlife Trust nature reserve. It features woodland, grassland, and wetland habitats supporting diverse wildlife, including birds and invertebrates. Conservation efforts focus on habitat management and species protection.

Name: Lower Medway Valley (Habitat Corridors)
Description: The Lower Medway Valley project creates an ecological network of wildlife-rich sites between Maidstone and Rochester. It improves habitats and establishes corridors for various species, enhancing biodiversity across the valley.

Name: Lower Minnetts Wood (Connectivity Hub)
Description: Lower Minnetts Wood is a woodland and grassland reserve near Monmouth, offering diverse habitats for wildlife. Managed by Gwent Wildlife Trust, it contributes to local biodiversity and ecological connectivity.

Name: Lower Moor Farm (Water Park Gateway)
Description: Lower Moor Farm, managed by Wiltshire Wildlife Trust, is a nature reserve at the Cotswold Water Park gateway. It features diverse habitats like wildflower meadows, ponds, and wetlands, supporting various wildlife. Accessible trails and hides are present.

Name: Lower Moors (The SSSI Wetland Hub)
Description: Lower Moors is a freshwater wetland on St Mary's, Scilly, designated as a Site of Special Scientific Interest. It features diverse habitats like fen, reedbed, and wet grassland, supporting rare plants, invertebrates, and migratory birds.

Name: Lower Nethan Gorge
Description: Lower Nethan Gorge is a wooded gorge in South Lanarkshire, protecting ancient woodlands and diverse plant species. The reserve supports wildlife including otters and kingfishers. Visitors can explore marked trails along the River Nethan.

Name: Lower Raypits
Description: Lower Raypits is a coastal grazing marsh on the River Roach estuary in Essex, managed by Essex Wildlife Trust. The reserve features saltmarsh, reedbeds, and freshwater ditches, supporting diverse birdlife and notable plant species.

Name: Lower Smite Farm (Education & BNG Hub)
Description: Lower Smite Farm serves as the Worcestershire Wildlife Trust's headquarters, operating as a working farm managed for wildlife. It includes a nature trail, pond, and wetlands, demonstrating sustainable land management and acting as an education and biodiversity net gain hub.

Name: Lower Test
Description: Lower Test Marshes reserve protects 230 hectares along the River Test floodplain in Southampton. Its diverse habitats, including reedbeds, saltmarsh, grazing marsh, and wet grassland, support a wide array of wetland wildlife. It is managed for nature conservation and public access.

Name: Lower Test Nature Reserve (Southampton)
Description: Lower Test Nature Reserve is a nationally important site near Southampton Water, part of a SSSI. It provides vital habitat for wintering wildfowl and wading birds across diverse environments including mudflats, reedbeds, and meadows. Footpaths and hides allow observation.

Name: Lower Wharfe Valley (Wetland Recovery)
Description: The Lower Wharfe Valley project restores wetland habitats. It creates new scrapes, ponds, wet woodland, and re-profiles ditches to improve biodiversity, water quality, and natural flood management in the area.

Name: Lower Wood Ashwellthorpe
Description: Lower Wood Ashwellthorpe is an ancient woodland in Norfolk, managed by the Norfolk Wildlife Trust. It features diverse tree species and a rich ground flora, supporting various birds and invertebrates. Public access paths are available for visitors.

Name: Lower Woods
Description: Lower Woods is a 285-hectare ancient woodland nature reserve near Wickwar, South Gloucestershire. Comprising 24 individual woods, it holds National Nature Reserve and SSSI status, supporting diverse wildlife, including rare species, butterflies, bats, fungi, and ancient trees.

Name: Lower Woods (managed sections)
Description: Lower Woods is a large ancient woodland in South Gloucestershire, forming a Site of Special Scientific Interest. It comprises various woodland types, including hazel coppice and wet ash-alder, supporting diverse wildlife. The site is actively managed for conservation.

Name: Lowick Common (Medicinal Leech Pond)
Description: Lowick Common is a wet heathland with pools and flushes in Cumbria. It supports a rare population of medicinal leeches, one of only two sites in the county. Other species include bog mosses, sundew, white-faced darter dragonflies, and wading birds.

Name: Lowland Raised Bogs (General)
Description: The Scottish Wildlife Trust manages a project restoring Lowland Raised Bogs across various sites in Scotland. These vital ecosystems are significant carbon stores and support unique biodiversity. Restoration efforts include rewetting and improving bog health.

Name: Loxley Church Meadow
Description: Loxley Church Meadow is a small, secluded traditional hay meadow west of St Nicholas church, Warwickshire. Managed for over 30 years, it represents a fragment of historic grassland. It features greater burnet and diverse meadow flowers, supporting abundant insects.

Name: Loynton Moss (Ice Age Wetland)
Description: Loynton Moss is an internationally important raised bog and a Site of Special Scientific Interest (SSSI). It features a unique relict lowland raised bog habitat with specialist plant and invertebrate species. The site includes open water, swamp, and wet woodland areas.

Name: Lucas’s Marsh (Urban Wetland)
Description: Lucas’s Marsh is an urban wetland in Kirkstall Valley, Leeds. Managed by the Yorkshire Wildlife Trust, it features ponds, ditches, and reedbeds, supporting diverse wildlife and providing a valuable green space.

Name: Luckett / Greenscombe Wood
Description: Greenscombe Wood, a Site of Special Scientific Interest, protects ancient woodland within the Tamar Valley. The reserve features sessile oak, beech, birch, and alder, supporting diverse bird and invertebrate species. River Kensey flows through its lower reaches.

Name: Ludshott Commons (East Hampshire Heathland)
Description: Ludshott Commons is one of the largest remaining areas of heathland in East Hampshire, known for its diverse habitats supporting rare birds, reptiles, and insects. The site features woodlands, open heath, and ponds, offering extensive walking trails. It is a vital area for biodiversity conservation.

Name: Ludwell Valley Park (Cherry Orchard Link)
Description: Ludwell Valley Park is a 3km local nature reserve stretching from Exeter's city boundary to the River Exe. It features woodlands, orchards, meadows, and Ludwell Brook, supporting diverse wildlife habitats. The site offers informal recreation.

Name: Lugg Meadow (Lammas Meadow)
Description: Lugg Meadow is the UK's largest Lammas meadow, managed by commoners with traditional grazing rights. This ancient grassland, beside the River Lugg, supports diverse wading birds, otters, and invertebrates. It hosts rare plants like snake's-head fritillary and floods annually.

Name: Luggiebank Wood
Description: Luggiebank Wood is a mixed woodland near Cumbernauld, supporting diverse wildlife along Luggie Water. It features ancient oaks, alder, ash, and elm, providing habitat for roe deer, woodpeckers, and tawny owls. Paths offer opportunities for quiet riverside walks.

Name: Lullingstone Country Park
Description: Lullingstone Country Park is a Green Flag award-winning park near Eynsford, Kent. It features ancient trees, grasslands, and a wildflower meadow, supporting diverse wildlife. The Darent Valley Path runs through the park, offering walking routes and scenic views. It is managed by Kent Wildlife Trust.

Name: Lullingstone Roman Villa
Description: Lullingstone Roman Villa is a Scheduled Ancient Monument within Lullingstone Country Park, situated in the Darent Valley. Managed by Kent Wildlife Trust, the site features varied habitats including woodlands and grasslands. It supports diverse wildlife and nationally important ancient trees.

Name: Lundy Bay (Cornwall)
Description: Lundy Bay is a secluded sandy cove on the North Cornwall coast, notable for its dramatic cliffs and unique geological formations. The site, managed by the National Trust, provides access to coastal walks along the South West Coast Path.

Name: Lunt Meadows
Description: Lunt Meadows, near Sefton, is a nationally important wetland nature reserve in the Mersey floodplain. The site features recreated reedbed and wet grassland, attracting diverse wading birds. It also holds archaeological significance as a Mesolithic settlement.

Name: LWT Oak Sanctuary
Description: LWT Oak Sanctuary is a small woodland nature reserve located in north London. Managed by the London Wildlife Trust, it features mature oak and hornbeam trees. The site supports diverse birdlife and fungi, offering a valuable urban green space.

Name: LWT Uxbridge Alder Moor
Description: A small wet woodland and marshland nature reserve in Uxbridge. It features mature alder trees and a diverse ground flora, including marsh marigold and ragged-robin. Managed for biodiversity, it provides important habitat for various wildlife species.

Name: LWT Yeading Brook Meadows
Description: LWT Yeading Brook Meadows is a mosaic of grassland, scrub, hedgerows, and mature trees in the Yeading Brook green corridor. This nature reserve, near Ruislip and Southall, provides a tranquil green space. It supports diverse wildflowers, insects, and wildlife, with paths for exploration.

Name: Lydd-on-Sea (Shingle Habitat)
Description: Dungeness, encompassing Lydd-on-Sea's shingle habitat, is a unique landscape. This vast shingle beach supports rare plants, insects, and birds. It is a nationally and internationally important site for wildlife, managed by Kent Wildlife Trust to protect its fragile ecosystem.

Name: Lydden Meadows
Description: Lydden Meadows, a Kent nature reserve managed by the RSPB, features damp meadows, ditches, and woodland. It provides habitat for diverse wildlife, including a nationally important population of the rare marsh fritillary butterfly. Visitors can explore paths.

Name: Lydford Gorge (Deepest SW Gorge)
Description: Lydford Gorge is the deepest gorge in South West England, featuring ancient woodland, dramatic river features, and the impressive 30-metre Whitelady Waterfall. Visitors can explore walking trails through its rich natural landscape.

Name: Lydlinch Common
Description: Lydlinch Common is a 15-hectare Dorset Wildlife Trust nature reserve. It features flower-rich wet meadows, lowland heathland, and ancient woodland. The site supports diverse insects, amphibians, reptiles, and birds. Visitors can explore varied habitats.

Name: Lyme (Moorland & Parkland)
Description: Lyme, a National Trust property in Cheshire, offers a historic house, formal gardens, and a medieval deer park. Located on the edge of the Peak District, it features extensive moorland and parkland for walking and wildlife spotting, including a herd of red deer.

Name: Lyminge Forest
Description: Lyminge Forest lies within the Kent Downs AONB, offering extensive trails for walking, cycling, and horse riding. It features diverse habitats supporting various wildlife species. Forestry England manages the site for public enjoyment and conservation.

Name: Lymington and Keyhaven Marshes
Description: Lymington and Keyhaven Marshes is a coastal nature reserve in Hampshire. It features diverse habitats including saltmarsh, mudflats, and lagoons. The site is internationally important for migratory and wading birds, supporting rare plants and insects.

Name: Lymington and Keyhaven Marshes (NNR)
Description: Lymington and Keyhaven Marshes is a nationally important site along the Solent, featuring extensive saltmarshes, mudflats, and shingle banks. It supports diverse migratory birds, including avocets and terns, plus rare plants. Coastal defences protect fragile wetland habitats.

Name: Lymington Reedbeds
Description: Lymington Reedbeds is a vital freshwater reedbed habitat on the Lymington Estuary. Managed by Hampshire and Isle of Wight Wildlife Trust, it supports significant numbers of breeding and wintering birds, including Cetti's warblers and bearded tits. It is part of a larger SSSI and SPA.

Name: Lyndon Visitor Centre (Osprey Hub)
Description: Lyndon Visitor Centre is a primary hub for viewing ospreys at Rutland Water Nature Reserve. The site offers accessible trails, bird hides, and visitor information on local wildlife and the successful osprey reintroduction programme.

Name: Lyndon Visitor Centre & Reserve (Osprey Site)
Description: Lyndon Visitor Centre on Rutland Water’s south shore is part of Anglian Water Birdwatching Centre. It offers views of diverse wildlife, including Rutland Ospreys, from hides and via a dedicated team. The centre provides key osprey observation points.

Name: Lynford Arboretum and Water Garden
Description: Lynford Arboretum features a diverse collection of trees, a picturesque water garden, and walking trails. It provides habitat for various bird species, including rare raptors. Visitors can explore a sensory garden and observe wildlife.

Name: Mab Lane Community Woodland (Liverpool)
Description: Mab Lane Community Woodland is a 28-hectare site in Liverpool, established in 2004. It features native trees and shrubs, creating wildlife habitats. The woodland offers walking paths and green space for residents to enjoy nature.

Name: Macclesfield Forest (Peak District Edge)
Description: Macclesfield Forest is an upland woodland and reservoir complex on the western edge of the Peak District, Cheshire. Managed by Forestry England, it features diverse habitats including conifer plantations and broadleaf woodlands, supporting varied wildlife.

Name: Maes Hiraddug (Limestone Grassland)
Description: Maes Hiraddug is a limestone grassland nature reserve near Dyserth, managed by North Wales Wildlife Trust. It supports diverse flora, including various orchid species, and offers views of the Clwydian Hills. The site is notable for its rich invertebrate life and specific butterfly species.

Name: Magog Down (Arable to Grassland Restoration)
Description: Magog Down is 163.5 acres of former arable land on the Gog Magog Hills, south of Cambridge. Transformed since 1989, it is now a habitat for wildlife, supports species diversity, and offers quiet recreation and educational visits.

Name: Magor Marsh SSSI (The Fenland Ark)
Description: Magor Marsh is Gwent Wildlife Trust's only remaining fenland habitat and a Site of Special Scientific Interest (SSSI). It is a National Nature Reserve (NNR) featuring reedbeds, wet woodland, grasslands, and open water. Accessible boardwalks and a viewing hide are present.

Name: Magor Meadow (New Wetland Creation)
Description: A new wetland is being created at Magor Marsh nature reserve to improve flood attenuation, enhance biodiversity, and capture carbon. This project transforms a 2-hectare river meadow, linking two existing wetland sites and providing habitat for various species.

Name: Magpie Bottom
Description: Magpie Bottom is a chalk downland valley near Barham, Kent, managed by Kent Wildlife Trust. The reserve features slopes with wildflowers like cowslips and orchids, supporting various butterflies including the Adonis blue. Ancient woodland is also present.

Name: Maidenhead Thicket
Description: Maidenhead Thicket is an ancient woodland and common near Maidenhead, Berkshire, managed by the National Trust. It features diverse habitats including mature trees, heathland, and ponds, supporting varied wildlife. Public footpaths offer walking and nature observation opportunities.

Name: Maldon Wick Meadow
Description: Maldon Wick Meadow is a grassland reserve managed by Essex Wildlife Trust. It features ancient ridge and furrow, indicating historic agricultural use. The site supports a variety of wildflowers and invertebrates.

Name: Malham Tarn Estate (High Altitude Limestone)
Description: Malham Tarn Estate, within the Yorkshire Dales National Park, encompasses a high-altitude lake. This National Nature Reserve hosts diverse habitats, including fen, woodland, grassland, and limestone pavement, supporting unique wildlife.

Name: Mallards Pike - Forest of Dean
Description: Mallards Pike is a popular recreation site within the Forest of Dean, Gloucestershire. It features a large lake, walking trails, cycle routes, and a cafe. Activities include paddleboarding, kayaking, and archery. Go Ape offers treetop adventures.

Name: Malling Down (Flower-rich Chalk Grassland)
Description: Malling Down is a large area of flower-rich chalk grassland above the River Ouse near Lewes, East Sussex. Part of the Lewes Downs SSSI, it is a stronghold for rare plants and invertebrates. Grazing maintains its ecological value, offering panoramic views of the South Downs.

Name: Maltby Low Common
Description: Maltby Low Common is a nature reserve near Rotherham, South Yorkshire. It features ancient woodland, wetlands, and open grassland, supporting diverse wildlife. The site is managed for conservation and public enjoyment.

Name: Mambury Moor (Smooth Culm Rarity)
Description: Mambury Moor is a 24-hectare nature reserve with a mix of grassland and marshy areas near Okehampton. It is important for its large population of smooth culm-grass. The reserve supports rare plants and diverse insect life.

Name: Manesty Woods (Wet Woodland Flagship)
Description: Manesty Woods is a key wet woodland habitat within Borrowdale, forming part of a wider project aimed at restoring and protecting these unique ecosystems. It supports a diverse range of plants, mosses, fungi, and invertebrates in the Lake District.

Name: Manor Farm
Description: Manor Farm is a Surrey Wildlife Trust nature reserve comprising wet meadows, woodlands, and scrub along the River Wey. This mosaic of habitats supports diverse flora and fauna, including orchids, butterflies, kingfishers, and otters. It offers opportunities for quiet walks.

Name: Mansey Common
Description: Mansey Common is a wetland nature reserve south of Sutton-cum-Lound. Habitats include wet woodland, fen, and grassland, supporting diverse wildlife like dragonflies, damselflies, and birds. A boardwalk provides visitor access.

Name: Mansey Common (Wet Grassland)
Description: Mansey Common is a wet grassland and marshy area in Nottinghamshire, near the River Idle. It hosts diverse flora, including blunt-flowered rush. Wading birds like snipe and lapwing, and otters, are present. The site is managed for wetland conservation.

Name: Mapledurwell Fen (Rare Chalk Fen)
Description: Mapledurwell Fen is a rare chalk fen, a Site of Special Scientific Interest near Basingstoke. It features alkaline mires and wet woodlands, supporting a rich diversity of plant and invertebrate species, including various orchids. This unique habitat is crucial for biodiversity.

Name: Mapledurwell Fen and The Hatch
Description: Mapledurwell Fen and The Hatch is a Hampshire nature reserve near Basingstoke, featuring diverse fen, woodland, and marsh habitats. A chalk stream supports rare plants and insects, including various orchids and dragonflies, within its rich ecosystem.

Name: Mapperley Wood
Description: Mapperley Wood is an ancient woodland near Ilkeston, Derbyshire, featuring mature oak, ash, birch, and hazel trees. It provides habitats for diverse wildlife, including birds and insects, with a network of paths for visitors. Managed by Derbyshire Wildlife Trust, the site focuses on conservation.

Name: Mar Lodge Estate NNR (Cairngorms)
Description: Mar Lodge Estate National Nature Reserve is a vast wilderness covering over 7% of the Cairngorms National Park. It features four of the five highest mountains in the UK and Caledonian pine forest. The estate encompasses wild land and habitats.

Name: Marbury Reedbed
Description: Marbury Reedbed, a Cheshire Wildlife Trust reserve near Marbury Country Park, features extensive reedbeds. These provide a vital habitat for endangered birds such as bitterns and bearded tits. The site supports local conservation and offers birdwatching opportunities.

Name: March Haigh (Landscapes for Water)
Description: March Haigh is a key component of the Landscapes for Water project on Marsden Moor. This initiative aims to restore peatlands to natural conditions, improving water quality and increasing biodiversity. The project reduces flood risk and enhances carbon capture, supporting local ecosystems.

Name: Marden Meadows (Unploughed Hay Meadow)
Description: Marden Meadows is an ancient, unploughed hay meadow in Kent, supporting diverse wildflowers like green-winged orchids, cowslips, and adder's-tongue fern. Managed by Kent Wildlife Trust, it provides a vital natural habitat for insects and other wildlife.

Name: Marden Park (Woldingham)
Description: Marden Park is a scenic valley reserve near Woldingham, featuring ancient woodland and chalk grassland. Home to pyramidal orchids and common blue butterflies, the site offers extensive views and supports diverse wildlife habitats. Managed for conservation.

Name: Marford Quarry (Invertebrate Capital)
Description: Marford Quarry, a disused limestone quarry in Wrexham, Wales, is a Site of Special Scientific Interest. It features diverse habitats like limestone grassland and ponds, supporting a rich invertebrate population. North Wales Wildlife Trust manages the site.

Name: Margrove Heritage Centre & Reserve
Description: Margrove Heritage Centre, a former ironstone mine near Guisborough, is managed by Tees Valley Wildlife Trust. The reserve features diverse habitats including grasslands, wetlands, and woodlands. It offers accessible paths and a visitor centre.

Name: Mark's Corner & Briddlesford Woods
Description: Mark's Corner & Briddlesford Woods is a small ancient woodland and SSSI, an extension of Briddlesford Copse in Dorset. It features fine oak standards, typical ancient woodland flora, and is managed by Dorset Wildlife Trust, with grazing by Poll Dorset cattle.

Name: Market Weston Fen
Description: Market Weston Fen is a Suffolk Wildlife Trust nature reserve in Suffolk. This site features rich fens, wet meadows, and scrub, home to rare plants and insects. Designated a Site of Special Scientific Interest, it protects unique wetland habitats and biodiversity.

Name: Marks Hall
Description: Marks Hall is a nature reserve near Coggeshall, Essex, featuring ancient woodlands, ponds, and meadows. Managed by Essex Wildlife Trust, it supports diverse wildlife, including various bird species and invertebrates. Visitors can explore walking trails through different habitats.

Name: Marks Hall Estate (Arboretum & SSSI Wood)
Description: Marks Hall Estate in Essex features an internationally important arboretum, ancient woodlands, and a historic walled garden. The site encompasses lakes, botanical collections, and is designated as a Site of Special Scientific Interest.

Name: Markstakes Common (Chailey Heath/Woodland)
Description: Markstakes Common, owned by the Woodland Trust, is an ancient woodland and heathland site in Chailey, East Sussex. Its mosaic of habitats supports diverse flora and fauna. The common is actively managed for conservation.

Name: Marley Common (Chestnut Woodland & Heath)
Description: Marley Common, near Haslemere in Surrey, features historic chestnut woodland, open heathland, and steep wooded glades. This National Trust site provides rich and varied habitats, supporting diverse wildlife including rare birds and insects.

Name: Marloes Sands & Mere (NT Coastal)
Description: Marloes Sands is a wild, remote beach on the Marloes Peninsula in Pembrokeshire, Wales, known for its rock pools and geological formations. Adjacent is Marloes Mere, a freshwater habitat supporting diverse wetland wildlife.

Name: Marsden Moor Estate (Landscape for Water)
Description: Over 5,000 acres of moorland in the South Pennines, Marsden Moor Estate is a Site of Special Scientific Interest. It features blanket bog habitats and is crucial for birdlife, with a rich industrial and ancient heritage.

Name: Marshes Hill Common (Heathland Island)
Description: Marshes Hill Common is a precious heathland habitat, home to various specialist plants and invertebrates. It is an isolated 'island' of heathland within a surrounding area of farmland and woodland, making it a unique ecological site. The reserve is managed for biodiversity.

Name: Marsland (Atlantic Rainforest Gully)
Description: Marsland nature reserve, straddling the Devon/Cornwall border, encompasses a coastal valley. It features ancient woodland, heathland, and coastal cliffs, supporting diverse wildlife. The gully environment is characteristic of an Atlantic rainforest.

Name: Marsland Valley
Description: Marsland Valley is a secluded nature reserve on the Devon-Cornwall border, managed by Devon Wildlife Trust. It features ancient woodland, grasslands, and a stream flowing to the sea. The valley supports otters, dormice, and various bird species.

Name: Martham Broad
Description: Martham Broad, part of the Norfolk Broads, is a Site of Special Scientific Interest for wildlife, especially birds. Owned by Norfolk Wildlife Trust, it features reedswamp and wet woodland, supporting species like Bittern, Marsh Harrier, and diverse insects. Access is primarily by canoe or kayak.

Name: Martham Broad and Marshes
Description: Martham Broad and Marshes is a private nature reserve and Site of Special Scientific Interest in Norfolk. It features one of the largest expanses of open water on the Broads, surrounded by diverse reedbeds and wet woodland. The site supports a variety of rare plants and invertebrates.

Name: Martham Broad and Marshes (NNR)
Description: Martham Broad and Marshes is a National Nature Reserve in Norfolk, part of the Upper Thurne Broads and Marshes SSSI. Managed by Norfolk Wildlife Trust, it features open water, reedbeds, and fens, supporting diverse wildlife including rare plants, otters, and many bird species.

Name: Martham Ferry
Description: Martham Broad and Marshes, managed by Norfolk Wildlife Trust, features extensive reedbeds, fens, and scrub within the Broads National Park. This diverse habitat supports marsh harriers, bitterns, otters, and kingfishers, forming part of a significant SSSI.

Name: Martins' Meadows
Description: Martin's Meadows is a small, species-rich grassland in Suffolk, designated as a Site of Special Scientific Interest. It comprises a flower-rich hay meadow, cut in late summer, and is part of the Stour Valley Living Landscape project.

Name: Martlesham Wilds
Description: Martlesham Wilds is a 140-hectare nature recovery project near Martlesham and Woodbridge in Suffolk. This site includes woodlands, grasslands, and wetlands, managed to benefit diverse wildlife and provide public access.

Name: Maryculter Slasher
Description: Maryculter Slasher is a small woodland reserve near Maryculter, 10 miles west of Aberdeen. It features Scots pine, birch, and oak, supporting tits, finches, woodpeckers, and red squirrels. A circular path allows for easy walks through the site, situated on the south side of the River Dee valley.

Name: Masbury Camp (Iron Age Connectivity)
Description: Masbury Camp is a prominent Iron Age hillfort site near Shepton Mallet in Somerset. It features visible earthworks, including banks and ditches, and offers panoramic views across the Mendip Hills. The site is a Scheduled Ancient Monument and provides habitat for diverse flora and fauna, including wildflowers and butterflies.

Name: Mascall’s Wood
Description: Mascall's Wood is a small, ancient semi-natural woodland reserve near Tatworth, south Somerset. It features mature oak, ash, and hazel trees with a diverse ground flora. The site supports various bird species and invertebrates, with traditional woodland management ongoing.

Name: Matlock Moors (Cromford Moor)
Description: Matlock Moors in Derbyshire includes Cromford Moor and Matlock Forest. It features diverse wildlife, accessible trails, and views across the Derwent Valley. Matlock Forest offers walking, cycling, and mountain biking routes, with bird and wildlife spotting.

Name: Mayes Brook Park (Climate Change Park)
Description: Thames21 transforms Mayes Brook Park into an urban wetland, improving water quality, enhancing biodiversity, and increasing flood resilience. This project creates new green spaces for the local Barking community.

Name: Mayon Cliff & Land's End Coast
Description: Mayon and Sennen Cliffs, located on the Land's End peninsula in Cornwall, features dramatic granite clifftops, diverse wildlife, and archaeological remains. The site provides views of the Atlantic, coastal heathland, and habitat for choughs and peregrine falcons.

Name: Maze Park
Description: Maze Park is a former landfill site in Middlesbrough, transformed into a mosaic of grassland, ponds, and woodland habitats. Adjacent to the River Tees, it hosts diverse wildlife, including breeding birds and rare insects. The reserve offers valuable greenspace with views of the Transporter Bridge.

Name: Meadow Farm (Wildflower Meadows)
Description: Meadow Farm is a significant site for nature conservation due to its species-rich meadows and ancient hedgerows. It provides a habitat for various wildflowers, insects, and birds. The reserve is carefully managed to enhance its biodiversity.

Name: Meathop Moss
Description: Meathop Moss, a lowland raised bog in South Lakeland, Cumbria, is an internationally important habitat. It supports sphagnum mosses, cotton grasses, and sundews. Various dragonflies, damselflies, and butterflies thrive here. Cumbria Wildlife Trust manages this reserve.

Name: Meer Court
Description: Meer Court is a wildflower meadow and former orchard, rich in plants and insects, managed for biodiversity. It is located near Stoke Edith in Herefordshire, offering a diverse habitat.

Name: Meeth Quarry
Description: Meeth Quarry is a former quarry in North Devon, now a large nature reserve. It features species-rich grasslands, heathland, woodland, and ponds. The site provides habitats for diverse birds, insects, and plants.

Name: Meeth Quarry (Industrial to Oasis)
Description: Meeth Quarry Nature Reserve is a former industrial site in Devon, transformed into a mosaic of wetlands, grasslands, and woodlands. It supports diverse wildlife, including dragonflies, butterflies, and various bird species. Visitors can explore walking and cycling trails.

Name: Melford Hall (River Meadows)
Description: Melford Hall is a Grade I listed Elizabethan manor house in Long Melford, Suffolk. The property features historical interiors, formal gardens, parkland, and riverside meadows along the River Stour. It offers visitor access to the house and grounds.

Name: Melincwrt Falls (Turner's Rainforest Falls)
Description: Melincwrt Falls is a woodland reserve in Neath Port Talbot, South Wales, known for its waterfall and ancient woodland. The site supports various mosses, ferns, and lichens, thriving in the moist environment.

Name: Mellis Common
Description: Mellis Common is a traditional grazing common, important for its array of grassland fungi, including waxcaps. Part of the site is wet pasture, supporting plants like marsh orchid. Scrub clearance and grazing maintain the habitat, benefiting diverse wildlife.

Name: Mellis Common (Traditional Grazing)
Description: Mellis Common is a large traditional common in Suffolk, managed by Suffolk Wildlife Trust. It features species-rich grassland, wet fen habitats, and ancient oak trees, grazed by cattle. It supports diverse flora, including Marsh-marigold and Southern Marsh-orchid, and many insect species.

Name: Melverley Meadows (Traditional Hay Meadow)
Description: Melverley Meadows comprises two traditionally managed hay meadows adjacent to the River Vyrnwy in Shropshire. The site supports a rich diversity of grassland flora, including betony, greater bird's-foot trefoil, and meadow rue. Autumn grazing after the hay cut maintains the diverse sward, benefiting insects and ground-nesting birds.

Name: Mere Sands Wood
Description: Mere Sands Wood is a diverse nature reserve featuring woodlands, wetlands, and open water, offering habitats for various bird species, dragonflies, and butterflies. Footpaths and hides provide visitor access and wildlife viewing opportunities.

Name: Meresfelle (Pristine Culm Remnant)
Description: Meresfelle is a nature reserve near Holsworthy, Devon, managed by Devon Wildlife Trust. This site protects a rare fragment of pristine culm grassland, a habitat unique to the South West of England. It features acidic grassland, rush pasture, and wet heath, supporting diverse flora and fauna.

Name: Mereway Nature Park and Orchard
Description: Mereway Nature Park and Orchard is one of several West London sites where The Conservation Volunteers Biodiversity Action Teams improve wildlife habitats. Activities include tree and hedge planting, woodland management, and pond work.

Name: Merry's Meadows
Description: Merry's Meadows, a Leicestershire and Rutland Wildlife Trust nature reserve near Stathern, is a Site of Special Scientific Interest. The reserve preserves ancient ridge and furrow within a diverse wildflower meadow, supporting various insects and plant species.

Name: Mersehead (Solway Coast)
Description: RSPB Mersehead is a wetland reserve on the Solway Coast in Dumfries and Galloway, Scotland. It hosts a significant wintering population of barnacle geese. The reserve features saltmarsh, wetlands, and farmland, with hides available for wildlife observation.

Name: Merstham Gault (Wetland/Chalk interface)
Description: Merstham Gault is a Surrey Wildlife Trust reserve on the Greensand Ridge, within the Surrey Hills AONB. This wetland site features wet woodland, unimproved grassland, and ponds fed by chalk springs. It supports diverse wildlife, including invertebrates, birds, and plants, with water voles present.

Name: Merthyr Mawr NNR (The Big Dipper)
Description: Merthyr Mawr National Nature Reserve is a vast area of sand dunes in South Wales, featuring the second-highest dune in Europe, known as the 'Big Dipper'. This diverse landscape includes coastal habitats, woodland, and the River Ogmore, supporting a variety of rare plants and wildlife, including specialist dune species.

Name: Meshaw Moor (Unchanged Culm Ark)
Description: Meshaw Moor, a Devon Wildlife Trust nature reserve north of Exeter, protects 40 hectares of rare Culm Grassland. This wet, acidic habitat supports marsh fritillary butterflies, sundews, and sphagnum mosses, alongside diverse bird species. Managed by grazing, it maintains optimal conditions for biodiversity.

Name: Messingham Sand Quarry
Description: Messingham Sand Quarry, a former quarry, is now a Lincolnshire Wildlife Trust nature reserve. It features diverse habitats like wet woodland, marsh, grassland, and open water. The site supports dragonflies, butterflies, birds, and amphibians, providing a rich habitat.

Name: Micheldever Wood
Description: Micheldever Wood is located north of Winchester, near the village of Micheldever. It is known for its spectacular display of bluebells in late April and early May. The wood also features a Roman road, ancient trees, and diverse wildlife, making it a popular spot for walkers.

Name: Mickfield Meadow
Description: Mickfield Meadow is a species-rich ancient hay meadow in Suffolk, managed by Suffolk Wildlife Trust. It supports green-winged orchids, adder's tongue fern, and diverse meadow grasses. Regular hay cutting and grazing maintain its biodiversity. Public access is available.

Name: Mickfield Meadow (Unploughed Hay Meadow)
Description: Mickfield Meadow is an ancient, unploughed hay meadow in Suffolk, known for its rich diversity of wildflowers and insects. It stands as one of Suffolk's premier examples of species-rich grassland, providing vital habitat for various species.

Name: Mickle Mere
Description: Mickle Mere is a mixed habitat reserve in Suffolk, featuring a shallow lake fringed with reed and fen. The site includes wet woodland, ancient hedgerows, and species-rich grassland, supporting diverse wildlife.

Name: Middle Hope
Description: Middle Hope is a coastal headland on the Bristol Channel, featuring limestone grassland, ancient hedgerows, and scrub habitats. The site supports diverse wildlife, including peregrine falcons, linnets, and rare plants, and is managed for nature conservation and public access.

Name: Middlebriars Wood
Description: Middlebriars Wood is a small ancient woodland reserve in Dorking, Surrey. It features mature oak, ash, and hazel trees, providing a habitat for woodland birds and wildflowers. The site offers peaceful walking trails for visitors.

Name: Middleton Down (Traditional Chalk Valley)
Description: Middleton Down features a traditional chalk valley and ancient woodland in Wiltshire, hosting diverse flora including orchids and numerous butterfly species. The site is actively managed to conserve its biodiverse habitats.

Name: Middleton Moor (Rewilding Project)
Description: Middleton Moor, a former limestone quarry near Bakewell, is managed by Derbyshire Wildlife Trust. The site undergoes rewilding, transforming it into a diverse, thriving landscape. Efforts focus on restoring peatland, creating various habitats, and boosting biodiversity for unique wildlife. It is a long-term conservation project.

Name: Middletown
Description: Middletown Nature Reserve is a grassland habitat near Pickering, North Yorkshire. It features unimproved meadows rich in wildflowers and grasses, supporting a variety of insect life, including butterflies and moths. The reserve also provides habitat for ground-nesting birds.

Name: Midger Wood
Description: Midger Wood is an ancient woodland in South Gloucestershire, featuring mature ash, beech, and oak trees. It provides diverse habitats for birds, mammals, and invertebrates, with seasonal wildflowers creating a vibrant understory. The site is managed for biodiversity by Avon Wildlife Trust.

Name: Midger Wood (Cotswold Escarpment Ancient Wood)
Description: Midger Wood is a steep ancient woodland on the Cotswold Escarpment, managed for biodiversity. It features mature trees, diverse flora like bluebells and wild garlic, and supports various bird species. The reserve offers walking trails with seasonal highlights.

Name: Migneint Plateau (The Sponge of Eryri)
Description: The Migneint plateau is a vast upland area of blanket bog in Eryri (Snowdonia), North Wales. It acts as a crucial carbon store and a natural flood defence, feeding numerous rivers. This globally important landscape supports rare wildlife.

Name: Milborne Port Community Conservation
Description: Milborne Port Community Conservation manages local green spaces, enhancing biodiversity and creating wildlife habitats. The team organises tree planting and other ecological projects, collaborating with local residents and volunteers.

Name: Mile End Park (Eco Pavilion)
Description: Mile End Park is a 32-hectare linear park in East London, created on former industrial land and a WWII bomb site. It features an Ecology Park, Children's Park, and sports facilities. The Eco Pavilion serves as an educational and community hub within the park.

Name: Milford Cuttings (Wildflower Railway)
Description: Milford Cuttings is a former railway line near Lough Neagh, now a nature reserve managed by Ulster Wildlife. It features diverse wildflowers and grasses, supporting a range of insects and birds. The site provides a valuable wildlife corridor and a quiet space for visitors.

Name: Milford Green and Coxhill Green
Description: Milford Green and Coxhill Green comprise damp meadows, acid grassland, and woodland. The reserve supports plant species typical of acid soils, including purple moor-grass and common cottongrass. It provides habitat for diverse insects and birds, contributing to local biodiversity.

Name: Milkhall Pond
Description: Milkhall Pond, a lowland pond and wetland in Dumfries and Galloway, provides habitat for waterfowl and waders. Managed by the Scottish Wildlife Trust, its surrounding grasslands and scrub offer shelter for diverse wildlife, forming a key local haven.

Name: Mill Crook
Description: Mill Crook is a small wet grassland nature reserve bordering the River Nene in Northamptonshire. Ditches and a pond support aquatic plants and invertebrates. Notable species include greater water parsnip and great fen-sedge.

Name: Mill Dam (Shapinsay)
Description: Mill Dam on Shapinsay is an RSPB reserve featuring a small freshwater loch and adjacent marshland. It provides a vital breeding and feeding ground for a variety of wetland birds, including lapwings, redshanks, and waders. The site is important for local biodiversity.

Name: Mill Ham
Description: Mill Ham is a tranquil wet meadow and woodland, formerly part of the Sherborne Castle Estate, bordering the River Yeo. This reserve supports diverse wildlife, including otters and kingfishers. Traditional management methods are used to maintain its ecological value.

Name: Mill Meadows
Description: Mill Meadows, Billericay, is a diverse nature reserve in Essex, featuring ancient unimproved grassland, woodland, and a pond. This site supports a rich variety of wildflowers, butterflies, and birds, including scarce species. It is actively managed to preserve its unique habitats and biodiversity.

Name: Millennium Wood (Exeter)
Description: Millennium Wood is a small urban woodland in Exeter, Devon. Planted in 2000, it features a mix of broadleaf trees and offers a tranquil green space for local residents. It provides habitat for diverse wildlife.

Name: Miller's Dale Quarry
Description: Miller's Dale Quarry, a former limestone quarry in the Peak District, is now a Derbyshire Wildlife Trust nature reserve. It features diverse habitats including grassland, scrub, and woodland, supporting various plants and animals. Rare orchids and butterflies are present, and public access is available via footpaths.

Name: Millfield Wood (High Wycombe)
Description: Millfield Wood is a Site of Special Scientific Interest (SSSI) in Buckinghamshire. Ancient woodland hosts diverse trees including hornbeam, hazel, and field maple. Seasonal wildflowers carpet the woodland floor, supporting varied birdlife and invertebrates.

Name: Millington Wood (Yorkshire Wolds)
Description: Millington Wood is a large ancient woodland and local nature reserve in the Yorkshire Wolds. It features diverse flora and fauna, including notable butterfly populations. Managed for conservation and public access, it offers walking trails within its varied habitats.

Name: Millisons Wood
Description: Millisons Wood is an ancient woodland near Coventry, dominated by oak and hazel coppice. It features diverse ground flora including bluebells, wood anemone, and yellow archangel. The reserve supports a range of woodland birds and insects. Paths are flat, suitable for most visitors.

Name: Milton Creek Country Park (Urban Coastal)
Description: Milton Creek Country Park in Sittingbourne, Kent, is an urban coastal park with diverse habitats including saltmarsh and reedbeds. It offers walking, cycling, and nature observation along the Swale Estuary. The site is popular for community events.

Name: Milton Locks
Description: Milton Locks Nature Reserve, on Langstone Harbour's eastern shore in Portsmouth, is a small wetland site. It features saltmarsh, brackish pools, and reedbeds, providing habitat for various wading birds and wildfowl. Managed for wildlife, it offers public access.

Name: Milton Locks Nature Reserve (Portsmouth Oasis)
Description: Milton Locks Nature Reserve is a small urban site at the eastern end of Langstone Harbour in Portsmouth. It features saltmarsh, mudflats, and a small area of scrub and grassland, providing habitat for various bird species and marine invertebrates. The reserve offers views across the harbour.

Name: Minchinhampton & Rodborough Commons
Description: Minchinhampton and Rodborough Commons are extensive limestone grasslands in the Cotswolds. These biodiverse sites host rare plants, insects, and butterflies. Cattle graze freely, maintaining the unique habitat.

Name: Mincinglake Valley Park (Orchard & Meadow)
Description: Mincinglake Valley Park is located in Exeter, featuring flower-rich meadows, young and mature woodlands, hedgerows, and ponds. It offers various paths, some steep, for local visitors on foot or bicycle. Managed by Devon Wildlife Trust and Exeter City Council.

Name: Minera Quarry (Industrial Limestone)
Description: Minera Quarry is an old limestone quarry and lead mine near Wrexham, designated a nature reserve in 1989. This site, leased by North Wales Wildlife Trust, features limestone grassland, woodland, and wetland. It combines industrial archaeology, including calcining kilns, with diverse natural habitats, offering public access.

Name: Mingulay
Description: Mingulay is an uninhabited island in Scotland's Outer Hebrides, owned by the National Trust for Scotland. It is renowned for its towering sea cliffs and significant seabird colonies, including puffins and guillemots. The island also holds archaeological remnants of its past human settlements.

Name: Minnowburn (Belfast Urban Fringe)
Description: Minnowburn is a tranquil riverside property located within Lagan Valley Regional Park near Belfast. It features extensive walking trails, diverse woodland, meadows, and historic buildings including the Mary Peters Track. Visitors enjoy its varied natural habitats and scenic paths.

Name: Minsmere
Description: Minsmere is an RSPB nature reserve on the Suffolk coast, featuring diverse habitats like reedbeds, woodlands, and heathland. Visitors can explore trails, hides, and an accessible discovery area, observing marsh harriers, bitterns, and avocets.

Name: Minsterley Meadows
Description: Minsterley Meadows is a Shropshire Wildlife Trust reserve near Minsterley. It features a mosaic of damp grassland, fen, and rush pasture. The site supports diverse plant species including marsh orchids and ragged-robin, providing important habitat for local wildlife.

Name: Misson Carr
Description: Misson Carr is a surviving remnant of Sherwood Forest, featuring ancient peat bogs, wet woodlands, and open water. It provides habitat for rare plants and wildlife, rich in biodiversity, and is a Site of Special Scientific Interest.

Name: Misson Carr (Wet Woodland SSSI)
Description: Misson Carr is a wet woodland SSSI in North Nottinghamshire, managed by Nottinghamshire Wildlife Trust. It represents a fragment of the ancient Sherwood Forest wetland system. The site supports willow tits, great spotted woodpeckers, and diverse invertebrates, with walking trails through alder and birch woodland.

Name: Moncreiffe Hill (Perth)
Description: Moncreiffe Hill features mature woodland with walking trails, an Iron Age hill fort, and panoramic views over Perth, the River Tay, and the Earn valley. It is home to diverse wildlife and offers clear routes.

Name: Monks Dale (Limestone Ash Woodland)
Description: Monks Dale is a limestone ash woodland within the Derbyshire Dales National Nature Reserve. It features species-rich grassland and ancient ash woodland. The dale is part of the Peak District National Park and home to several rare plant species.

Name: Monkwood (Butterfly Sanctuary)
Description: Monkwood is an ancient woodland in Worcestershire, managed by Worcestershire Wildlife Trust. It is renowned for its butterflies and moths, supporting dormice and diverse species. Management focuses on enhancing its significant biodiversity.

Name: Montgomeryshire Curlew Project
Description: The Montgomeryshire Curlew Project protects the UK's most threatened wading bird. It engages landowners and farmers in Montgomeryshire, monitoring and safeguarding breeding pairs and their nests. Focused efforts secure vital habitats.

Name: Montrose Basin
Description: Montrose Basin is a large enclosed tidal estuary in Angus, Scotland, covering 750 hectares. A designated Local Nature Reserve and SSSI, it serves as a vital feeding and resting ground for thousands of migratory birds, including pink-footed geese and wigeon.

Name: Moor Copse
Description: Moor Copse is a wet woodland nature reserve in the Pang Valley, Berkshire. It comprises two distinct woodland areas with meadows, reedbed, and a small stream. Diverse flora and fauna thrive, including rare sedges and various bird species. The reserve offers walking trails.

Name: Moor Farm
Description: Moor Farm is a 75-hectare nature reserve near Witham St Hughs, Lincolnshire, featuring wildflower-rich grassland and a network of ditches. The site supports nationally important populations of water vole, alongside diverse insect life. The reserve is managed to enhance its biodiversity.

Name: Moorbridge Pond
Description: Moorbridge Pond is a small nature reserve near Bulwell, north of Nottingham. It features a pond, stream, and marshy grassland, surrounded by woodland. The site supports diverse aquatic invertebrates, amphibians, and bird species. Local groups aid habitat management.

Name: Moorlands
Description: Moorlands Nature Reserve is a hidden woodland and wetland gem near York. It features ponds, ditches, and reedbeds, supporting diverse wildlife including dragonflies, amphibians, and various bird species like kingfishers and warblers. The reserve offers peaceful paths for visitors.

Name: Moors Valley Country Park & Forest
Description: Moors Valley Country Park and Forest offers diverse outdoor activities in East Dorset, including walking trails, cycling, and adventure play. Managed by Forestry England, it features a Tree Top Walk, steam railway, and Gruffalo trail for families and nature enthusiasts.

Name: Morden Hall Park (National Trust)
Description: Morden Hall Park is a tranquil oasis in south London. The River Wandle flows through the park, which features meadows, wetlands, and a rose garden. Historically a deer park, it now offers open spaces, a cafe, and a garden centre for visitors to explore.

Name: Mordon Carrs
Description: Mordon Carrs is a wetland nature reserve in County Durham, featuring fen, wet woodland, and reedbeds. It supports diverse wildlife, including various bird species, invertebrates, and rare plants. The site contributes to local biodiversity.

Name: Moreton Forest
Description: Moreton Forest, near Dorchester in Dorset, offers waymarked trails for walking, cycling, and horse riding. This tranquil woodland features mature trees and open heathland, providing recreational opportunities and a connection to nature for visitors.

Name: Morgan's Hill (Butterfly Capital)
Description: Morgan's Hill is a chalk downland nature reserve near Calne, Wiltshire. It hosts over 25 butterfly species, including Adonis and chalkhill blue, alongside diverse wildflowers and ancient earthworks. Panoramic views across the Pewsey Vale are visible.

Name: Morley Brickyard
Description: Morley Brickyard is a Derbyshire Wildlife Trust nature reserve located on a former brickworks site. It features diverse habitats including a large pond, developing woodland, and grassland, supporting a variety of amphibians, invertebrates, birds, and small mammals.

Name: Morley Dale
Description: Morley Dale is an ancient woodland, home to bluebells, wild garlic, and ancient oak and ash trees. It is a nationally important site due to its diverse mosses, liverworts, and fungi, managed by Derbyshire Wildlife Trust.

Name: Morningside Meadows (Royal Wootton Bassett)
Description: Morningside Meadows is a small nature reserve in Royal Wootton Bassett, Wiltshire, managed by Wiltshire Wildlife Trust. It features two traditional hay meadows, supporting diverse grassland flowers and insect species. Public footpaths provide access to the site.

Name: Morridge Hill Country (Landscape Recovery)
Description: Morridge Hill Country is a 1,000-hectare landscape recovery area in Staffordshire, encompassing upland grasslands, moorland, and river valleys. The project aims to restore peatlands, enhance biodiversity, and improve water quality.

Name: Morston Quay
Description: Morston Quay serves as the gateway to Blakeney Point, a significant Norfolk wildlife haven. Managed by the National Trust, it offers seal trips and is a hub for nature watching. The quay supports conservation efforts along the North Norfolk coast.

Name: Morte Point
Description: Morte Point is a dramatic headland of rugged cliffs and rocky coves near Woolacombe. The reserve features stunning views out to sea, and a diverse range of wildlife including seals, peregrine falcons, and rare plants. It is a key coastal site in North Devon.

Name: Mortimer Forest (Ludlow Edge)
Description: Mortimer Forest near Ludlow spans the Shropshire Hills and Welsh border. This diverse woodland offers walking, cycling, and horse riding trails. Home to red deer and buzzards, it provides scenic views and recreational opportunities.

Name: Morville Hall (Countryside & Gardens)
Description: Morville Hall is an Elizabethan house with a medieval core near Bridgnorth, Shropshire. The National Trust manages the three-acre walled gardens, featuring a cloister garden, kitchen garden, and an 18th-century landscape park. A Norman church is also on site.

Name: Moseley Bog & Joy's Wood (Tolkien's Inspiration)
Description: Moseley Bog and Joy's Wood Local Nature Reserve features ancient woodland and fen habitat, 3 miles south of Birmingham. This rare mire wetland, undisturbed since the Ice Age, is a Site of Special Scientific Interest (SSSI). It inspired Tolkien and offers a boardwalk for visitors.

Name: Moseley Old Hall (Knot Garden & Parkland)
Description: Moseley Old Hall is a 17th-century timber-framed farmhouse near Wolverhampton. It features a knot garden, orchard, and a priest hole used by Charles II. The site is managed by the National Trust.

Name: Moss Side Farm (Natural England - Peatland Pilot)
Description: Moss Side Farm is a significant peatland restoration project in Cumbria, part of Natural England's pilot scheme. It focuses on rewetting and restoring degraded peat bogs, enhancing biodiversity, and sequestering carbon. The site actively contributes to wider landscape-scale conservation efforts.

Name: Moss Valley Woodlands (Ancient Bluebell Woods)
Description: Moss Valley Woodlands, near Sheffield and Rotherham, comprise ancient woodlands known for their impressive seasonal bluebell displays. The area supports diverse wildlife, offering public access for walking and exploration.

Name: Mostney Moss (Cheshire Peatland)
Description: Mostney Moss is a nationally important lowland raised bog, a Site of Special Scientific Interest on the Cheshire/Shropshire border. It provides a vital haven for wildlife including dragonflies and sphagnum mosses. Belted Galloway cattle graze here as part of a peatland restoration project.

Name: Moston Fairway
Description: Moston Fairway is a former railway line in north Manchester transformed into a thriving wildflower meadow and woodland habitat. This urban nature reserve provides essential green space for wildlife and local residents. It supports diverse flora and fauna, including various bird species throughout the year.

Name: Mote Park
Description: Mote Park is a large country park in Maidstone, Kent, featuring diverse habitats including grassland, woodland, and a significant lake. It supports various wildlife and offers recreational opportunities for visitors.

Name: Mottisfont (Riverside & Woodland)
Description: Mottisfont is a historic house and gallery, a former priory located in the River Test valley. It features 20th-century art within the house, extensive gardens, ancient trees, and riverside walks. An adventure playground is also present.

Name: Mottistone Down
Description: Mottistone Down, part of the Mottistone Estate on the Isle of Wight, offers sweeping views across the island and out to sea. This downland supports a variety of wildlife, including red squirrels and numerous bird species. Visitors can explore ancient burial mounds and follow walking trails throughout the estate.

Name: Moulton Marsh (The Wash Saltmarsh)
Description: Moulton Marsh is an expansive saltmarsh on The Wash, forming part of the UK's largest intertidal area. It is internationally important for numerous wintering wildfowl and wading birds.

Name: Mount Grace Priory (Ancient Woodland Partner)
Description: Mount Grace Priory is a medieval Carthusian monastery site in North Yorkshire. It features the best-preserved Carthusian priory in England, a manor house, and gardens. The site is an Ancient Woodland Partner, supporting biodiversity and conservation efforts.

Name: Mount Stewart (Winterwatch 2026 Host)
Description: Mount Stewart, a National Trust property in County Down, Northern Ireland, features a neoclassical house, stunning gardens, and a lake. The estate displays a unique collection of plants, sculptures, and furniture. Visitors can explore walking trails, an adventure play area, and a rich history.

Name: Mountsorrel and Rothley Marshes
Description: Mountsorrel and Rothley Marshes is a Leicestershire flood meadow alongside the River Soar. The site features reedbed, scrub, and wet grassland habitats, supporting diverse birdlife, dragonflies, and damselflies. Public footpaths allow wildlife observation.

Name: Mountsorrel Meadows
Description: Mountsorrel Meadows is a nature reserve on the River Soar floodplain. It features wet grassland, reedbed, and species-rich meadows, providing habitat for diverse wildlife including otters and birds. Accessible paths are available.

Name: Mourne Mountains SAC (PeatPlus Target)
Description: The Mourne Mountains SAC is a special area in Northern Ireland, encompassing approximately 270 hectares of blanket bog. The PeatPLUS Project, funded by INTERREG VA, focuses on enhancing degraded peatlands through restoration work, re-wetting, and monitoring.

Name: Mousa (Shetland)
Description: Mousa is a small, uninhabited island south-east of Mainland Shetland, known for its Iron Age broch, a well-preserved drystone tower. The island is an RSPB nature reserve, a vital breeding ground for storm petrels, Arctic terns, and common terns. Grey seals often haul out on its shores.

Name: Muckton Wood
Description: Muckton Wood is an ancient woodland site covering 27.5 hectares near Louth, Lincolnshire. It features mature oak and ash trees with a hazel understory. The site is managed for biodiversity, supporting woodland birds and various insects. Public footpaths allow access.

Name: Mudchute Park and Farm (Biodiversity Hub)
Description: Mudchute Park and Farm is a 32-acre community charity on London's Isle of Dogs. It offers a unique countryside oasis in the city, housing British rare breed animals. The site provides educational, recreational, and therapeutic opportunities for the local community and visitors.

Name: Muir of Dinnet NNR (Ice Age Legacy)
Description: Muir of Dinnet NNR is an Aberdeenshire reserve, preserving Ice Age landscapes and diverse habitats. It features lochs, heath, and ancient woodlands, supporting rare birds, insects, and plants. Visitor facilities include waymarked trails and a car park.

Name: Mull of Galloway
Description: Mull of Galloway is Scotland's most southerly point, home to an RSPB nature reserve. Dramatic cliffs support thousands of nesting seabirds, including guillemots, razorbills, and kittiwakes during spring and summer. Visitors enjoy panoramic views across the Irish Sea and can spot marine wildlife.

Name: Mullion Island & Harbour
Description: Mullion Cove, on Cornwall's Lizard peninsula, is a historic harbour completed in 1895, sheltering a small fishing fleet. Mullion Island, a National Trust bird sanctuary, lies offshore. The site offers coastal walks and wildlife viewing.

Name: Mundford Glebe
Description: Mundford Glebe is a small Norfolk Wildlife Trust reserve with grassland, woodland, and a pond. It supports diverse wildlife, including amphibians, insects, and birds. The site features accessible paths for visitors and is actively managed for conservation.

Name: Murlough NNR (Ireland’s First Nature Reserve)
Description: Murlough National Nature Reserve, near Newcastle, County Down, protects a dune system and heathland. It is home to diverse wildlife, including a large population of Brent geese in winter. Visitors can explore marked trails through dunes and woodland, offering coastal views.

Name: Muxton Marsh
Description: Muxton Marsh is a small wet woodland and marshland nature reserve in Telford, Shropshire. The site features various mature trees including willow and alder, with an understorey of hawthorn and blackthorn. Open pools support diverse aquatic flora and fauna. Maintained by Shropshire Wildlife Trust.

Name: Mwnt (Coastal Headland)
Description: Mwnt is a dramatic coastal headland in Ceredigion, West Wales, owned by the National Trust. It features a beautiful sandy cove, a small medieval church, and offers excellent opportunities to spot Cardigan Bay bottlenose dolphins and grey seals.

Name: Mynydd Waun Fawr (Renewables & Nature Pilot)
Description: Mynydd Waun Fawr, near Llandovery, Carmarthenshire, is an 110-hectare site managed by the Wildlife Trust of South and West Wales. This pilot project focuses on peatland restoration and renewable energy. It aims to enhance biodiversity, explore sustainable energy solutions, and demonstrate nature-based approaches.

Name: Nagshead NNR - Forest of Dean
Description: Nagshead NNR in the Forest of Dean, Gloucestershire, is managed by Forestry England. It protects ancient woodlands, primarily oak and beech, supporting diverse birdlife including pied flycatchers and redstarts. Visitors can enjoy waymarked walking trails.

Name: Nansmellyn Marsh
Description: Nansmellyn Marsh is a small, freshwater wetland located near Hayle in West Cornwall. The site supports diverse wetland plants and invertebrates, including several species of dragonflies and damselflies. It provides a vital habitat for local wildlife.

Name: Naphill Common
Description: Naphill Common is an ancient woodland, home to veteran trees, diverse fungi, and a variety of bird species. The site features open clearings and ponds, supporting a rich biodiversity within the Chilterns Area of Outstanding Natural Beauty.

Name: Narborough Bog
Description: Narborough Bog is a Leicestershire and Rutland Wildlife Trust nature reserve. This wetland features fen, wet woodland, and reedbeds, supporting diverse plants, insects, and birds. Boardwalks provide visitor access for wildlife observation and nature appreciation.

Name: Narborough Railway Line (Butterfly Haven)
Description: Narborough Railway Line (Butterfly Haven) is a former railway cutting in Norfolk. It supports chalk grassland, attracting numerous butterfly species including dingy skipper and brown argus. Wildflowers like bee orchid thrive here. It offers a linear habitat for diverse wildlife.

Name: Nare Head (The Roseland)
Description: Nare Head is a prominent headland on the wild south coast of the Roseland Peninsula, Cornwall. It offers dramatic coastal walks with far-reaching views across Gerrans Bay and the English Channel. The area is known for its diverse wildlife.

Name: Nashenden Down (Chalk Grassland Restoration)
Description: Nashenden Down is a 130-acre chalk grassland site near Rochester, Kent, managed by Kent Wildlife Trust. It undergoes restoration to enhance biodiversity, providing habitat for wildflowers and invertebrates. The reserve aims to improve landscape connectivity and is publicly accessible for visitors.

Name: National Trust Formby (Victoria Road Restoration)
Description: Formby features vast beaches, sand dunes, and pinewoods along the Sefton coast, home to red squirrels and natterjack toads. Prehistoric footprints are visible at low tide. The National Trust manages the landscape, focusing on wildlife and habitat conservation, including dune restoration near Victoria Road.

Name: Nature Discovery Centre (Thatcham)
Description: The Nature Discovery Centre in Thatcham, Berkshire, offers diverse habitats including woodland, meadows, and lakes. It provides an educational experience for visitors, focusing on local wildlife and conservation efforts. Accessible trails and bird hides are available for public use.

Name: Necton Wood
Description: Necton Wood is an ancient woodland in Norfolk, managed by the Norfolk Wildlife Trust. It features coppice stools reflecting historical management. Spring brings carpets of wood anemones and bluebells. The wood supports diverse fungi in autumn and birds like great spotted woodpeckers and nuthatches.

Name: Needles Old Battery and New Battery (Headland)
Description: The Needles Old Battery, a Victorian fort from 1862, protected the western Isle of Wight approaches. It provides views of the Needles rocks and lighthouse. The nearby New Battery hosted secret rocket testing during World War Two.

Name: Nene Wetlands (Rushden Lakes interface)
Description: Nene Wetlands in Northamptonshire is a nature reserve with lakes, reedbeds, and woodlands. Adjacent to Rushden Lakes, it offers accessible trails, bird hides, and a visitor centre. The site supports diverse wildlife, including otters and kingfishers.

Name: Neroche Forest (Blackdown Hills)
Description: Neroche Forest is a network of woodlands and open spaces within the Blackdown Hills AONB in Somerset. It offers routes for walking, cycling, and horse riding in a tranquil setting, featuring an ancient hillfort. Forestry England manages the site.

Name: Netherton Braes
Description: Netherton Braes is an urban reserve on Glasgow's outskirts. It provides a vital green space for local wildlife and people. Woodland, scrub, and grassland habitats support birds, insects, and small mammals. The site offers views across the city and Clyde Valley.

Name: New Buckenham Common
Description: New Buckenham Common in Norfolk features acid grassland, marsh, scrub, and woodland. The site is actively grazed by cattle and ponies, which helps maintain its diverse habitats and supports a variety of wildflowers, insects, and bird species.

Name: New Cross Gate Cutting
Description: New Cross Gate Cutting is a Site of Importance for Nature Conservation in London. This steep-sided railway cutting hosts diverse habitats including woodland, scrub, grassland, and wetland. It is noted for ferns, mosses, invertebrates, and birds, acting as an urban wildlife refuge.

Name: New Decoy Farm (Information Point)
Description: New Decoy Farm operates as an information point within the Great Fen, offering visitors a starting location to explore the expansive nature reserve. It provides access to walking routes and details on vital conservation work. Car park facilities are available.

Name: New Forest Commons (Mosaic of Heather & Gorse)
Description: The New Forest Commons, managed by the National Trust, comprise extensive areas of heathland, grassland, and ancient woodlands. This landscape supports diverse wildlife, including native ponies and cattle that graze freely. It is a designated Site of Special Scientific Interest and Special Area of Conservation, vital for its ecological value.

Name: New Forest National Park (Forestry England)
Description: The New Forest National Park is a historic landscape in Hampshire, featuring extensive woodlands, heathlands, and coastline. Managed by Forestry England, it hosts diverse wildlife, including wild ponies, and offers visitor recreation.

Name: New Forest Northern Commons
Description: The New Forest Northern Commons feature expansive heathland and ancient woodland, crucial for biodiversity. Home to iconic New Forest ponies and diverse wildlife, the commons offer extensive public access for recreation and are managed by the National Trust.

Name: New Hill & Tannager
Description: New Hill & Tannager is a 10-hectare nature reserve near Shepton Mallet, Somerset. It comprises wet grassland, ancient woodland, and hedgerows, creating diverse habitats. The site supports a range of flora and fauna, including various bird species and rare butterflies. It is managed for biodiversity.

Name: New Romney Warren
Description: New Romney Warren is a Kent Wildlife Trust nature reserve on the Kent coast. It preserves a rare sand dune and acidic grassland habitat, home to diverse invertebrates, reptiles, and wildflowers. The site is part of the Dungeness Special Area of Conservation.

Name: Newark Park (Cotswold Edge)
Description: Newark Park, a Tudor country house in Gloucestershire, sits on a promontory overlooking the Ozleworth valley. It features historic interiors, diverse gardens, and offers woodland and parkland walks with views across the Cotswolds.

Name: Newbiggin Pastures
Description: Newbiggin Pastures is a small, species-rich limestone grassland on a south-facing slope with views across Nidderdale. It is home to nationally declining butterflies like northern brown argus and dark-green fritillary. Wildflowers include orchids and rock-rose. It is part of the Upper Nidderdale NNR.

Name: Newbourne Springs
Description: Newbourne Springs is a Suffolk Wildlife Trust reserve near Woodbridge, featuring ancient woodland, wet grassland, and diverse habitats. It includes a spring-fed stream, ponds, and areas for wildlife observation, supporting a range of species.

Name: Newbourne Springs (River Deben tributary)
Description: Newbourne Springs is a valley fen site in Suffolk, fed by a River Deben tributary. It features spring-fed woodland, meadows, and reedbeds. Habitats support otters, water voles, kingfishers, and diverse birds, including Cetti's warblers, alongside dragonflies and rare plants.

Name: Newchurch Moors
Description: Newchurch Moors Nature Reserve is a key wetland site in the Newchurch valley on the Isle of Wight, managed by Hampshire and Isle of Wight Wildlife Trust. It comprises wet meadows, reedbeds, and woodland, supporting diverse wildlife including water voles and various bird species. Public access is available via a footpath. The reserve plays a vital role in local biodiversity conservation.

Name: Newchurch Moors Nature Reserve (IOW)
Description: Newchurch Moors Nature Reserve on the Isle of Wight features a mosaic of habitats, including wet woodland, reedbed, and species-rich meadows. It is a vital area for water voles, dragonflies, and wetland birds. The River Yar flows through the reserve, offering walking trails.

Name: Newhall Mere
Description: Newhall Mere is a small coastal valley wetland in Devon, restored from an arable field in 2011. The site features reedbeds, ponds, and wet grassland, attracting diverse wildlife. It is home to otters, water voles, and various bird species.

Name: Newlands Corner
Description: Newlands Corner is a famous beauty spot in Surrey, offering panoramic views across the Weald. This site features significant chalk grassland and ancient woodlands, designated as a Site of Special Scientific Interest. It provides diverse habitats for wildlife.

Name: Newtimber Hill
Description: Newtimber Hill is a chalk downland near Poynings, West Sussex, featuring grassland, woodland, and scrub habitats. The site offers views across the surrounding landscape and provides walking paths. Managed for conservation, it supports diverse local wildlife.

Name: Newton Coppice
Description: Newton Coppice is an ancient Herefordshire woodland near Leominster, dominated by oak and hazel. It supports woodland birds and wildflowers like early purple orchid. The site is actively managed, including coppicing, to enhance its biodiversity.

Name: Newton Farm
Description: Newton Farm is a 27-hectare nature reserve near Bath. Managed for wildlife since 2011, it features ancient woodlands, wildflower meadows, and a stream valley. Diverse habitats support bats, butterflies, and farmland birds. Public footpaths provide access.

Name: Newton Park West (Roxbourne)
Description: Roxbourne Park in Harrow features diverse habitats, including Yeading Brook, woodland, and open grassland. Thames21 partners with local groups to enhance the park's biodiversity and improve river health within the London Borough of Harrow.

Name: Newtown National Nature Reserve
Description: Newtown National Nature Reserve is the only National Nature Reserve on the Isle of Wight. This site offers diverse habitats including woodlands, meadows, and mudflats, supporting numerous bird species like Brent geese and avocets.
