Visitors can explore ancient woodland and observe migratory birds.

Name: Nichols Moss (Peatland Recovery)
Description: Nichols Moss is a rare raised mire in Cumbria, with deep peat supporting bog mosses, cotton grasses, heather, a rare sphagnum, and large heath butterflies. Restoration re-wets the site to regenerate vegetation and enhance carbon storage for wetland wildlife.

Name: Nidd Gorge (Woodland Trust / Forestry interface)
Description: Nidd Gorge is an ancient woodland near Knaresborough, North Yorkshire, forming part of a larger Site of Importance for Nature Conservation. Its steep sides and river support diverse wildlife. The site is a Local Nature Reserve.

Name: Ningwood Common
Description: Ningwood Common is a nature reserve on the Isle of Wight. It features wet woodland, wet grassland, and heathland, supporting various dragonflies, damselflies, butterflies, and rare plants like marsh violet. It is managed to conserve its diverse habitats.

Name: Ningwood Common (Isle of Wight Heath)
Description: Ningwood Common is a small, species-rich heathland on the Isle of Wight. This Site of Special Scientific Interest features acid grassland and gorse scrub, supporting rare birds like nightjar and Dartford warbler. The site also hosts a variety of invertebrates and reptiles. Historically, it was a common for grazing.

Name: Nipstone Rock (Stiperstones Outlier)
Description: Nipstone Rock is a mosaic of habitats, including dry heathland, acid grassland, and woodland. It is notable for its tors, geology, and wildlife, including rare bryophytes and lichens. The site offers panoramic views across the Shropshire Hills.

Name: Noar Hill
Description: Noar Hill in Hampshire is a chalk grassland, scrub, and woodland nature reserve, a Site of Special Scientific Interest. It is famed for its diverse orchids and butterflies thriving on ancient chalk workings, supporting a rich array of wildlife.

Name: Noar Hill (Orchid-rich Chalk Pit)
Description: Noar Hill is an ancient chalk pit supporting over ten orchid species and a rich butterfly fauna, including the rare Duke of Burgundy. Managed through conservation grazing, this site preserves vital chalk grassland habitat, crucial for diverse insect life.

Name: Noddle Hill
Description: Noddle Hill Nature Reserve is an urban wetland on the eastern edge of Hull, transformed from arable land. It features ponds, ditches, and reedbeds, providing habitats for waterfowl, warblers, and insects. This quiet oasis supports local wildlife and offers community recreation.

Name: Nomadic Community Garden
Description: Nomadic Community Garden is a green space in East London, established on former railway sidings. It operates as a public park and community-run garden. The garden features diverse flora, unique structures, and is home to local wildlife. It encourages biodiversity and sustainable urban living.

Name: Norah Hanbury-Kelk Nature Reserve
Description: Norah Hanbury-Kelk Nature Reserve, near the River Waveney in Suffolk, comprises grassland and fen habitats. It protects diverse flora, including rare fen orchid, marsh marigold, and water mint. The site supports various insects and bird species like water rail.

Name: Norbury Park
Description: Norbury Park is a mixed-use estate near Leatherhead in Surrey. It features ancient woodland, grasslands, and the River Mole, supporting diverse wildlife. The park offers walking trails and views of the Surrey Hills AONB. Managed for conservation and public access.

Name: Norbury Park (Surrey Hills Core)
Description: Norbury Park is a significant nature reserve managed by Surrey Wildlife Trust, featuring diverse habitats including mixed woodland, grasslands, and chalk downs. It supports varied wildlife and offers an extensive network of footpaths for public access.

Name: Nore Hill Pinnacle (Geological Site)
Description: Nore Hill Pinnacle is a small geological site featuring a prominent pinnacle of hard chalk in Surrey. This Site of Special Scientific Interest (SSSI) displays a rare geological unconformity between Upper and Middle Chalk strata, important for study.

Name: North Cliffe Wood
Description: North Cliffe Wood is an ancient woodland in East Yorkshire, home to various wildflowers including bluebells and wood anemone. The site supports a range of bird species and ponds provide habitat for dragonflies. A boardwalk and surfaced path ensure accessibility. Yorkshire Wildlife Trust manages the reserve.

Name: North Downs Way
Description: The North Downs Way is a 153-mile path from Farnham, Surrey, to Dover, Kent. It follows the North Downs escarpment, traversing chalk grasslands and ancient woodlands. The trail offers diverse habitats for wildlife and panoramic views.

Name: North Hill (Papa Westray)
Description: North Hill, on Papa Westray, is a remote RSPB reserve renowned for its seabird colonies, including puffins, guillemots, and Arctic terns during breeding season. Rich in wildflowers and home to grey seals and otters, it offers dramatic clifftop walks with expansive views.

Name: North Muskham Lake
Description: North Muskham Lake is an SSSI gravel pit next to the River Trent, managed by Nottinghamshire Wildlife Trust. It features diverse habitats including open water, reedbeds, scrub, and grassland. The site supports a wide array of bird species, rare stoneworts, dragonflies, and otters.

Name: North Norfolk Woodlands (Assorted blocks)
Description: North Norfolk Woodlands are a collection of diverse sites owned by Forestry England, including Sheringham Wood and Holt Lowes. These woodlands provide recreational opportunities for visitors to enjoy nature and explore trails within North Norfolk.

Name: North Wingfield (Wetland & Harvest Mice)
Description: North Wingfield, a Derbyshire Wildlife Trust reserve, transforms former farmland into a biodiverse wetland. Ponds, scrapes, and ditches support great crested newts and dragonflies. Conservation efforts focus on establishing harvest mouse populations and developing species-rich grasslands.

Name: Northey Island
Description: Northey Island in the Blackwater Estuary is a significant wildlife haven and the UK's first causewayed island. Accessible at low tide, it showcases managed retreat with its unique saltmarsh and mudflat habitats, supporting diverse bird species.

Name: Northeye (Pevensey Levels)
Description: Pevensey Levels, including Northeye, is a significant grazing marsh in East Sussex. It offers a vital habitat for rare invertebrates, notably the endangered Fen Raft Spider, and is designated a Site of Special Scientific Interest (SSSI).

Name: Northumberlandia (Landform Park)
Description: Northumberlandia is a 19-acre public park featuring a human landform sculpture. Created from 1.5 million tonnes of subsoil, the 34-metre-high figure, sculpted as a reclining female, sits at the park's heart.

Name: Northwold Chalk Pit
Description: Northwold Chalk Pit is a former chalk quarry in West Norfolk. The site features chalk grassland, woodland, and a disused chalk pit. It supports diverse flora including orchids and wildflowers, along with various insect species. It serves as a valuable habitat for wildlife.

Name: Northwood Community Woodland (Kirkby)
Description: Northwood Community Woodland in Kirkby, Merseyside, offers a significant green space for local residents. The site features various paths, trees, and open areas, suitable for walking and recreation. Managed with active community involvement, it focuses on enhancing local biodiversity and providing public enjoyment.

Name: Norwood Road (March)
Description: Norwood Road in March, Cambridgeshire, is a former Fenland potato field now being restored to a traditional hay meadow. The site features scrapes for wetland birds, a wildflower meadow, and a young orchard. It provides habitat for diverse wildlife.

Name: Noss Mayo & Gara Point
Description: Noss Mayo and Gara Point offer picturesque coastal and estuary walks along the River Yealm in South Devon. Visitors can explore ancient woodland, cliffs, and coves, enjoying varied wildlife and stunning sea views. The area includes the villages of Noss Mayo and Newton Ferrers.

Name: Nostell - Obelisk Park (Ecological Restoration)
Description: Nostell Obelisk Park, part of the wider Nostell estate in West Yorkshire, is a landscape undergoing ecological restoration. The parkland and woodlands feature mature trees and pathways, with conservation efforts focused on enhancing biodiversity within the historic setting.

Name: Nostell (Lakes & Parkland)
Description: Nostell, a National Trust property near Wakefield, West Yorkshire, features an 18th-century Palladian house, landscaped parkland, and gardens. The estate includes lakes, woodlands, and walking trails, supporting diverse wildlife.

Name: Nower Wood
Description: Nower Wood, owned by Surrey Wildlife Trust, is a mixed woodland nature reserve in Surrey. It features ancient woodlands, unimproved grasslands, and ponds, supporting diverse flora and fauna. The site is a key educational hub, hosting school groups and public events focused on nature conservation.

Name: Nunnery Mead
Description: Nunnery Mead is a tranquil wet woodland and grassland reserve near Gillingham. It supports diverse wildlife, including otters, kingfishers, and various insect species. The River Stour flows through, maintaining wetland habitats and enhancing biodiversity.

Name: Nunnery Wood
Description: Nunnery Wood is a semi-natural ancient woodland near Brimpsfield on the Cotswold scarp. The site, within the Cotswolds AONB, hosts ancient woodland indicator species. The Trust removes non-native trees and restores traditional coppicing.

Name: Nunnington Hall (River Rye Project)
Description: Nunnington Hall is a picturesque 17th-century manor house nestled beside the River Rye. It features organic gardens, including a walled garden, and a tea-room. The site hosts art exhibitions and offers various family-friendly activities.

Name: Nutfield Marshes
Description: Nutfield Marshes is a key wetland reserve in Surrey, featuring open water, extensive reedbeds, damp grassland, and willow scrub. This former aggregate quarry now supports diverse bird species and provides important habitats for wildlife.

Name: Oak Hill Wood
Description: Oak Hill Wood is an ancient woodland in East Barnet, North London, featuring mature oak, hornbeam, and ash trees. The woodland floor supports a rich understorey of bluebells and wild garlic. It provides a vital habitat for various bird species and small mammals within an urban setting.

Name: Oak Tree Farm (Lugg Valley Restoration)
Description: Oak Tree Farm is a 30-hectare Herefordshire Wildlife Trust site near Bodenham. It forms a key part of the Lugg Valley restoration project, creating wetlands and improving water quality. The farm supports biodiversity with wet grassland, traditional orchards, and new woodlands.

Name: Oak Tree Heath (Mansfield Heathland)
Description: Oak Tree Heath is a Local Nature Reserve near Mansfield, Nottinghamshire. This scarce lowland heath and acid grassland habitat supports heather, gorse, insects, reptiles, and birds. Managed through grazing by cattle and sheep to preserve its unique biodiversity.

Name: Oakfield Wood
Description: Oakfield Wood is a mixed woodland near Chinnor, covering 10.5 hectares. It features ancient woodland characteristics with diverse trees like oak and hazel, along with wildflowers such as bluebells and wild garlic. Paths wind through the wood, providing visitor access.

Name: Oakhill (Goole)
Description: Oakhill Nature Reserve near Goole is a wetland site managed by Yorkshire Wildlife Trust. It features an open water scrape, reedbeds, and wet grassland, supporting diverse birdlife like sedge warblers and kingfishers, plus amphibians. A surfaced path and hide offer visitor access.

Name: Oakley Wood (Ancient Woodland Restoration)
Description: Oakley Wood, near Warwick, is a 54-hectare ancient woodland site managed by Warwickshire Wildlife Trust. It features mature oak, ash, and hazel trees and is undergoing restoration. The site provides habitat for diverse wildlife and is open for public access.

Name: Oakwood & Blacklow Spinney
Description: Oakwood and Blacklow Spinney are ancient woodlands, dominated by mature oak, ash, hazel and elm. Spring brings a carpet of bluebells and wood anemones. The reserve supports breeding birds and badgers. Maintained for its biodiversity.

Name: Oare Marshes Nature Reserve (Faversham)
Description: Oare Marshes Nature Reserve is a key wetland site near Faversham, Kent, noted for its diverse birdlife and coastal habitats. It comprises extensive reedbeds, grazing marshes, and saline lagoons, supporting migrating and breeding waterfowl and waders throughout the year. The reserve offers hides and viewing screens.

Name: Ockbrook Wood
Description: Ockbrook Wood is a 12-hectare nature reserve in Derbyshire, preserving ancient woodland features. The site supports a diverse ground flora, including dog's mercury and yellow archangel, under oak, ash, hazel, and field maple canopies. Steep slopes and a stream create varied habitats for tawny owls, jays, and bats.

Name: Ockham Common & Bolder Mere
Description: Ockham Common & Bolder Mere forms part of a large heathland area in Surrey, featuring open heath, woodland, and a picturesque lake. The site supports various bird species, reptiles, and invertebrates.

Name: Old Broom (Ancient Pollarded Oaks)
Description: Old Broom (Ancient Pollarded Oaks) is a small Suffolk Wildlife Trust nature reserve. It features ancient pollarded oaks in a woodland setting. The site supports a diverse range of fungi and invertebrates, with mature trees providing important habitat. Public access is limited to a small area.

Name: Old Burghclere Lime Quarry
Description: Old Burghclere Lime Quarry is a Hampshire and Isle of Wight Wildlife Trust nature reserve. This former quarry protects valuable chalk grassland, supporting diverse wildflowers, butterflies, and orchids. The site offers refuge for various insect species. Public access is available for nature observation and walking.

Name: Old Hall Marshes (RSPB partnership)
Description: Old Hall Marshes forms part of the Blackwater and Dengie National Nature Reserve partnership. Managed by Essex Wildlife Trust, its varied habitats include saltmarsh, lagoons, and freshwater marsh, supporting significant bird populations year-round.

Name: Old Lodge (Ashdown Forest Heath)
Description: Old Lodge is a former deer hunting lodge on Ashdown Forest, Sussex. This heathland, woodland, and pond site is managed for rare bird species like Dartford warblers and nightjars. Public access allows walking and birdwatching.

Name: Old Moor (Partner Hub)
Description: Old Moor Nature Reserve, near Barnsley, South Yorkshire, is a wetland habitat managed for diverse wildlife. It features lagoons, reedbeds, and grasslands, providing refuge for birds. Visitor facilities include hides and a visitor centre.

Name: Old Nun Wood
Description: Old Nun Wood is an ancient woodland in Warwickshire. It features mature oak, ash, and hazel trees, with a rich understory of wildflowers including bluebells. The reserve provides crucial habitat for various bird species and small mammals.

Name: Old Park Wood (Harefield)
Description: Old Park Wood is an ancient woodland spanning 33 hectares near Harefield, Hertfordshire. It features mature oaks, hornbeams, and a rich ground flora, providing a vital habitat for various bird species. The site is managed by Herts and Middlesex Wildlife Trust.

Name: Old Sludge Beds
Description: Old Sludge Beds, a Devon Wildlife Trust nature reserve in Tiverton, features reedbeds, scrub, and open water. It attracts diverse bird species year-round, with dragonflies and damselflies common in summer. Accessible paths allow for wildlife observation.

Name: Old Sludge Beds (Reedbed Ark)
Description: Old Sludge Beds, known as Reedbed Ark, is a valuable wetland habitat near Exeter. It features reedbeds, ditches, and ponds, supporting diverse wildlife including otters, water voles, and rare insects. The reserve is a stronghold for protected species.

Name: Old Sulehay (Rockingham Forest Limestone Mosaic)
Description: Old Sulehay is a Northamptonshire nature reserve, part of Rockingham Forest Limestone Mosaic. It features ancient woodland, species-rich grassland, and disused limestone quarries. Habitats support diverse flora and fauna, including rare orchids. Walking trails are present.

Name: Old Warden Tunnel
Description: Old Warden Tunnel is a disused Victorian railway tunnel, now a vital bat roost designated as a SSSI. It provides a crucial habitat for Natterer's, brown long-eared, and Daubenton's bats. Surrounding woodlands and grasslands enhance its ecological value, managed by Wildlife BCN.

Name: Old Wood of Drum (Ancient Royal Forest)
Description: The Old Wood of Drum, near Drumoak, is a significant remnant of an ancient Royal Forest, featuring oak trees over 500 years old. It supports diverse wildlife and is managed for conservation by the National Trust for Scotland.

Name: Oldbury Hill (Iron Age Hillfort & Woods)
Description: Oldbury Hill, an Iron Age hillfort and ancient woodland near Ightham, is a Site of Special Scientific Interest. It boasts mature trees and diverse flora, including bluebells and wood anemones. The reserve provides habitats for various bird species and offers marked walking trails.

Name: Oldbury-on-Severn Wood
Description: Oldbury-on-Severn Wood is a small, developing woodland. Planted in 2000, it features mixed broadleaves and conifer species. The site offers views of the River Severn estuary and is accessible via public footpaths.

Name: Oldhall Ponds
Description: Oldhall Ponds, a reserve in a former quarry, protects shallow ponds, grassland, and scrub. It serves as a habitat for amphibians, including great crested newts, along with dragonflies, damselflies, and migrant bird species.

Name: Oliver Land
Description: Oliver Land is a 33.5-acre nature reserve near Wadebridge, Cornwall. It features flower-rich pastures, Cornish hedges, and ancient woodland. A River Camel tributary supports barn owls, otters, and kingfishers. Waymarked trails are open for visitors.

Name: Ollerton Pit Wood (Wetland & Strawberry Walk)
Description: Ollerton Pit Wood, a former colliery site, is now a diverse woodland and wetland in Nottinghamshire. Managed by Forestry England, it features accessible walking trails, including the Strawberry Walk, and supports a range of wildlife, providing a quiet green space.

Name: One Planet Bruton (Nature Recovery Network)
Description: One Planet Bruton leads nature recovery efforts for Bruton, Somerset, focusing on biodiversity and habitat restoration across the parish. Initiatives include creating wildlife corridors and enhancing green spaces. It encourages community participation in environmental projects.

Name: One Tree Hill (Sevenoaks)
Description: One Tree Hill offers panoramic views across the Weald of Kent. Visitors can explore ancient woodlands, rare acid grassland, and heathland. The site is managed by the National Trust, providing opportunities for walking and enjoying nature. Notable features include mature trees and diverse wildlife habitats.

Name: Onziebust (Egilsay)
Description: Onziebust is a nature reserve on Egilsay, Orkney, managed by the RSPB. It features wetland habitats, including wet grassland and reedbeds, supporting breeding waders like curlews, lapwings, and snipe. Raptors such as hen harriers hunt here. The reserve is also home to wildflowers.

Name: Orford Ness National Nature Reserve
Description: Orford Ness is a remote, shingle spit on the Suffolk coast, managed by the National Trust. It holds a unique history as a secret military testing site and is now a significant nature reserve for wildlife, including rare birds and plants.

Name: Orlestone Forest
Description: Orlestone Forest is a mosaic of habitats in southeast Kent near Hamstreet. It features ancient woodland, coppiced hazel, rides, and open glades. The site supports various bird species, invertebrates, and wildflowers. It is managed for biodiversity and public access.

Name: Orlestone Forest (Ancient Kentish Wood)
Description: Orlestone Forest is an ancient woodland in Kent, managed by Forestry England. It features mixed broadleaf and conifer trees, offering walking trails. The site focuses on timber production and biodiversity, supporting various wildlife species like dormice.

Name: Orlock Point (Coastal Grazing Restoration)
Description: Orlock Point, near Groomsport, is part of the North Down Coastal Path. A coastal grazing restoration project is underway here, benefiting pollinators and coastal flora. The site offers stunning views.

Name: Orlock Point (Coastal Grazing)
Description: Orlock Point is a coastal area in Northern Ireland, near Donaghadee, known for its important wildlife habitats. The site features coastal grazing pastures, supporting diverse flora and fauna, and offers views across Belfast Lough. It is managed for conservation.

Name: Ormesby Beck
Description: Ormesby Beck Nature Reserve in Middlesbrough is a linear wetland habitat along the beck. It supports species like water vole, kingfisher, and various dragonflies. The reserve features reedbeds, ponds, and wet grassland, providing a vital green corridor.

Name: Ormesby Hall (Urban Green Space & Parkland)
Description: Ormesby Hall is a Georgian manor house, gardens, and parkland near Middlesbrough, managed by the National Trust. The estate includes a walled garden, woodland walks, and a popular model railway layout. It hosts various community events and seasonal activities throughout the year.

Name: Ormesby Hall (Urban Parkland Oasis)
Description: Ormesby Hall is a National Trust Palladian mansion near Middlesbrough, showcasing fine furniture and ceramics. Landscaped gardens, woodland, and a children's play area create an urban green space. The site preserves the Bouskell family's heritage.

Name: Orton Moss
Description: Orton Moss is a peat bog formed in a glacial basin after the last ice age. Peat has been cut here for centuries. Cumbria Wildlife Trust is restoring the peatland habitat, supporting diverse plant and animal species like sphagnum mosses, cotton grass, dragonflies, and various birds.

Name: Osmington White Horse & Cliffs
Description: The Osmington White Horse is a large hill figure, carved into the limestone above Osmington village in Dorset. It depicts King George III on horseback. Nearby cliffs offer views of Weymouth Bay. The site is accessible for walkers, part of the Jurassic Coast World Heritage Site.

Name: Osterley Park (Biodiversity Recovery)
Description: Osterley Park, a large Georgian estate in west London, focuses on biodiversity recovery. Its varied habitats, including ancient trees, meadows, and lakes, support diverse wildlife. Conservation efforts protect and enhance these environments for future generations.

Name: Oughtonhead Common
Description: Oughtonhead Common is a nature reserve near Hitchin, featuring wet woodland, fen, and grassland habitats fed by the River Oughton. The River Oughton originates from Oughtonhead Spring, a SSSI. Wildlife includes kingfishers, water voles, otters, and Daubenton's bats.

Name: Oulton Marshes
Description: Oulton Marshes is a tranquil nature reserve within the Broads National Park. This landscape features grazing marsh, reedbed, and wet woodland. It supports nationally important bird species like marsh harrier, bittern, and bearded tit, alongside rare plants and insects.

Name: Oulton Marshes (Fen & Marsh)
Description: Oulton Marshes is a wetland nature reserve near Lowestoft, within the Broads National Park. It comprises fen, marsh, wet woodland, and reedbeds, providing habitat for water voles, otters, and diverse bird species. Public footpaths allow visitor access.

Name: Ouse Estuary Nature Reserve (Newhaven)
Description: The Ouse Estuary Nature Reserve, near Newhaven, is managed by Sussex Wildlife Trust. This vital wildlife corridor along the River Ouse is not open to the public. The site is managed primarily for wildlife benefit, forming part of a wider conservation effort in the area.

Name: Overbeck's Coastal Garden & Cliffs
Description: Overbeck's is an exotic subtropical garden near Salcombe, Devon, housing rare plants that thrive in its microclimate. The site offers stunning coastal views and a museum showcasing the original owner's eclectic collections.

Name: Overdale
Description: Overdale Nature Reserve, near Buxton, is a dramatic limestone dale with species-rich grasslands. It supports wildflowers, butterflies, and birds, with steep slopes hosting orchids. Managed by Derbyshire Wildlife Trust, it offers public footpaths through unique geology.

Name: Overhall Grove
Description: Overhall Grove is an ancient woodland in Cambridgeshire, managed for diverse trees, wildflowers, and fungi. It provides important habitat for bird species, invertebrates, and mammals. Public access allows for quiet enjoyment, with conservation work maintaining biodiversity.

Name: Overton Carr
Description: Overton Carr is a 1.25-hectare nature reserve near Ashover, Derbyshire, featuring wet woodland and species-rich grassland. Managed for wildlife, it supports diverse flora and fauna, including rare sedges. Public access is available via a footpath.

Name: Owl Wood and Pit Plantation
Description: Owl Wood and Pit Plantation is a Yorkshire Wildlife Trust nature reserve, featuring ancient oak, ash, and hazel woodland alongside a newer plantation. The site supports diverse bird species and ground flora, with a network of paths for public access.

Name: Oxburgh Estate (Parkland Restoration & Wood Pasture)
Description: Oxburgh Estate features a moated manor house, gardens, and parkland in Norfolk. Home to the Bedingfeld family for 500 years, it showcases historic interiors, rare needlework, and a priest hole. Visitors explore extensive grounds with river walks, supporting ongoing heritage and landscape conservation.

Name: Oxey Mead (Unimproved Hay Meadow)
Description: Oxey Mead is an unimproved hay meadow near Oxford, adjacent to the River Thames. It supports diverse grasses and wildflowers, including large numbers of snakeshead fritillaries. Wading birds are present in winter, and cattle graze the area. This site is managed by BBOWT.

Name: Oxford Island NNR (Lough Neagh)
Description: Oxford Island NNR is a National Nature Reserve on Lough Neagh's southern shore. It features diverse habitats including woodlands, reedbeds, and meadows, supporting significant bird populations. Managed by DAERA, the site offers visitor facilities and walking trails.

Name: Oxhouse Farm (Wildlife Farming)
Description: Oxhouse Farm, a 95-acre site run by Warwickshire Wildlife Trust, demonstrates wildlife-friendly farming. It integrates conservation with sustainable agriculture, creating diverse habitats including meadows, hedgerows, and wetlands for various species.

Name: Oxshott Heath & Woods
Description: Oxshott Heath and Woods is a mosaic of woodland and heathland in Surrey, featuring ancient oak and Scots pine trees. The site supports diverse wildlife including birds, invertebrates, and reptiles, with ponds and streams adding ecological value.

Name: Oxwich NNR (Gower Mosaic)
Description: Oxwich National Nature Reserve, located within the Gower AONB, is a mosaic of diverse habitats. It features extensive sand dunes, saltmarsh, woodland, and a freshwater lake. The reserve supports a rich array of wildlife, including rare insects and plants. Public footpaths offer access for visitors.

Name: Oyster Creek (Suffolk)
Description: Oyster Creek forms part of the Orford Ness National Nature Reserve in Suffolk, a vast shingle spit managed by the National Trust. This remote coastal landscape features brackish lagoons, creeks, and saltmarsh habitats, supporting diverse wildlife and significant bird populations.

Name: Oysters Coppice (Vale of Wardour)
Description: Oysters Coppice is an ancient woodland located on the edge of the Vale of Wardour in Wiltshire. It features diverse tree species, including oak, ash, and hazel, with a rich ground flora of bluebells and primroses. It supports varied wildlife.

Name: Packwood House (Park & Garden)
Description: Packwood House is a restored timber-framed Tudor manor house in Warwickshire, set within extensive gardens, parkland, and a historic yew garden. It features period rooms, a collection of textiles, and rural walks. The estate reflects centuries of development.

Name: Padworth Common
Description: Padworth Common is a rare lowland heath in West Berkshire. It features diverse habitats including heathland, woodland, and acid grassland, supporting specific birds, reptiles, and invertebrates. The site is managed for nature conservation, offering accessible walking trails.

Name: Pagham Harbour
Description: Pagham Harbour, a West Sussex Site of Special Scientific Interest, is a protected wildlife haven. It features extensive mudflats, saltmarshes, and shingle beaches, supporting diverse bird species. Hides and walking trails are available.

Name: Paices Wood Country Parkland
Description: Paices Wood Country Parkland, a former sand and gravel quarry, is now a nature reserve managed by BBOWT. It features diverse habitats including woodlands, grasslands, ponds, and streams, supporting a variety of wildlife. The site offers walking trails.

Name: Paignton & Torquay Coastal Path
Description: Berry Head is a limestone headland and National Nature Reserve on the South West Coast Path, offering extensive views across Torbay. This Site of Special Scientific Interest features Napoleonic War fortifications and supports significant seabird colonies, including guillemots and kittiwakes.

Name: Pam's Pools (Restored Wetland)
Description: Pam's Pools is a restored wetland reserve in Shropshire, transformed from a disused gravel pit. It now features open water, extensive reedbeds, and grassland, supporting a diverse array of wetland birds, insects, and amphibians. A valuable habitat for wildlife.

Name: Pamber Forest and Silchester Common (NNR)
Description: Pamber Forest is an ancient woodland and SSSI in Hampshire, co-managed by Hampshire & Isle of Wight Wildlife Trust. Adjoining Silchester Common, it features diverse woodlands and heathland. The site supports various fungi, plants, and insects, including dormice, woodcock, and rare beetles.

Name: Pamber Forest and Upper Inhams Copse
Description: Pamber Forest and Upper Inhams Copse is an ancient 108.8-hectare woodland in Hampshire, managed by HIWWT. It features oak, birch, hornbeam, and hazel coppice. Rare butterflies like white admiral and purple emperor thrive here, alongside various birds, streams, and ponds.

Name: Panshanger Park (Partner)
Description: Panshanger Park, near Hertford, is a historic landscape and country park. It holds SSSI status for its geological features and diverse habitats including ancient woodlands and wetlands. The park supports varied wildlife and offers public access for recreation and nature observation.

Name: Panty-Sais (Fen & Reedbed)
Description: Panty-Sais is a valuable wetland site in north Pembrokeshire, featuring fen and reedbed habitats. It supports diverse wildlife, including otters, water voles, and various bird species. The reserve contributes to local biodiversity conservation.

Name: Papercourt Marshes
Description: Papercourt Marshes in the Wey Valley features wet grassland, scrub, and woodland by the River Wey. This SSSI and SAC site supports water voles, otters, kingfishers, and diverse dragonflies. Key for wintering wildfowl, waders, and breeding lapwing and snipe.

Name: Papercourt Marshes & Meadows
Description: Papercourt Marshes & Meadows is a wetland habitat by the River Wey, featuring grazed meadows and reedbeds. It supports diverse birdlife, dragonflies, and various plant species. Water levels fluctuate seasonally.

Name: Parc Slip (Glamorgan Wildlife Hub)
Description: Parc Slip is a nature reserve and visitor centre managed by the Wildlife Trust of South and West Wales. It features diverse habitats including wetlands, woodlands, and grasslands, supporting various bird species and wildflowers. The visitor centre offers facilities and information about local wildlife.

Name: Park Hoskyn (The Hayman Reserve)
Description: Park Hoskyn, near Nanpean in mid-Cornwall, is a Hayman Reserve site. It features an alder and willow carr, grassland, and a section of the Luxulyan River. This small nature reserve supports diverse wildlife, including otters and bats. It provides a peaceful setting for nature observation.

Name: Park Road Pond
Description: Park Road Pond is a small, enclosed aquatic habitat located in Crouch End, Haringey, North London. This urban oasis supports diverse invertebrate life, including rare species. It is a community-managed green space.

Name: Parliament Piece
Description: Parliament Piece is a small, species-rich meadow near Kenilworth, Warwickshire, supporting diverse wildflowers like ragged-robin and common spotted-orchid. The site is managed for biodiversity, enhancing its ecological value, and features unimproved grassland and scrub habitats.

Name: Parndon Wood
Description: Parndon Wood Nature Reserve is ancient woodland, designated a Local Nature Reserve in Essex. It features mature hornbeam, oak, and hazel trees, supporting diverse flora and fauna. The reserve provides footpaths for visitors to explore its varied habitats.

Name: Parsonage Moor
Description: Parsonage Moor is a small, species-rich fen meadow, a stronghold for rare wetland plants including orchids like common spotted and marsh fragrant orchid. The reserve supports diverse insect life and is managed by BBOWT for conservation.

Name: Parsonage Wood (Ancient Wealden Ghyll)
Description: Parsonage Wood, an ancient Wealden ghyll woodland, lies on a steep sandstone bank. It features mature sweet chestnut, oak, birch, hazel, and hornbeam alongside diverse flora and fauna. The site supports a rich array of fungi and invertebrates.

Name: Pasture Wharf
Description: Pasture Wharf is a nature reserve on the north bank of the River Humber. It features diverse habitats including grassland, scrub, reedbed, and a pond. The site supports various bird species such as reed warblers and Cetti’s warblers. Winter brings waders and wildfowl to the area.

Name: Pathways for Pearls (Butterfly Recovery)
Description: Pathways for Pearls enhances biodiversity at Welshpool's Old Station, creating wildflower meadows and butterfly habitats. It supports the Pearl-bordered Fritillary by planting nectar-rich flowers and sheltered areas. The project offers educational and volunteering opportunities.

Name: Patmore Heath
Description: Patmore Heath is a 17-hectare nature reserve in Hertfordshire, featuring a mosaic of valuable habitats including damp grassland, heathland, and ancient woodland. It supports notable plant and insect species. The site is managed for biodiversity and provides public access via footpaths.

Name: Paull Holme Strays (Humber)
Description: Paull Holme Strays is a large wetland nature reserve on the Humber Estuary, managed by Yorkshire Wildlife Trust. It provides vital habitat for thousands of migrating waders and wildfowl, especially in winter. The reserve features saline lagoons, mudflats, and reedbeds, with bird hides for visitors.

Name: Pavenham Osier Beds
Description: Pavenham Osier Beds is a 1.2-hectare site beside the River Great Ouse, managed by the Wildlife Trust BCN. It features wet woodland, reedbed, and floodplain grassland, supporting diverse birds, insects, and plant species.

Name: Paxton Pits (managed interface)
Description: Paxton Pits Natural Park is a working sand and gravel quarry, with flooded gravel pits, islands, meadows, and woodland beside the Great Ouse river. This site is managed as a nature reserve, offering diverse habitats and a range of wildlife year-round.

Name: Paycocke’s House (Cottage Garden & Meadow)
Description: Paycocke’s House is a surviving medieval merchant’s house in Coggeshall, Essex. Dating from around 1500, it features intricate timber framing and carvings. The property includes a cottage garden and meadow, showcasing traditional planting.

Name: Peascombe
Description: Peascombe is a 0.8-hectare nature reserve in Dorset, featuring broadleaf woodland and acidic grassland. Historically a WWII minefield, the site is bordered by arable fields. It supports various plants, including dwarf thistle and a range of lichens.

Name: Pease Dean (Ancient Border Wood)
Description: Pease Dean is an ancient woodland reserve featuring steep gorges, a burn, and diverse flora in the Scottish Borders. Mature ash, oak, and elm trees dominate, supporting rich invertebrate communities. Otters and badgers are resident.

Name: Peckover House (Victorian Walled Garden & Parkland)
Description: A Georgian merchant's house in Wisbech, Cambridgeshire, with a two-acre Victorian walled garden. The house contains original furnishings, and the garden features a parterre, fruit trees, and various garden rooms.

Name: Pegsdon Hills and Hoo Bit
Description: Pegsdon Hills and Hoo Bit is a chalk grassland nature reserve in Bedfordshire. It features a mosaic of habitats including woodland, scrub, and agricultural land. The site supports rare butterflies like the Chalkhill Blue and Duke of Burgundy. Panoramic views across the Chilterns are a highlight.

Name: Pen Ponds (Richmond Park)
Description: Pen Ponds are two large, man-made ponds in the centre of Richmond Park, created in 1746. Fed by a spring, they host diverse birdlife like Grey Herons and Great Crested Grebes. Fishing is permitted with a permit acquired from the Roehampton Gate office.

Name: Pen y Fan & Central Beacons (NT Landscape)
Description: Pen y Fan and the Central Beacons are a prominent landscape in the Brecon Beacons National Park. This area encompasses the highest peak in southern Britain, offering extensive views and diverse habitats. Popular for walking and outdoor activities, it features rugged terrain and rich natural beauty.

Name: Penbryn (Upland-to-Sea Corridor)
Description: Penbryn estate on the Ceredigion coast features a mile of golden sand beach and ancient woodlands. A river, the Hoffnant, flows through the estate. It offers habitat for wildlife and trails for walkers, with a focus on nature conservation.

Name: Pencnwc Mawr (Ancient Rainforest Core)
Description: Pencnwc Mawr is a temperate rainforest site in Montgomeryshire, managed by Montgomeryshire Wildlife Trust. The project restores 17ha of ancient and semi-natural woodland, replacing conifer plantations with native broadleaf trees. It is part of a wider 500ha rainforest project.

Name: Pendarves Wood
Description: Pendarves Wood is an ancient woodland near Camborne, Cornwall, featuring mature broadleaf and conifer trees with a diverse understory. Bluebells and ramsons carpet the woodland floor in spring. The site is home to badger setts and diverse wildlife.

Name: Peninnis Head (Maritime Heathland Ark)
Description: Peninnis Head is a maritime heathland site on St Mary's, Isles of Scilly. It hosts rare plants like fescue and early meadow grass. The site is part of a restoration project aiming to return native heathland to the islands. Managed by the Isles of Scilly Wildlife Trust.

Name: Penlee Battery Woods
Description: Penlee Battery Woods overlooks Plymouth Sound. The woods contain diverse habitats including ancient semi-natural woodland, maritime grassland and scrub, alongside historic military features.

Name: Penmon Point (Anglesey Marine Gateway)
Description: Penmon Point, on the eastern tip of Anglesey, offers expansive views across to Puffin Island and the Great Orme. An iconic offshore lighthouse marks the site. It features a pebble beach, strong tidal currents, and is recognised for its diverse wildlife, especially seabirds. The area forms part of a significant marine gateway.

Name: Penn Wood (Calne Rewilding)
Description: Penn Wood is an ancient woodland near Calne, Wiltshire, managed by Wiltshire Wildlife Trust. It features ancient trees like oak and ash, a diverse wildflower understorey, and supports various wildlife species. The Trust is also rewilding 50 acres adjacent to the site.

Name: Pennington Flash (Nature Recovery Hub)
Description: Pennington Flash, near Leigh, is a significant nature reserve managed by Wigan Council. It offers diverse habitats for wildlife, including many rare bird species, and provides opportunities for walking, cycling, and fishing. The site operates as a key nature recovery hub.

Name: Pennytown Ponds (Somercotes)
Description: Pennytown Ponds is a 2.2-hectare nature reserve near Somercotes, Ripley, Derbyshire. It features a mosaic of ponds, marshland, and woodland, providing habitats for amphibians, insects, and birds. Amber Valley Borough Council manages the site.

Name: Penorchard Meadows (Species-Rich Grassland)
Description: Penorchard Meadows is a traditional hay meadow system in the Teme Valley. It features diverse wildflowers, including great burnet, knapweed, and ragged-robin. The reserve supports varied insect life, notably marbled white butterflies. A public footpath provides access.

Name: Penrose & Loe Pool (Largest Natural Lake)
Description: Penrose, encompassing Cornwall's largest natural freshwater lake, Loe Pool, is separated from the sea by a shingle bar. The estate offers diverse habitats across woodlands, historic parkland, and farmland, providing various walking routes.

Name: Pensthorpe Natural Park
Description: Pensthorpe Natural Park is an award-winning 700-acre nature reserve and conservation charity in Norfolk, UK. It features diverse habitats, including wetlands, woodlands, and gardens, supporting a variety of wildlife and offering conservation experiences.

Name: Penthellyg (Species-rich Grassland)
Description: Penthellyg is a small field of unimproved, species-rich grassland in mid-Wales, adjacent to the River Dyfi. It supports wildflowers including greater butterfly-orchid and yellow rattle. Wetter areas host sedges, rushes, and various invertebrates.

Name: Pentrosfa Mire (Rare Lowland Mire)
Description: Pentrosfa Mire is a small lowland mire in Powys, Wales, managed by Radnorshire Wildlife Trust. It supports a diverse range of plant and insect life, including sundew, bog mosses, and several rare species. The site is a significant habitat for biodiversity.

Name: Pentwyn (The 164-acre Rewilding Flagship)
Description: Pentwyn, a 164-acre former farm in mid Wales, serves as Radnorshire Wildlife Trust's rewilding flagship. The site is transforming into diverse habitats like woodlands, meadows, and wetlands, driven by natural processes and species reintroduction. It aims to restore biodiversity and ecological resilience in the Marches.

Name: Pentwyn Farm
Description: Pentwyn Farm is a 65-hectare nature reserve near Kingstone, Herefordshire, managed by Herefordshire Wildlife Trust. It encompasses ancient woodland, flower-rich meadows, and traditional orchards, supporting diverse wildlife including dormice, bats, and rare butterflies.

Name: Pentwyn Farm (Traditional Hay Meadow)
Description: Pentwyn Farm, in the Usk Valley, conserves a traditional hay meadow. Rich in wildflowers like orchids and various insect species, it provides habitat for butterflies. Sheep grazing maintains the sward structure. The reserve offers peaceful walking trails.

Name: Pentwyn Market Garden (New Entrant Project)
Description: Pentwyn Market Garden, near Penybont, Powys, is a three-year Radnorshire Wildlife Trust project. It establishes a market garden, orchards, and tree nursery for agroecological food and nature recovery. The project offers community training, workshops, and volunteering.

Name: Pepper Wood
Description: Pepper Wood is a small, ancient deciduous woodland in Renfrewshire, managed by the Scottish Wildlife Trust. It features mature oak, ash, and hazel trees, supporting a diverse array of bird species, fungi, and wildflowers. Public footpaths offer access for visitors.

Name: Pepperbox Wood
Description: Pepperbox Wood is a small ancient woodland near Salisbury, Wiltshire. It features oak and ash trees with a diverse ground flora including bluebells and wild garlic. The site is known for its distinctive 17th-century pepperbox-shaped lodge.

Name: Peppercombe Wood (Urchfont Scarp)
Description: Peppercombe Wood is an ancient woodland located on the Urchfont Scarp. It features diverse broadleaf trees, including oak and ash, with ground flora like bluebells. The wood supports various bird species and invertebrates. Public footpaths offer visitor access.

Name: Perry Woods
Description: Perry Woods spans 149 acres near Selling, Kent, comprising woodland, orchards, and grassland. It sits on a north-facing chalk escarpment. The site is noted for its spring bluebells and hosts over 50 bird species and 20 butterfly species, including the grizzled skipper.

Name: Peter's Stone (Wardlow Mires)
Description: Peter's Stone is a limestone grassland nature reserve near Wardlow Mires in the Peak District. It features species-rich grassland and ancient ash woodland, known for diverse flora including rare orchids. Managed by Derbyshire Wildlife Trust, it offers views across the Wye Valley.

Name: Petershill
Description: Petershill, a Scottish Wildlife Trust reserve near West Linton in the Scottish Borders, features varied habitats including woodland, grassland, and a pond. It supports diverse birds, insects, and wildflowers, providing crucial local wildlife habitat.

Name: Pett Level (Coastal Marsh)
Description: Pett Level in East Sussex encompasses a significant coastal marsh and shingle environment, including the freshwater wetland known as Pett Pools. This area is crucial for diverse marine life and provides important nesting grounds for various bird species along the East Sussex coastline.

Name: Pevensey Marshes
Description: Pevensey Marshes is a large expanse of grazing marsh and dykes, home to rare plants and insects. It is a Site of Special Scientific Interest (SSSI) and a Ramsar site, recognised internationally for its wetland importance.

Name: Pevensey Marshes Nature Reserve (Grazing Marsh)
Description: Pevensey Marshes is a grazing marsh in East Sussex, designated a Ramsar wetland and SSSI. It supports diverse wildlife, including rare plants, birds like lapwing, and various insects. This significant site forms part of a crucial network of habitats.

Name: Pevensey Road Nature Reserve
Description: Pevensey Road Nature Reserve is a small urban green space in Forest Gate, East London. Managed by The Conservation Volunteers, it provides a habitat for local wildlife and a tranquil area for community engagement. The site features woodland and diverse plant life.

Name: Pewit Island
Description: Pewit Island is an uninhabited nature reserve in Langstone Harbour, Hampshire. It serves as a crucial high-tide roost for wading birds like dunlin and grey plover, and supports breeding terns. Saltmarsh and shingle habitats host diverse coastal flora.

Name: Pewit Island Nature Reserve (Portsmouth)
Description: Pewit Island is an uninhabited island within Langstone Harbour, providing a vital sanctuary for wildlife. It is a nationally important breeding site for little terns and other seabirds. Access is restricted to protect nesting birds and sensitive habitats.

Name: Pewley Down & Pewley Down Fields
Description: Pewley Down is a traditional chalk grassland near Guildford, known for its diverse wildflowers and butterflies. The site offers panoramic views across the Wey Valley. It is managed to enhance biodiversity and public access.

Name: Philips Park (Whitefield/Bury)
Description: Philips Park is a Victorian Green Flag park in Whitefield, Bury, offering a lake, cafe, and children's play areas. It is also a local nature reserve, providing walking trails and recreational activities for the community.

Name: Phillips's Point
Description: Phillips's Point is a quiet Cornwall Wildlife Trust nature reserve located on the River Lynher, near Antony Passage. It protects important saltmarsh and estuarine mudflats, vital habitats for diverse birdlife, including waders and wildfowl.

Name: Phoenix Garden (Covent Garden)
Description: The Phoenix Garden is a community green space and registered charity in London's West End. Established in 1984 on a former car park, it provides an oasis for urban wildlife and people, featuring frogs, newts, and foxes.

Name: Phoenix Park (South Yorkshire Community Woodland)
Description: Phoenix Park is a community woodland located in Sheffield, South Yorkshire. The Conservation Volunteers (TCV) manage the site, conducting weekly practical conservation sessions every Wednesday. It provides an accessible green space for local residents.

Name: Phyllis Currie
Description: Phyllis Currie is a 2.5-hectare reserve in Essex, featuring mixed woodland and an old gravel pit. The woodland is mainly oak, with hornbeam and sweet chestnut. The gravel pit area hosts scrub, grassland, a small pond, and a marshy section.

Name: Phyllis Currie (Partner)
Description: Phyllis Currie (Partner) is a small nature reserve in Tring, Hertfordshire, managed by Herts and Middlesex Wildlife Trust. This open area features wildflowers, hedgerows, and trees, providing habitats for insects and birds. It offers a tranquil space for local wildlife.

Name: Phyllis Currie Nature Reserve
Description: Phyllis Currie Nature Reserve features grassland, scrub, and mature oak woodland. It provides a habitat for woodland birds and wildflowers, forming a key part of a wildlife corridor between other local reserves.

Name: Pick Triangle Wood
Description: Pick Triangle Wood, opened 2005, is a young woodland near Measham, Leicestershire. It features accessible paths, a small stream, and native broadleaf species. The wood includes an arboretum area and connects to a longer walking route along the Ashby Canal.

Name: Pickering's Meadow
Description: Pickering's Meadow is a nature reserve in West Lindsey, near Caenby Corner, featuring neutral grassland, a pond, and woodland. It provides a habitat for various orchids, butterflies, and birds, and is managed for wildlife. Public access is available via a short path.

Name: Pigneys Wood
Description: Pigneys Wood is a diverse Norfolk Wildlife Trust reserve near North Walsham. It features ancient woodland, mixed plantations, meadow, and wetland habitats. The site supports a variety of wildlife, including bats, barn owls, and otters, with accessible trails for visitors.

Name: Pigneys Wood (Ancient Trackway Wood)
Description: Pigneys Wood near North Walsham is a 54-acre nature reserve with ancient woodland, grassland and wetland. It is part of an ecological network between the Ant and Bure river valleys and features a network of paths, a boardwalk, and a bird hide.

Name: Pilch Field (Unimproved Grassland)
Description: Pilch Field is an ancient hay meadow in the Chilterns Area of Outstanding Natural Beauty. This unimproved grassland supports diverse wildflowers and grasses, providing valuable habitat for insects and birds.

Name: Pilmuir Riparian Project (2026 Flagship)
Description: The Pilmuir Riparian Project, near Peebles in the Scottish Borders, restores riverbanks along the Eddleston Water. It involves planting native trees and improving biodiversity. This is a collaboration between SP Energy Networks, Borders Forest Trust, and Tweed Forum.

Name: Pilsdon Pen (Dorset's Second Highest Point)
Description: Pilsdon Pen is a prominent hill in west Dorset, reaching 277 metres (909 feet) above sea level. It features an Iron Age hill fort, offering expansive views across the Marshwood Vale and to the coast. Managed by the National Trust, it is a significant archaeological and natural landmark.

Name: Pinchbeck Fen Slipe
Description: Pinchbeck Fen Slipe is a small Lincolnshire Wildlife Trust nature reserve near Spalding. It features diverse grassland and wetland habitats, supporting various dragonflies, damselflies, and wildflowers. The site includes a pond and areas of wet woodland.

Name: Pirbright Common
Description: Pirbright Common is a heathland site in Surrey, providing habitats for a range of wildlife, including rare birds, reptiles, and invertebrates. The common is managed to conserve its diverse ecosystems and support local biodiversity.

Name: Pitsford Water
Description: Pitsford Water Nature Reserve, managed by the Wildlife Trust BCN, is a significant site for overwintering wildfowl and migrating birds in Northamptonshire. The reserve features diverse habitats including woodlands, grasslands, and reedbeds. Walking trails are available.

Name: Pitts Wood (Chilterns)
Description: Pitts Wood is a small ancient woodland in the Chiltern Hills, located near Lane End, Buckinghamshire. The site features mature beech and oak trees, a stream, and diverse ground flora. It supports varied wildlife including various birds and invertebrates, offering a quiet natural retreat.

Name: Pity Me Carrs
Description: Pity Me Carrs is a wetland nature reserve situated near Pity Me in County Durham. It encompasses diverse marshland and grassland habitats, providing a vital home for various bird species and plant life.

Name: Pixey and Yarnton Meads
Description: These two large flood meadows on the River Cherwell are ancient and species-rich. Designated as a Site of Special Scientific Interest, they support a diverse range of grassland flora and fauna. Management involves traditional hay-cutting and grazing.

Name: Pleasley Pit Country Park (Colliery Restoration)
Description: Pleasley Pit Country Park, a former Nottinghamshire coal mine, has been transformed into a vibrant nature reserve. It features a preserved working steam winding engine and heritage centre, offering visitors walking trails and rich wildlife habitats. The park uniquely combines industrial history with diverse biodiversity for public enjoyment.

Name: Ploughman Wood
Description: Ploughman Wood is an ancient woodland in Nottinghamshire, rich in oak and hazel. The reserve supports a diverse ground flora, including bluebells and wood anemones, providing habitats for various bird and insect species throughout the year.

Name: Ploughman Wood (Ancient Woodland)
Description: Ploughman Wood is an ancient woodland, once part of Sherwood Forest, covering 41 acres. The reserve features diverse habitats including mature oak, birch, and hazel coppice, supporting numerous bird species and butterflies. Ancient earthworks are also present.

Name: Polhill Bank
Description: Polhill Bank is a sloping chalk grassland on the Kent Downs with ancient woodland at its summit. This reserve provides a habitat for diverse wildflowers, including orchids, and numerous invertebrates, such as butterflies. It offers panoramic views over the valley.

Name: Polzeath Coast
Description: Pentire, near Polzeath on the North Cornwall coast, offers dramatic clifftop walks and views across the Atlantic. This headland includes diverse habitats from maritime heathland to secluded coves, supporting varied coastal wildlife.

Name: Pontesford Hill (Lower Woods)
Description: Pontesford Hill (Lower Woods) is part of a 137-hectare Shropshire Wildlife Trust nature reserve. It features ancient semi-natural woodland dominated by oak, ash, birch, and hazel, supporting diverse wildlife. It forms part of a wider SSSI.

Name: Poole Hill
Description: Poole Hill is a small nature reserve near Flax Bourton, North Somerset. Ancient woodland with mature oak and hazel supports diverse fungi and invertebrates. Adjacent limestone grassland supports varied flora. Public access is available.

Name: Poolhay Meadows
Description: Poolhay Meadows, near Longhope in the Forest of Dean, is a Gloucestershire Wildlife Trust nature reserve. Four species-rich hay meadows provide habitats for diverse wildflowers, grasses, insects, and small mammals, contributing to local biodiversity.

Name: Portbury Wharf
Description: Portbury Wharf Nature Reserve, a key coastal wetland in North Somerset, features salt marsh, open water, and reedbeds. It supports diverse bird species, including waterfowl and waders. The reserve offers walking trails and bird hides.

Name: Porth y Swnt (Llyn Peninsula NT)
Description: Porth y Swnt is a National Trust visitor centre in Aberdaron, Gwynedd, on the Llyn Peninsula. It serves as an interpretive hub, detailing the area's geology, wildlife, and cultural heritage through displays. The centre offers visitor information.

Name: Porthdinllaen (Seagrass Protection Hub)
Description: Porthdinllaen, a fishing village on the Llŷn Peninsula, is managed by the National Trust. Its coastal waters host extensive seagrass meadows, vital for marine biodiversity. Efforts focus on protecting and restoring these habitats, engaging local communities and visitors in conservation.

Name: Portmore Lough (Lowland Wet Grassland)
Description: Portmore Lough is an important area of lowland wet grassland with open water, reedbeds and scrub, supporting many wetland birds. Significant for wintering wildfowl and breeding waders, it also hosts a range of invertebrates and plant species.

Name: Portrack Marsh SSSI
Description: Portrack Marsh is a tidal reedbed and wet grassland habitat on the River Tees' northern bank. Designated as a Site of Special Scientific Interest (SSSI), it supports diverse birdlife, including breeding reed warblers and overwintering wildfowl.

Name: Portstewart Strand (Dynamic Dunes)
Description: Portstewart Strand is a two-mile sandy beach and dune system in County Londonderry, Northern Ireland, managed by the National Trust. It supports diverse wildlife, including a range of birds and flora, and offers access to the River Bann estuary.

Name: Portway Hill (The Rowley Hills Gateway)
Description: Portway Hill is a prominent hill, grassland, and scrub area located in the Rowley Hills, part of the wider Black Country Geopark. It offers views across the surrounding landscape and supports diverse flora and fauna, including butterflies and wildflowers.

Name: Pot of Doan (Mournes PEAT+ Site)
Description: Pot of Doan is a peatland restoration site in the Mourne Mountains, Northern Ireland. It is part of the PEATLANDS+ cross-border project, led by Ulster Wildlife. This initiative restores degraded peatlands to enhance carbon storage, biodiversity, and water quality.

Name: Potteric Carr (Urban Wetland Giant)
Description: Potteric Carr, near Doncaster, is a large urban wetland managed by Yorkshire Wildlife Trust. It protects diverse habitats like wetlands, woodlands, and grasslands, supporting many bird species, invertebrates, and plants. Visitor trails and hides are available.

Name: Potterland Wood
Description: Potterland Wood is a small ancient woodland covering 2.4 hectares near Arnold, Nottinghamshire. It features oak, ash, and hazel trees, with a rich ground flora including bluebell and wood anemone. The reserve supports diverse wildlife, including various bird species and invertebrates, providing a valuable habitat.

Name: Poulter Country Park (Former Colliery)
Description: Poulter Country Park is a reclaimed colliery site offering walking trails, wildlife habitats, and historical remnants. Located near Bolsover, it features diverse woodlands, wetlands, and open grassland areas, providing recreational and educational opportunities.

Name: Pound Wood
Description: Pound Wood is an ancient woodland nature reserve in Essex, known for its diverse flora and fauna. It features mature hornbeam and oak trees, bluebells, and a variety of butterflies and bird species like nightingales. Managed for biodiversity.

Name: Pound Wood (Heath Fritillary Site)
Description: Pound Wood is an ancient woodland in Essex, managed by Essex Wildlife Trust. It supports diverse wildlife, including the nationally rare Heath Fritillary butterfly. Other species present are dormice, nightingales, and marsh tits, contributing to the South Essex Living Landscape initiative.

Name: Prawle Point (Devon's Southernmost Point)
Description: Prawle Point is Devon's southernmost point, offering expansive coastal views and diverse wildlife. It features a historic coastguard station, serving as a lookout for passing ships. The area is known for its rugged cliffs, rare migrating birds, and wildflowers, providing a habitat for many species.

Name: Prees Heath (Silver-studded Blue Sanctuary)
Description: Prees Heath is a crucial refuge for the rare silver-studded blue butterfly, one of only three sites in England. This lowland heathland, part of a former WWII airfield, supports diverse flora and fauna, including rare birds and reptiles. Managed for conservation.

Name: Prehen Wood (Ancient Derry Wood)
Description: Prehen Wood, an ancient woodland overlooking the River Foyle, is a crucial urban green space in Derry/Londonderry. It hosts a rich diversity of native trees including oak, ash, and hazel, supporting various wildlife species. Managed for conservation and public access.

Name: Pressmennan Wood (East Lothian)
Description: Pressmennan Wood is a steep-sided ancient woodland in East Lothian, known for its veteran trees including oak, ash, and elm. It boasts a diverse range of flora and fauna, making it an important ecological site. The wood features a small freshwater loch and a network of paths.

Name: Priest Hill
Description: Priest Hill, a former MOD site near Epsom, is managed by Surrey Wildlife Trust. It comprises wildflower meadows, scrub, and woodland, supporting diverse wildlife including rare plants, butterflies, and birds. It offers public access for walking.

Name: Priest Hill (Epsom Restoration)
Description: Priest Hill is a 60-hectare nature reserve on Epsom Downs, a former landfill site now transformed into wildflower-rich chalk grassland and new woodland. It provides habitat for butterflies, birds, and small mammals, offering walking trails and panoramic views.

Name: Priestcliffe Lees (Wye Valley Scrub & Grassland)
Description: Priestcliffe Lees is a limestone grassland and scrub nature reserve located in the Wye Valley. It supports a diverse flora, including several orchid species and the rare dark red helleborine. Butterflies, such as the northern brown argus and small pearl-bordered fritillary, thrive here. The site is actively managed for biodiversity.

Name: Priestclose Wood
Description: Priestclose Wood is an ancient semi-natural woodland nature reserve near Slaley, Northumberland. Managed by Northumberland Wildlife Trust, it features oak, birch, and hazel trees. The site supports diverse flora like bluebells and wood anemones, and various bird species. Walking trails are present.

Name: Priestley Wood
Description: Priestley Wood is an ancient woodland, once part of the deer park of Priestley House. It features a diverse range of tree species, including oak, ash, and field maple, along with a rich ground flora. Public access is available.

Name: Prince's Community Wood
Description: Prince's Community Wood is a 36-hectare young woodland within The National Forest, near Swadlincote. Planted 2004-2007, it offers broadleaf trees, ponds, and open areas. The site supports diverse wildlife habitats and provides recreational opportunities for visitors.

Name: Prior Park Landscape Garden
Description: Prior Park Landscape Garden is an 18th-century landscape garden overlooking Bath. Created by Ralph Allen with advice from Alexander Pope and Capability Brown, it features a Palladian Bridge, serpentine lake, and historic garden buildings. The National Trust cares for the garden.

Name: Prior's Coppice
Description: Prior's Coppice is a 13.5-hectare ancient woodland in Leicestershire, managed for biodiversity. It features mature oak, ash, and hazel coppice, supporting diverse flora and fauna. The site is a Leicestershire and Rutland Wildlife Trust nature reserve.

Name: Prior's Wood
Description: Prior's Wood is an ancient woodland near Portbury known for its seasonal bluebells. The site features mature trees, diverse ground flora, and a stream, providing habitat for various wildlife species including dormice and birds.

Name: Prisk Wood (The Wye Valley Rainforest)
Description: Prisk Wood is a 35-hectare Gwent Wildlife Trust reserve in the Wye Valley Area of Outstanding Natural Beauty. This ancient semi-natural woodland features a rich diversity of mosses, lichens, and ferns. It provides a vital habitat for local wildlife within its mosaic of woodland ecosystems.

Name: Prospect Fields
Description: Prospect Fields is a 6.7 hectare nature reserve near Pitney in Somerset. The site protects species-rich grassland, providing habitats for a variety of wildflowers and insects. Traditional hay meadow management maintains biodiversity.

Name: Pryor's Wood
Description: Pryor's Wood is an ancient woodland in Hertfordshire, featuring mature oak, hornbeam, and hazel coppice. The reserve supports diverse flora including bluebells, wood anemones, and wild daffodils. Birdlife thrives here, alongside various butterfly species.

Name: Pumlumon Project (40,000ha Landscape Scale)
Description: The Pumlumon Project covers 40,000 hectares in mid-Wales, working to restore peatlands, improve water quality, and enhance wildlife habitats. It supports sustainable farming practices, aiming to increase biodiversity and carbon storage within the landscape.

Name: Purple Horizons (10
Description: Purple Horizons is a collaborative project restoring and connecting 10,000 hectares of lowland heathland across Dorset, Hampshire, and the Isle of Wight. Partners include the Wildlife Trusts, National Trust, and RSPB, working to enhance biodiversity and landscape resilience for key species.

Name: Purwell Ninesprings
Description: Purwell Ninesprings is a mosaic of grassland, scrub, and wetland habitats on the northern edge of Hitchin, Hertfordshire. The site features the source of the River Purwell, emerging from chalk springs.

Name: Putney Heath (Independent)
Description: Putney Heath is a significant open space in South West London, managed by the Wimbledon and Putney Commons Conservators. It comprises heathland, woodland, and ponds, providing public access for recreation and supporting diverse wildlife within a protected landscape.

Name: Puttenham Common
Description: Puttenham Common is a large nature reserve near Guildford, managed by Surrey Wildlife Trust. It features extensive lowland heath, woodland, ponds, and acidic grassland, supporting diverse flora and fauna. Visitors can explore walking trails and observe various bird species and invertebrates.

Name: Puxton Moor
Description: Puxton Moor is a Somerset wetland reserve, managed by Avon Wildlife Trust. It features wet grassland, ditches, and rhynes, supporting diverse wetland plants and invertebrates. The site supports breeding wading birds and water voles. Public access is restricted.

Name: Pwllywrach (The Witch's Pool & Dormice)
Description: Pwllywrach Nature Reserve, near Colwinston in the Vale of Glamorgan, protects a steep-sided woodland valley. The reserve features ancient trees, a stream, and a notable waterfall. It is a vital habitat for various wildlife, including a nationally important population of dormice.

Name: Pymmes Brook (Restoration)
Description: Thames21 is restoring Pymmes Brook, a highly urbanised river in Enfield. Work focuses on improving river habitats, water quality, and biodiversity through various interventions like natural river features and riparian planting. The project enhances local green spaces.

Name: Pymmes Park (Wetland Project)
Description: Pymmes Park in Lower Edmonton, Enfield, is a Green Flag Award-winning urban park. It features a restored lake and wetland habitat, play areas, sports facilities, and a café. The park is open 24/7. The Friends of Pymmes Park actively run community events and regular volunteering days.

Name: Quants
Description: Quants reserve near Pitminster in the Blackdown Hills features ancient semi-natural woodland and species-rich unimproved grassland. It supports dormice, bats, butterflies, and various birds like marsh tit and willow warbler. The site maintains a mixed habitat mosaic.

Name: Quarr Community Woodland (Sherborne LNR)
Description: Quarr Community Woodland is a Local Nature Reserve bordering the A30 in Sherborne. Spanning 6.5 hectares, it provides diverse wildlife habitats and recreational space. Managed by Sherborne Town Council, the site offers public access.

Name: Quarry Bank & Styal Estate (Bollin Valley)
Description: Quarry Bank is a significant industrial heritage site featuring an 18th-century cotton mill, gardens, and woodlands within the Bollin Valley. The estate includes worker's cottages, the apprentice house, and extensive natural landscapes.

Name: Quarry Banks (Allan Wilson Memorial)
Description: Quarry Banks is a species-rich limestone grassland nature reserve near Bowness-on-Windermere, Cumbria. This former quarry offers diverse habitats including scrub, woodland, and exposed rock. It supports wildflowers like orchids and various butterfly species.

Name: Quarry Hangers (Caterham)
Description: Quarry Hangers is a steep chalk escarpment with woodland and open grassland, managed by Surrey Wildlife Trust. It supports diverse flora, including orchids and insects like the rare Adonis blue butterfly. It offers habitat for birds such as nightingales and various fungi species.

Name: Queen Elizabeth Country Park
Description: Queen Elizabeth Country Park sits within the South Downs National Park, offering vast woodlands, walking, and cycling trails. The park includes an adventure playground and a visitor centre. It preserves diverse habitats, from chalk grassland to mature beech hangers.

Name: Queen Elizabeth Country Park (managed forest)
Description: Queen Elizabeth Country Park is a large, diverse managed forest and downland park in Hampshire, cared for by Forestry England. It offers walking, cycling, and horse riding trails through woodlands and open spaces, plus a visitor centre and cafe. A range of habitats support local wildlife.

Name: Queen's Park
Description: Queen's Park is a Grade II listed historic park in north west London, managed by the City of London Corporation. It features formal gardens, woodlands, ornamental ponds, and sports facilities. The park also includes a children's playground, cafe, and a bandstand. It has received Green Flag status.

Name: Queendown Warren
Description: Queendown Warren is a Kent Wildlife Trust nature reserve on the North Downs. It comprises ancient chalk grassland, scrub, and woodland. The reserve supports diverse wildflowers, insects, and birds, including rare butterflies and orchids. Visitors can access various walking trails.

Name: Queendown Warren (SSSI Chalk Grassland)
Description: Queendown Warren is a nationally important nature reserve managed by Kent Wildlife Trust, a designated SSSI. This site features rich chalk grassland and woodland, supporting diverse flora and fauna. Rare orchids, butterflies, and invertebrates thrive here, making it a key conservation location in Kent.

Name: Queensbury Park (Kenton Brook)
Description: Queensbury Park, in Harrow, is a site for river habitat improvements along Kenton Brook. The project engages local residents and schools in environmental activities, enhancing biodiversity and waterway health.

Name: Queenswood Country Park & Arboretum
Description: Queenswood Country Park & Arboretum is a nationally important arboretum and ancient woodland, developed over 200 years. It holds a significant tree collection and offers expansive views, covering 123 acres. The site features a visitor centre and various walking trails.

Name: Quoit Heathland
Description: Quoit Heathland is a 16-hectare nature reserve near Padstow, Cornwall, protecting rare dry heathland. It provides a vital habitat for species such as grayling butterflies and nightjars. The site features open heath and gorse scrub, managed for wildlife.

Name: Rackenford and Knowstone Moors
Description: Rackenford and Knowstone Moors, a complex of Culm grassland and heathland in Mid Devon, features wet rush pasture and mire. It supports rare plants like wavy St John's-wort and marsh violet. The site provides a habitat for various bird species and butterflies.

Name: Rackenford and Knowstone Moors (NNR Heart)
Description: Rackenford and Knowstone Moors are a nationally important complex of culm grassland, valley mire, and heathland near Tiverton. These rare habitats support a diverse range of plant and insect species, including sundews and marsh fritillary butterflies. The site offers walking routes.

Name: Railway Fields (Haringey)
Description: Railway Fields is a local nature reserve in Haringey, North London, managed by The Conservation Volunteers. This former railway goods yard now boasts diverse habitats including woodland, meadow, and ponds, supporting various wildlife species. It serves as a vital urban green space and educational resource.

Name: Raincliffe Woods (Scarborough)
Description: Raincliffe Woods is a 200-hectare woodland site near Scarborough, North Yorkshire. It comprises three distinct woodlands: Raincliffe, Forge Valley, and Lady Edith's. The site offers diverse habitats and recreational paths. It is managed for biodiversity, recreation, and education.

Name: Rainham Hall (Wildlife Garden)
Description: Rainham Hall, a Georgian merchant's house built in 1729 in east London, offers insights into its past residents. The site features a historic house, a community garden, and a dedicated wildlife garden for visitors to explore.

Name: Rainton Meadows
Description: Rainton Meadows is a nature reserve managed by Durham Wildlife Trust, transformed from an open cast coal mine. It features wetlands, grasslands, and woodlands, offering walking trails, a visitor centre, and a cafe.

Name: Rainworth Heath
Description: Rainworth Heath, a Nottinghamshire SSSI, is a remnant of Sherwood Forest. It features heathland, acid grassland, and woodland, supporting rare plants, diverse invertebrates, and ground-nesting birds like woodlark and nightjar.

Name: Rainworth Heath (Rare Lowland Heath)
Description: Rainworth Heath is a rare lowland heathland habitat in Nottinghamshire, supporting adders, common lizards, and birds like woodlark and nightjar. It features heather, bell heather, and bilberry, which attract diverse insects in summer. This site is a significant remaining lowland heathland.

Name: Raisby Hill Grassland
Description: Raisby Hill Grassland in County Durham is a species-rich magnesian limestone grassland, formerly a quarry. It features dry grassland, scrub, and woodland habitats, supporting diverse wildflowers and insects. Rare species are present. The site is managed for biodiversity.

Name: Rammey Marsh
Description: Rammey Marsh is a significant area of open space in Enfield, bordering the River Lee Navigation. This marshland habitat supports diverse flora and fauna, including water voles and kingfishers. It offers walking routes and connects to the wider Lee Valley Regional Park.

Name: Ramsbury Meadow (Kennet Floodplain)
Description: Ramsbury Meadow is a species-rich wet grassland reserve on the River Kennet floodplain. Managed for biodiversity, it supports water voles, otters, and wetland birds. Delicate plants like marsh marigold thrive, enhancing its ecological value.

Name: Ramsden Corner (Acidic Woodland)
Description: Ramsden Corner is a small area of acidic woodland featuring mature oak, birch, and rowan trees. Its ground flora includes heather, bilberry, and bracken, typical of acid soils. The site provides a habitat for various woodland birds.

Name: Ramsey Heights Countryside Centre
Description: Ramsey Heights Countryside Centre, run by Wildlife Trust BCN, is the visitor centre for the Great Fen. It features diverse habitats, nature trails, and a bird hide overlooking wet grassland, serving as a key starting point for exploring the wider Great Fen.

Name: Ramsey Island - Grassland for Chough
Description: Ramsey Island is an offshore Site of Special Scientific Interest (SSSI) in Wales, managed by RSPB. Its diverse habitats support grey seals, choughs, peregrines, and ravens. Grasslands are vital for choughs, with breeding occurring on cliffs. It is a significant wildlife haven.

Name: Ramsey Island - Marine 30x30 Buffer
Description: Ramsey Island, off the Pembrokeshire coast, is a rugged RSPB reserve. It provides vital habitat for grey seals, seabirds, and peregrine falcons. Its dramatic cliffs and coastline support diverse marine and avian life, managed for conservation.

Name: Ramsey Island (Manx Shearwater Record)
Description: Ramsey Island, an RSPB nature reserve off the Pembrokeshire coast, is a vital sanctuary for seabirds, including the UK's largest population of Manx shearwaters. It also hosts choughs and grey seals, offering dramatic coastal scenery and rich wildlife encounters.

Name: Ranscombe Farm (Plantlife)
Description: Ranscombe Farm Reserve, located on the North Downs in Kent, is one of Kent's largest ancient woodlands and a designated Site of Special Scientific Interest (SSSI). It features globally important plantlife and wildlife, offering miles of footpaths through diverse habitats.

Name: Ranscombe Farm (Wildflower Hotspot)
Description: Ranscombe Farm, a Plantlife nature reserve near Rochester, Kent, features ancient woodland, traditional orchards, and arable fields managed for wildflowers. It supports diverse flora, including rare arable weeds, and offers public walking trails.

Name: Ranscombe Farm Reserve
Description: Ranscombe Farm Reserve is a significant site for rare arable wildflowers and ancient woodland near Cuxton in Kent. Managed by Kent Wildlife Trust, it offers diverse habitats including chalk grassland and orchards. It supports important conservation efforts.

Name: Ransom Wood (Sherwood Heath)
Description: Ransom Wood and Sherwood Heath is a Nottinghamshire nature reserve. It features ancient oak woodland, plantation woodland, and lowland heath. The site supports specialist heathland invertebrates, woodcock, and tree pipit, with areas of managed open heathland.

Name: Ranworth Broad and Marshes
Description: Ranworth Broad and Marshes is a Norfolk Wildlife Trust reserve within the Broads National Park. It features open water, extensive reedbeds, and wet woodland. A floating visitor centre provides views of diverse wildlife, including overwintering wildfowl and rare insects.

Name: Rathlin Island - Kebble NNR (Seabird Colony)
Description: Rathlin Island's Kebble NNR hosts significant seabird colonies, including puffins and guillemots, on its dramatic cliffs. The reserve features unique cliff-top flora, providing vital nesting habitats and is a key ecological site in Northern Ireland.

Name: Rauceby Warren
Description: Rauceby Warren, a former RAF bomb store, is a Lincolnshire grassland and woodland site. It supports diverse invertebrates like marbled white butterflies and six-spot burnet moths. Rare flora includes fragrant, common spotted, and bee orchids in the grassland areas.

Name: Raveley Wood
Description: Raveley Wood is an ancient woodland in Cambridgeshire, located near Huntingdon. It features coppiced hazel and hornbeam alongside mature oak trees. The wood is a habitat for dormice and various butterfly species, with seasonal displays of wildflowers in spring and fungi in autumn.

Name: Ravensroost Wood (Oak-Hazel Coppice)
Description: Ravensroost Wood is an ancient oak-hazel coppice woodland in Wiltshire, supporting a rich diversity of wildlife including dormice, badgers, and numerous bird species. The reserve features meadows, ponds, and traditional management practices.

Name: Red Cross Garden (Southwark)
Description: Red Cross Garden is a historic Victorian garden established by Octavia Hill in 1887, located in Southwark, London.
