It features a tranquil pond, mature trees, and flowerbeds, offering a peaceful green space. The garden is open daily to the public for relaxation and community engagement.

Name: Red Hill
Description: Red Hill, a Lincolnshire Wildlife Trust nature reserve near Barton-upon-Humber, features ancient woodland and species-rich chalk grassland. It supports notable plants like orchids and scarce insects. The site offers walking trails and provides important habitat for wildlife.

Name: Red House (Montgomery Canal Wetland)
Description: Red House is a small wetland nature reserve adjacent to the Montgomery Canal in Powys. It features marshy grassland, reedbed, and wet woodland habitats, supporting diverse wildlife including amphibians, insects, and birds. The site is managed for biodiversity.

Name: Red Moor (Marsh Fritillary Stronghold)
Description: Red Moor, a Cornwall Wildlife Trust reserve, is a nationally important site in a sheltered valley southwest of Bodmin. It features ancient woodland, open heath, and wet mires formed on former china clay workings, supporting diverse wildlife, including the marsh fritillary butterfly.

Name: Red Moss of Netherley
Description: Red Moss of Netherley is a raised bog south-west of Stonehaven, Aberdeenshire. It supports rare flora, including sphagnum mosses, cotton-grass, and heather. The site is a nature reserve managed for its ecological significance.

Name: Red River Nature Reserve (Loscoe)
Description: Red River Loscoe Nature Reserve is an important site for nature conservation in north Amber Valley. Owned by Amber Valley Borough Council since 1993, the 30-hectare site offers grassland, wetland, and woodland habitats. Accessible paths welcome walkers and nature enthusiasts.

Name: Red Rocks Marsh
Description: Red Rocks Marsh is a small coastal nature reserve, featuring sand dunes, saltmarsh, and adjacent beach habitats. It supports diverse bird species like waders and terns, serving as an important breeding ground. Unique saline-adapted plants also thrive here.

Name: Reddish Vale Country Park (Stockport)
Description: Reddish Vale Country Park spans 161 hectares in Stockport, Greater Manchester. It features woodlands, meadows, and riverside paths along the River Tame. The park offers walking, cycling, and horse riding, and is home to diverse wildlife and historical railway structures.

Name: Redesdale Forest (Otterburn Interface)
Description: Redesdale Forest lies within Northumberland, forming part of the expansive Border Forest Park managed by Forestry England. It is a working forest landscape situated south-east of Kielder Water and Forest Park, near the village of Otterburn, offering diverse habitats.

Name: Redesdale Peatland Pipeline (Investment Pilot)
Description: The Redesdale Peatland Pipeline is a pilot project restoring degraded peatland in Northumberland. It is a collaboration between Northumberland National Park Authority and Northumbrian Water. The 2023/24 phase involves contractors making significant progress on the restoration efforts.

Name: Redgrave and Lopham Fen
Description: Redgrave and Lopham Fen is England's largest valley fen, managed by Suffolk Wildlife Trust. It hosts nationally rare species, including the fen raft spider. Boardwalks and hides facilitate visitor access to diverse wetland habitats.

Name: Redlake Cottage Meadows
Description: Redlake Cottage Meadows is a small nature reserve located on the Lizard Peninsula, Cornwall. It contains two species-rich wet meadows, an example of unimproved culm grassland. Diverse wildflowers thrive, including ragged-robin and marsh orchid. The site is important for various insects.

Name: Reed Pond (Wollaton)
Description: Reed Pond is a small, quiet nature reserve tucked away behind the busy roads of Wollaton, Nottingham. The site is a valuable urban refuge for wildlife. The pond is fringed by reedbed and willow scrub, providing habitat for a range of invertebrates and birds.

Name: Rendlesham Forest (UFO Trail)
Description: Rendlesham Forest, near Woodbridge in Suffolk, is a large mixed woodland offering walking trails, cycle routes, and a popular UFO trail. The site is managed by Forestry England and provides a natural space for recreation, wildlife spotting, and family outdoor activities.

Name: Reservoir Meadow
Description: Reservoir Meadow is a small wetland nature reserve near Leominster, Herefordshire. It features a pond, wet grassland, and mature trees, supporting diverse wildflowers such as ragged robin and water mint. The site is managed by Herefordshire Wildlife Trust.

Name: Reston Scar (Staveley Hills)
Description: Reston Scar, a limestone escarpment, overlooks the Kentmere Valley. Diverse habitats, including limestone grassland, heathland, and woodland, support wildflowers, butterflies, and birds. The reserve offers excellent views of the Cumbrian landscape.

Name: Rewe Mead
Description: Rewe Mead is a small, species-rich grassland nature reserve located near Chew Valley Lake in Somerset. It features diverse wildflowers and grasses, supporting various insects, including rare butterflies. The site is managed by the Somerset Wildlife Trust to maintain its biodiversity.

Name: Reydon Wood
Description: Reydon Wood, a 33-hectare ancient woodland in Suffolk, hosts diverse trees and hazel coppice. Its rich ground flora includes bluebells, wood anemones, and early purple orchids. The site is a stronghold for the rare white admiral butterfly. A circular path allows exploration.

Name: Reydon Wood (Bluebell Stronghold)
Description: Reydon Wood is ancient woodland near Southwold, known for its extensive display of bluebells in spring. The reserve features diverse tree species, including oak and ash, alongside hazel coppice. It supports varied wildlife and offers walking trails.

Name: Rhos Cefn-bryn (Marshy Grassland)
Description: Rhos Cefn-bryn is a marshy grassland and fen habitat in Carmarthenshire, south-west Wales. It supports diverse plant species including bog pimpernel and marsh thistle. Notable invertebrates like the scarce blue-tailed damselfly are present. Managed by the Wildlife Trust of South and West Wales.

Name: Rhos Fiddle (Upland Peat Restoration)
Description: Rhos Fiddle is a 24-hectare nature reserve on the Welsh border, managed by Shropshire Wildlife Trust. Part of the Marches Mosses BogLIFE project, it restores rare raised bog and blanket bog habitats, featuring sphagnum mosses and peat-forming plants.

Name: Rhos Ithel (Marshy Grassland)
Description: Rhos Ithel is a 7.5-hectare marshy grassland in Anglesey, bordering Cors Goch NNR. It supports diverse plant and invertebrate life, characteristic of a 'Rhos' pasture. Grazing maintains its rich biodiversity, providing habitat for species such as Narrow-leaved Marsh-orchid.

Name: Rhosneigr Reefs (Marine 30x30 Pilot)
Description: This pilot focuses on seabed habitats off Rhosneigr, Anglesey, including rocky reefs, sand, and mixed sediments. These support diverse marine life. The project aims to establish a baseline understanding of habitats and species, monitoring changes over time.

Name: Rhossili Down & Worm's Head (Gower Archetype)
Description: Rhossili Down offers panoramic views across Rhossili Bay, featuring a three-mile sandy beach. Worm's Head is a tidal island extending from the Gower Peninsula. The site is noted for its geology, ancient burial cairns, and diverse coastal wildlife.

Name: Ribble Estuary NNR (NE/RSPB Partner)
Description: The Ribble Estuary National Nature Reserve spans vast wetland, mudflat, and saltmarsh habitats in Lancashire. It provides critical overwintering grounds for thousands of wildfowl and wading birds, making it internationally important. Managed by Natural England and RSPB.

Name: Richmond Park National Nature Reserve
Description: Richmond Park is London's largest Royal Park, spanning 2,500 acres. It is a National Nature Reserve and a European Special Area of Conservation. The park features herds of deer, ancient woodlands, and the renowned Isabella Plantation.

Name: Ricknall Carrs
Description: Ricknall Carrs is a Durham Wildlife Trust nature reserve near Newton Aycliffe, featuring ancient and wet woodland alongside marsh habitats. It supports diverse wildlife, including willow tit and marsh tit, and offers accessible public footpaths.

Name: Riddlesdown
Description: Riddlesdown offers a mosaic of chalk grassland, scrub, and woodland in South London. It supports diverse wildlife, including orchids, butterflies, and birds of prey, with extensive views and footpaths for public access.

Name: Ridlins Mire
Description: Ridlins Mire is a wetland nature reserve in Hertfordshire, featuring fen, marsh, and wet woodland habitats. It supports rare plant species such as marsh fern and great fen-sedge, alongside diverse invertebrates. Boardwalks offer visitor access for exploration.

Name: Rifle Butts Quarry (Chalk Flora)
Description: Rifle Butts Quarry, a disused chalk quarry in Yorkshire, preserves rare chalk grassland habitat. It supports over 100 plant species, including orchids and wildflowers, plus butterflies like dingy skipper and common blue. The site is managed via grazing and scrub clearance.

Name: Rigsby Wood
Description: Rigsby Wood is a Lincolnshire Wildlife Trust nature reserve near Rigsby, Lincolnshire. It features ancient woodland, primarily ash, oak, and hazel, supporting diverse flora and fauna. The site provides habitat for wildflowers, birds, and invertebrates.

Name: Rimrose Valley Country Park (Sefton)
Description: Rimrose Valley Country Park is a vital green space in Sefton, Merseyside, offering a haven for wildlife and local communities. The Friends group actively works to protect, enhance, and promote this significant natural asset.

Name: Ringdown
Description: Ringdown is a 7.5-hectare nature reserve in the Blackdown Hills AONB, Somerset. It features ancient woodland, wildflower meadows, and historic earthworks. The site supports dormice, butterflies, and woodland birds, offering managed public access.

Name: Ringstead Bay (Coastal Grassland)
Description: Ringstead Bay is a diverse coastal site in Dorset. It features shingle and sandy beaches, chalk cliffs, and ancient woodlands. The area is rich in wildlife, including rare insects and birds. Footpaths offer scenic views and access to the beach.

Name: Ringstead Downs
Description: Ringstead Downs is a chalk grassland valley with rare plants and insects. Habitats include flower-rich grassland, scrub, and woodland. The reserve features species such as the Pasque flower and various butterflies. It is actively managed to preserve its biodiversity.

Name: Ringstead Downs (Chalk Grassland)
Description: Ringstead Downs is Norfolk Wildlife Trust's only chalk grassland site, covering 30 hectares. It lies on glacial till overlaying chalk and is managed through grazing. The reserve supports diverse wildflowers and insects, including rare butterflies and orchids.

Name: Ripon City Wetlands
Description: Ripon City Wetlands is a new nature reserve, formerly a quarry near Ripon. It features diverse wetland habitats including reedbed, open water, and wet grassland, attracting numerous bird species. The reserve offers accessible paths and hides for wildlife observation.

Name: Risley Glebe
Description: Risley Glebe is a small wetland nature reserve in Derbyshire. It supports diverse wildlife, with ponds, reedbeds, wet grassland, and scrub creating a mosaic of habitats. Dragonflies, damselflies, and water voles are found here. The site is actively managed for biodiversity.

Name: Risley Moss (Local Authority Partner)
Description: Risley Moss Local Nature Reserve, a former Royal Ordnance Factory site in Warrington, features diverse habitats including woodland, heathland, and mossland. It provides a visitor centre, observation tower, and walking trails for public access and wildlife observation.

Name: River Lea Regional Park - Waterden
Description: River Lea Regional Park - Waterden forms part of the Queen Elizabeth Olympic Park in East London. It features significant wetland habitats, meadows, and the River Lea, supporting diverse wildlife including birds, insects, and aquatic species. The area contributes to urban biodiversity.

Name: River Lee Country Park
Description: River Lee Country Park, part of the Lee Valley Regional Park, offers diverse habitats like wetlands, grasslands, woodlands, supporting wildlife. The park features trails for walking, cycling, horse riding, watersports and fishing.

Name: River Otter (The Beaver Flagship)
Description: The River Otter Beaver Trial is a five-year study observing a wild beaver population on the River Otter in Devon, England. It monitors the ecological impacts and benefits of beavers in a riverine environment, focusing on their role in habitat creation and water management.

Name: River Rea Corridor (Natural Rivers Project)
Description: The River Rea Corridor project enhances urban river environments in Birmingham and the Black Country. It connects people and places along the River Rea, improving biodiversity and accessibility. The project links fragmented habitats, creating a wildlife-rich network for the local community.

Name: River Rom (The Chase LNR)
Description: The Rewilding the Rom project focuses on enhancing river habitats and increasing biodiversity along the River Rom. Located within the London Borough of Havering, this initiative undertakes habitat restoration work to improve the ecological health of the river.

Name: River Rye Project (Nunnington Hub)
Description: The River Rye Project, led by the Ryedale Catchment Management Group, improves the River Rye's health and biodiversity. The Nunnington area is a key hub for restoration work, focusing on water quality, fish passage at Nunnington Mill, and riparian habitat creation. It engages local communities in conservation efforts.

Name: River Sherbourne Project (Coventry Urban Recovery)
Description: The River Sherbourne Project restores Coventry's urban river, improving water quality and biodiversity. It enhances habitats and public access, transforming the Sherbourne into a green and blue corridor. Community and partner involvement drives its success.

Name: River Stour - Cradley Heath & Lye (Love Your River)
Description: The Love Your River Stour project operates along the River Stour in Cradley Heath and Lye, West Midlands. It focuses on habitat restoration, litter removal, and community engagement to improve the river environment and its biodiversity.

Name: Riverside Country Park (Medway Estuary)
Description: Riverside Country Park, Medway Estuary, covers 100 hectares of varied habitats including saltmarsh, coastal grazing marsh, and two Local Nature Reserves. It offers footpaths, cycling, picnic areas, and birdwatching, supporting significant wildlife.

Name: Robert's Field
Description: Robert's Field is a small traditional hay meadow, rich in wildflowers, enclosed by mature hedgerows and woodland. Situated on boulder clay, it remains wet in winter but retains moisture well in summer. Management involves traditional hay cutting and grazing.

Name: Robinswood Hill (Urban Gloucester 30x30)
Description: Robinswood Hill stands prominently south of Gloucester. It offers panoramic views across the Severn Vale to the Malvern Hills, May Hill, and the Cotswolds. Visitors can enjoy waymarked walking trails and a cafe.

Name: Rock & Daymer Bay
Description: Rock and Daymer Bay are located on the north Cornwall coast, part of the wider Pentire area. Visitors enjoy sandy beaches popular for watersports and family activities. A ferry service operates from Rock to Padstow, offering access to the Camel Estuary.

Name: Rockcliffe Marsh (Solway Firth Grazing)
Description: Rockcliffe Marsh is a vast expanse of saltmarsh and mudflats forming part of the Solway Firth. It is an internationally important site for wintering wildfowl and wading birds, managed for grazing and rich in unique marshland flora.

Name: Rockyfield Quarry (Geological Site)
Description: Rockyfield Quarry is a former sand and gravel quarry in Leicestershire, designated as a Site of Special Scientific Interest (SSSI) for its geology. It reveals a sequence of Triassic sediments laid down approximately 230 million years ago and supports diverse wildlife.

Name: Rodborough Common
Description: Rodborough Common is a Site of Special Scientific Interest and part of the Thursley, Hankley and Frensham Commons SAC. It features mixed woodland, acid grassland, and heathland. The common supports diverse wildlife, including birds, reptiles, and invertebrates. Active management aims to restore heathland habitats.

Name: Roding Valley Meadows
Description: Roding Valley Meadows is Essex's largest ancient riverside meadow system near Chigwell and Buckhurst Hill. It features diverse wildflowers and grasses, supporting various insect species. The River Roding flows through, providing important wetland habitats. Cattle grazing maintains the meadows.

Name: Roding Valley Meadows (Largest Floodplain Meadow)
Description: Roding Valley Meadows is Essex's largest floodplain meadow, managed by Essex Wildlife Trust. The site along the River Roding supports diverse wildlife, including water voles and kingfishers. It plays a crucial role in flood alleviation and is open for public access and nature observation.

Name: Roman River
Description: Roman River is a series of interconnected wildlife havens in north-east Essex, featuring meadows, ponds, and hedgerows. The reserve supports water voles, kingfishers, and rare plants, managed for conservation and public enjoyment. Footpaths offer access to diverse habitats along the river valley.

Name: Roman River Valley
Description: Roman River Valley is a varied site near Colchester, encompassing grasslands, hedgerows, and woodland. It provides habitats for various bird species, invertebrates, and wildflowers. The river meanders through the valley, supporting aquatic life and contributing to the diverse ecosystem.

Name: Romney Marsh Visitor Centre & Nature Reserve
Description: The Romney Marsh Visitor Centre provides a gateway to the unique landscape of Romney Marsh. It offers information, refreshments, and toilets for visitors. The surrounding nature reserve features footpaths, a pond, woods, and wildflower meadows for exploration.

Name: Romney Marsh Visitor Centre and Nature Reserve
Description: Romney Marsh Visitor Centre and Nature Reserve acts as a gateway to the wider Romney Marsh landscape, a nationally important site for rare species and habitats. The reserve features reedbeds, ponds, and grassland with accessible pathways, a wildlife garden, and bird hides. Educational activities and events take place here.

Name: Ron Ward's Meadow
Description: Ron Ward's Meadow is a sloping neutral grassland site near Alresford, Hampshire. It supports a variety of wildflowers, including common fleabane and lady's bedstraw, providing habitat for diverse insects.

Name: Ropley Lime Quarry
Description: Ropley Lime Quarry is a Site of Special Scientific Interest in Hampshire. It features chalk grassland, mature scrub, and supports a diverse range of butterflies, wildflowers, and birds. The former quarry provides important habitats.

Name: Rose End Meadows
Description: Rose End Meadows is a series of species-rich grassland, ancient woodland, and mature hedgerows near Cromford in Derbyshire. It is actively managed by Derbyshire Wildlife Trust to enhance biodiversity and protect various habitats for wildlife.

Name: Rosenannon Downs (Heathland Recovery)
Description: Rosenannon Downs, a Cornwall Wildlife Trust nature reserve, encompasses diverse habitats from dry to wet heathland. It supports a variety of wildlife, including specific butterfly species and birds. The site actively undergoes vital heathland recovery efforts.

Name: Roslin Glen
Description: Roslin Glen is a Scottish Wildlife Trust reserve protecting ancient woodland, meadow, and river habitats along the North Esk. It supports otters, kingfishers, dippers, and roe deer. The site includes the historic Roslin Glen Gunpowder Mills and is near Rosslyn Chapel.

Name: Rosliston Forestry Centre
Description: Rosliston Forestry Centre, a gateway site to the National Forest, offers woodlands, ponds, and meadows. Visitors enjoy walking trails, a children's play area, and a café. The centre, owned by South Derbyshire District Council, also hosts various events and educational programmes.

Name: Rothbury Estate (Simonside Hills)
Description: Rothbury Estate is a mosaic of moorland, woodland, and farmland in Northumberland. It encompasses part of the Simonside Hills, a popular destination for walkers. The estate supports diverse wildlife, including curlew, red grouse, and otter. It is managed for nature conservation and public access.

Name: Rothwell Country Park
Description: Rothwell Country Park is a former colliery site in south Leeds, transformed into a varied landscape of woodland, grassland, and wetland habitats. It features paths for walking and provides space for local wildlife.

Name: Rothwell Gullet
Description: Rothwell Gullet is a former ironstone quarry, now a nature reserve in Northamptonshire. Its lime-rich grassland supports diverse orchids, including common spotted and bee orchid. Butterflies, such as dingy skipper, thrive here. Various fungi are present, with conservation grazing managing the site.

Name: Rothwell Pastures
Description: Rothwell Pastures is a valuable habitat within the Lower Aire Valley, featuring fenland, grassland, and reedbed. The reserve supports diverse wildlife, including breeding birds and rare invertebrates. It serves as a vital green space for both nature and local community.

Name: Roudsea Wood and Mosses NNR
Description: Roudsea Wood and Mosses NNR comprises ancient woodlands, raised bogs, saltmarsh, and intertidal mudflats on the Leven Estuary in Cumbria. This diverse site supports a rich array of wildlife, including rare insects and plants.

Name: Rough Hill Wood (Ancient Oak & Birch)
Description: Rough Hill Wood, near Studley, is a small ancient woodland in Warwickshire. It features mature oaks, birch, hazel, and field maple. The ground flora includes bluebells, yellow archangel, and dog's mercury. It provides a habitat for woodland birds.

Name: Roughton Moor Wood
Description: Roughton Moor Wood is an ancient woodland in Lincolnshire, managed by the Lincolnshire Wildlife Trust. It features diverse trees and rich ground flora including bluebells and wood anemones. The reserve supports varied birdlife and invertebrates, offering accessible paths.

Name: Roundton Hill (The Volcanic Hillfort)
Description: Roundton Hill, a National Nature Reserve, is an isolated volcanic hill, home to wildflowers like mountain pansy and bloody crane's-bill. The summit features an Iron Age hillfort. The site offers panoramic views and diverse habitats for insects and birds. A prominent Shropshire Hills landmark.

Name: Rowallane Wood (NT Partner)
Description: Rowallane Garden, near Saintfield, County Down, is a historic demesne established in the mid-19th century. It features a walled garden, extensive shrubberies, and woodland with diverse plant collections. The estate offers tranquil walks and horticulture.

Name: Rowsley Sidings (Industrial Nature)
Description: Rowsley Sidings is a former railway marshalling yard, now a nature reserve managed by Derbyshire Wildlife Trust. It features diverse habitats including grasslands, scrub, and woodland, supporting wildflowers, insects, and birds. The site's industrial heritage is evident.

Name: Roydon Common
Description: Roydon Common is a key valley mire and heathland ecosystem in Norfolk, supporting diverse flora and fauna. It features rare invertebrates, natterjack toads, and various bird species. Habitats include wet and dry heath, fen, and woodland, managed for biodiversity.

Name: Roydon Common and Tony Hallatt Memorial Reserve
Description: Roydon Common is one of the largest and finest examples of a lowland acid valley mire in the UK. The reserve includes large areas of wet heath, fen, and woodland, supporting a wide variety of specialist plants and invertebrates. It is home to species such as redshank, nightjar, and raft spider. Management includes grazing and scrub removal to maintain open habitats.

Name: Roydon Fen (Bressingham)
Description: Roydon Fen, near Bressingham, is a unique valley fen fed by chalk-rich springs. It offers a mosaic of wet woodland, fen meadow, and open water habitats. Rare plants and diverse invertebrates thrive here. The site is managed for biodiversity.

Name: Roydon Woods
Description: Roydon Woods nature reserve is 375 acres of ancient woodland, grassland, heathland and wetland in the New Forest. It supports diverse species, including rare ground-nesting birds, reptiles, and invertebrates. Waymarked trails are available.

Name: Roydon Woods (Ancient New Forest Wood)
Description: Roydon Woods is an ancient woodland, heathland, and grassland complex within the New Forest. It supports diverse wildlife, including rare birds and plants. The reserve features veteran trees and is actively managed for conservation.

Name: RSPB Adur Estuary (Saltmarsh)
Description: Adur Estuary offers important intertidal mudflats and saltmarsh habitats for birds such as turnstone, dunlin, and grey plover. Located near Shoreham-by-Sea, it is a key site for overwintering waders and wildfowl, protected from development. The estuary supports a diverse ecosystem within a dynamic coastal environment.

Name: RSPB Adwick Washlands
Description: Adwick Washland is a developing wetland reserve in South Yorkshire, offering diverse habitats for wildlife. The site features new reedbeds, ponds, and grassland areas, attracting various bird species. It provides accessible trails and hides for visitors.

Name: RSPB Bempton Cliffs (England's Seabird City)
Description: RSPB Bempton Cliffs is a significant seabird colony on the Yorkshire coast, hosting around half a million birds, including gannets, puffins, and kittiwakes, between March and October. Visitors can observe nesting birds from cliff-edge viewpoints.

Name: RSPB Blacktoft Sands (Largest Tidal Reedbed)
Description: RSPB Blacktoft Sands is England's largest tidal reedbed, on the Humber Estuary. It provides vital habitat for avocets, marsh harriers, and bearded tits. The reserve features hides and accessible trails for birdwatching and observing wetland wildlife.

Name: RSPB Brading Marshes (Isle of Wight)
Description: Brading Marshes is the Isle of Wight's largest wetland, managed by RSPB. It provides a vital habitat for marsh harriers, bitterns, and water voles. Visitors use walking trails and hides to observe diverse wildlife across the former tidal estuary. It offers a significant haven for birds and insects.

Name: RSPB Buckenham Marshes (Yare Valley)
Description: RSPB Buckenham Marshes, located in the Yare Valley, is a nature reserve known for wintering wildfowl and raptors like marsh harriers and peregrines. The reserve features wet grassland habitats, attracting diverse bird species throughout the year.

Name: RSPB Burton Mere Wetlands (Dee Estuary)
Description: RSPB Burton Mere Wetlands is a wildlife haven on the Dee Estuary, featuring pools, reedbeds, and wet grassland. It hosts avocets, lapwings, and ducks, offering a visitor centre, hides, and trails for nature observation.

Name: RSPB Campfield Marsh (Solway Mosaic)
Description: RSPB Campfield Marsh is a vast expanse of saltmarsh, wet grassland, and peatbog on the Solway Estuary in Cumbria. It supports significant populations of breeding waders, wildfowl, and natterjack toads. Visitors can explore various trails and hides, observing diverse birdlife.

Name: RSPB Canvey Wick (Brownfield Rainforest)
Description: RSPB Canvey Wick is a unique brownfield SSSI in Essex, a former oil refinery site now a haven for rare insects and plants. It features varied habitats supporting species like the shrill carder bee and grey carpet moth, offering nature exploration.

Name: RSPB Dearne Valley - Adwick Sands
Description: Adwick Sands is a section of the RSPB Dearne Valley reserve, located in South Yorkshire. This area features managed wetlands and grassland, attracting a variety of birdlife, including wading birds and waterfowl throughout the year.

Name: RSPB Dee Estuary - Parkgate (Saltmarsh)
Description: The Dee Estuary's Parkgate saltmarsh offers expansive tidal habitats, a key site for wintering raptors like short-eared owls, hen harriers, and peregrines. Waders and geese feed here, making it a prime birdwatching location with easy footpath access.

Name: RSPB Dee Estuary - Point of Ayr (Dunes)
Description: RSPB Dee Estuary - Point of Ayr is a coastal reserve with dunes, saltmarsh, and mudflats. It is a vital habitat for migrant waders, warblers, and raptors. Visitors can also spot natterjack toads and sand lizards within its diverse ecosystems.

Name: RSPB Dove Stone (Blanket Bog Recovery)
Description: RSPB Dove Stone, near Greenfield, Oldham, is a partnership site focusing on blanket bog restoration within the Peak District National Park. Conservation work improves water quality, biodiversity, and carbon capture in the South Pennines.

Name: RSPB Fairhaven Lake (Coastal Hub)
Description: RSPB Fairhaven Lake is a coastal hub on the Ribble Estuary, Lancashire. The RSPB manages this site, focusing on nature conservation. It supports diverse bird species, including overwintering waders and wildfowl. The area offers various outdoor activities.

Name: RSPB Farnham Heath (Heathland Restoration)
Description: RSPB Farnham Heath is a restored heathland site in Surrey, managed by the RSPB. It features a mosaic of open heath, woodland, and ponds, providing habitats for rare birds like woodlark and nightjar, as well as sand lizards and slow worms. Visitors can explore walking trails.

Name: RSPB Fowlmere (Chalk Stream Fed)
Description: RSPB Fowlmere is a chalk stream fed wetland reserve in Cambridgeshire, offering habitats for diverse wildlife. Reedbeds and open water support breeding kingfishers, garganey, and water rails. Water voles thrive here. Accessible trails provide visitor access.

Name: RSPB Frampton Marsh (The Wash)
Description: Frampton Marsh is an RSPB nature reserve on the Lincolnshire coast, part of The Wash estuary. It features saltmarsh, freshwater scrapes, and reedbeds. This site provides vital feeding and breeding grounds for thousands of migratory and resident birds.

Name: RSPB Grange Farm (Wildlife Friendly Farming)
Description: RSPB Otmoor is a significant nature reserve in Oxfordshire, forming part of a wider, traditionally farmed basin. It focuses on habitat creation and restoration for wading birds and other wildlife, demonstrating wildlife-friendly farming practices.

Name: RSPB Havergate Island (Avocet Stronghold)
Description: Havergate Island is the RSPB's only island reserve, home to avocets, terns and gulls. Accessible only by boat, it features saline lagoons and mudflats, providing vital habitat for wading birds and other wildlife. Guided visits are offered.

Name: RSPB Haweswater (Landscape Partner)
Description: RSPB Haweswater is an upland nature reserve in the Lake District, managed with United Utilities. The site focuses on habitat restoration and protecting species such as golden eagles and red squirrels. Various walking trails are available.

Name: RSPB Hazeley Heath (North Hampshire)
Description: Hazeley Heath is an important area of lowland heathland in Hampshire, providing a home for Dartford warblers, woodlarks, and nightjars. It features heather, gorse, and birch, offering a diverse habitat. The site is actively managed for nature conservation.

Name: RSPB Hesketh Out Marsh (Managed Realignment)
Description: RSPB Hesketh Out Marsh is a managed realignment site on the Ribble Estuary. Formerly farmland, it transformed into intertidal saltmarsh and mudflat, supporting diverse wading birds and wildfowl, especially in winter. Saline lagoons and ditches are present.

Name: RSPB Lake Vyrnwy (Blanket Bog & Wood)
Description: RSPB Lake Vyrnwy covers over 9,000 hectares in Mid-Wales. It features upland blanket bog, woodlands, and a reservoir, supporting various bird species like peregrine falcons and merlins. Habitat restoration is a key conservation focus here.

Name: RSPB Lakenheath Fen (Wetland Creation)
Description: RSPB Lakenheath Fen is a large wetland reserve in Suffolk, England. Created from arable land, it features reedbeds, wet grasslands, and open water, attracting diverse wildlife including cranes, bitterns, and marsh harriers. Trails and hides are available.

Name: RSPB Langstone Harbour (Solent Islands)
Description: RSPB Langstone Harbour protects vital islands in South East England for nesting seabirds and waders. This important Ramsar site supports overwintering bird populations. Public access is limited to the harbour's shoreline, offering observation points.

Name: RSPB Leighton Moss (Coastal Interface)
Description: RSPB Leighton Moss is the largest reedbed in north-west England, near Silverdale. It hosts diverse wildlife, including otters, bearded tits, and marsh harriers. The reserve features accessible trails, hides, and a visitor centre. Coastal habitats are present, supporting a variety of bird species.

Name: RSPB Lyddon Meadow (Kent Downland)
Description: Lyddon Meadow is a chalk grassland reserve near Lydden, Dover, within the Kent Downs AONB. This site supports a rich diversity of wildflowers and insects, including the Adonis blue butterfly. Conservation efforts focus on maintaining its unimproved grassland habitat.

Name: RSPB Marshside (Ribble Estuary)
Description: Marshside is a coastal reserve on the Ribble estuary, featuring a mosaic of saltmarsh and wet grassland. It attracts thousands of wintering ducks, geese, and wading birds. Two hides offer views of species like avocets, lapwings, and golden plovers.

Name: RSPB North Warren (Suffolk Coast)
Description: North Warren is an RSPB reserve on the Suffolk coast, featuring a mosaic of woodland, reedbed, grassland, and heath habitats. It supports woodlarks, nightjars, Dartford warblers, and diverse invertebrates. Visitors can explore marked trails and observe the wildlife.

Name: RSPB Northward Hill (Heronry & Woodland)
Description: RSPB Northward Hill hosts the UK's largest heronry, alongside extensive ancient woodland, grazing marsh, and arable land. This Medway Estuary reserve supports diverse wildlife, including breeding herons, egrets, and raptors, offering walking trails.

Name: RSPB Old Hall Marshes (Blackwater Estuary)
Description: RSPB Old Hall Marshes spans extensive saltmarsh and grazing marsh by the Blackwater Estuary, Essex. It provides vital habitat for thousands of wintering ducks, geese, and waders. Spring sees important breeding waders. Several hides offer birdwatching opportunities across the landscape.

Name: RSPB Otmoor (Wetland Mosaic)
Description: RSPB Otmoor is a wetland mosaic in Oxfordshire, created from former arable land. It provides habitat for wading birds such as snipe and lapwing, and supports numerous wintering wildfowl. Reedbeds are home to marsh harriers and bitterns, making it a key site for birdwatching.

Name: RSPB Ouse Washes (Floodplain Wetland)
Description: RSPB Ouse Washes is a significant floodplain wetland in Cambridgeshire, providing crucial habitat for wintering wildfowl and breeding waders. The reserve features wet grassland, ditches, and reedbeds, playing a vital role in flood storage for the region.

Name: RSPB Point of Ayr (Dee Mouth)
Description: Point of Ayr is a vital high-tide roost for waders and wildfowl at the Dee Estuary mouth. Coastal marsh, saltmarsh, and sand dunes provide habitats. It attracts gulls, terns, and wintering hen harriers. Located on Wales' northernmost tip.

Name: RSPB Pulborough Brooks (Arun Valley)
Description: RSPB Pulborough Brooks is a wetland nature reserve in the Arun Valley, West Sussex. It comprises wet grasslands, woodlands, and heathland, providing habitat for diverse birdlife including ducks, geese, and waders. Visitors can explore nature trails and hides.

Name: RSPB Purfleet Environment and Education Centre
Description: RSPB Rainham Marshes is a significant nature reserve located in Purfleet, Essex, protecting diverse habitats including extensive marshland, reedbeds, and grasslands. It supports numerous bird species, insects, and water voles. The reserve features well-maintained walking trails, multiple bird hides, and a modern visitor centre offering educational programmes and wildlife observation opportunities. It focuses on vital conservation efforts and public access to nature.

Name: RSPB Ribble Discovery Centre
Description: The Ribble Discovery Centre, on Lytham seafront, offers information about the Ribble Estuary's wildlife and conservation. Visitors explore interactive displays and events, learning about this vital National Nature Reserve. It serves as a gateway to understanding the estuary's ecology.

Name: RSPB Rye Meads (Herts Wetland)
Description: Rye Meads is a wetland reserve on the River Lea, offering diverse habitats like reedbeds and wet grassland. It provides excellent birdwatching opportunities with several hides, making it a key site for waterfowl and migrating birds in Hertfordshire.

Name: RSPB Saltholme (Industrial Heartland)
Description: RSPB Saltholme is a nature reserve on Teesside, between Stockton-on-Tees and Hartlepool. The site features wetlands and grasslands, providing crucial habitats for diverse bird species and wildlife. Visitors explore trails, hides, and a visitor centre.

Name: RSPB Snettisham (The Wash Spectacle)
Description: RSPB Snettisham is a coastal reserve on The Wash in Norfolk, known for its large numbers of wading birds. Thousands of knots, oystercatchers, and godwits feed on the mudflats. High tides push vast flocks closer to the shoreline and hides, creating a spectacular natural event.

Name: RSPB South Stack Cliffs (Anglesey Seabirds)
Description: South Stack Cliffs, near Holyhead, is an RSPB reserve on Anglesey, North Wales. It features dramatic cliffs and heathland, home to guillemots, razorbills, and puffins during breeding season. Visitors observe seabirds and choughs from viewing points.

Name: RSPB St Aidan's (Reclaimed Open-Cast Mine)
Description: RSPB St Aidan's is a 400-hectare wetland reserve near Leeds, built on a former open-cast coal mine. It features reedbeds, lakes, and grasslands, providing habitat for various bird species and offering visitor walking routes.

Name: RSPB St Bees Head
Description: St Bees Head is England's only cliff-nesting colony of seabirds, located on the Cumbrian coast. It offers stunning views, significant geological features, and a thriving wildlife habitat for species like guillemots and razorbills.

Name: RSPB Strumpshaw Fen (The Broads)
Description: RSPB Strumpshaw Fen is a significant wetland reserve in the Broads, Norfolk. It features extensive reedbeds, grazing marsh, and wet woodland, supporting rare wildlife. Visitors can explore trails, hides, and an accessible boardwalk, observing marsh harriers, bitterns, and swallowtail butterflies. The site focuses on conservation and habitat management.

Name: RSPB Sutton Common
Description: Sutton Common is a small reserve, important for its wet woodland and fen habitats. It supports a variety of wildlife, including water voles, dragonflies, and many bird species. The site features a circular trail and a viewing screen, offering quiet observation opportunities.

Name: RSPB Tetney Marshes (Humber Estuary)
Description: Tetney Marshes is a coastal reserve on the Humber Estuary. It consists of saltmarsh, mudflats, and sand dunes, providing habitats for various wading birds and wildfowl, especially during migration periods. The site is a Ramsar wetland of international importance.

Name: RSPB Titchwell Marsh (North Norfolk Coast)
Description: RSPB Titchwell Marsh is a coastal wetland reserve in Norfolk, offering diverse habitats from reedbeds and saltmarsh to a sandy beach. It provides a vital stop-over for migratory birds and supports various resident species throughout the year.

Name: RSPB Tudeley Woods (Ancient Wealden Wood)
Description: Tudeley Woods is a diverse ancient woodland near Tonbridge, Kent, featuring ancient woodland, meadows, and new tree plantations. It supports hazel dormice, bats, butterflies, nightingales, and woodcocks. The reserve is managed for biodiversity conservation.

Name: RSPB Wallasea Island (Wild Coast Project)
Description: Wallasea Island in Essex is a significant wetland creation project, converting arable land into vital saltmarsh and mudflats. It provides crucial habitats for wading birds and waterfowl. Visitors can explore the diverse coastal landscape via footpaths and cycle routes, observing a range of wildlife.

Name: RSPB Wolves Wood (Ancient Woodland)
Description: Wolves Wood is an ancient woodland reserve in Suffolk, managed by the RSPB. It features a diverse mix of tree species, including oak, ash, and hazel, supporting varied wildlife. Accessible trails allow visitors to explore the peaceful habitats.

Name: Ruewood (Pasture & Ponds)
Description: Ruewood features wildflower-rich pasture, ponds, and wet woodland near Hinstock. The ponds support amphibians and dragonflies, while Belted Galloway cattle graze the pastures. This diverse site provides habitats for various wildlife species throughout the year.

Name: Rufford Country Park
Description: Rufford Country Park features the historic Rufford Abbey ruins, gardens, and woodland walks. Visitors can explore a lake, craft centre, and various outdoor activities. The park provides family-friendly events and educational opportunities.

Name: Rufford Old Hall (Woodland & River Douglas)
Description: Rufford Old Hall is a Grade I listed National Trust property in Lancashire, featuring a Tudor great hall with Victorian and Edwardian wings. Surrounded by extensive gardens, the estate includes woodland leading to the River Douglas, offering peaceful walks and showcasing arms and armour.

Name: Ruggin
Description: Ruggin is a small, diverse nature reserve nestled in a steep valley. It features wet woodland, ancient hedgerows, and species-rich unimproved grassland. Rare plants like meadow saffron thrive alongside buzzards and dormice. The stream supports various invertebrates.

Name: Ruislip Woods National Nature Reserve
Description: Ruislip Woods National Nature Reserve is a significant ancient woodland in the London Borough of Hillingdon. It encompasses Mad Bess Wood, Copse Wood, Manor Farm, and Park Wood. The site features diverse habitats, supporting varied flora and fauna.

Name: Runfold Wood
Description: Runfold Wood is an ancient woodland reserve near Farnham, Surrey. Coppiced hazel and hornbeam, with oak and birch, create a habitat for diverse flora and fauna, including rare butterflies and fungi. It is managed for conservation.

Name: Rush Furlong
Description: Rush Furlong is a 2-hectare nature reserve in Lincolnshire. An old arable field, it has naturally regenerated to grassland since 1993, following the removal of a piggery. Managed by Dexter cattle grazing, it supports wildflowers and borders wet grassland and the River Glen.

Name: Rushy Mead
Description: Rushy Mead is a small, wet meadow nature reserve in Essex, managed by Essex Wildlife Trust. It features species-rich grassland, providing a habitat for diverse flora and fauna, including various grasses, sedges, and wildflowers. The site supports a range of insects and bird species throughout the year.

Name: Russia Dock Woodland (Urban Ecology)
Description: Russia Dock Woodland in Rotherhithe, London, is a diverse urban woodland and ecological park managed by The Conservation Volunteers. Weekly volunteer sessions maintain its wildlife habitats. It offers green space for local communities and supports urban biodiversity.

Name: Rutland Water Nature Reserve
Description: Rutland Water Nature Reserve, managed by Leicestershire and Rutland Wildlife Trust, is a 1,000-acre site of international importance for wetland birds. Designated SSSI and RAMSAR, it features diverse habitats including lagoons and woodlands. It serves as a crucial area for bird migration and wintering wildfowl.

Name: Rye Harbour
Description: Rye Harbour Nature Reserve is a significant wildlife site on the UK's south coast. This diverse shingle beach and wetland habitat supports over 4,000 plant and animal species, including many rare birds and insects. Visitors can explore accessible paths and several bird hides.

Name: Rye Harbour Nature Reserve (Flagship Coastal)
Description: Rye Harbour Nature Reserve is a 465-hectare site in East Sussex, featuring brackish lagoons, saltmarsh, shingle, and freshwater habitats. It is home to over 4,200 species, with more than 300 being rare or endangered. The reserve is a significant location for bird migration and breeding.

Name: Rye Meads (managed interface)
Description: Rye Meads is a wetland reserve beside the River Lea, offering diverse habitats for wildlife near Hoddesdon, Hertfordshire. It features wet grassland, reedbeds, and open water, attracting numerous bird species and invertebrates. The site is managed by Herts and Middlesex Wildlife Trust.

Name: Rymes Reedbed (Restoration Site)
Description: Rymes Reedbed is a 4-hectare wetland restoration site, formerly an arable field. Situated west of RSPB Lakenheath Fen, it connects to the Great Fen landscape, providing valuable habitat for waterbirds and other reedbed specialists.

Name: Ryton Wood
Description: Ryton Wood is an ancient woodland, once part of the Forest of Arden. It features mature oaks, coppiced hazel, and a rich ground flora, including bluebells and wood anemones. The site supports diverse bird species, invertebrates, and mammals.

Name: Salcey Forest (Ancient Medieval Hunting Forest)
Description: Salcey Forest is an ancient woodland in Northamptonshire, home to towering trees and accessible trails. Visitors enjoy walking, cycling, and the treetop walkway, with facilities including a café. It preserves a rich natural habitat.

Name: Sale Water Park (Trafford)
Description: Sale Water Park is a large country park in Sale, Greater Manchester, covering 152 acres around a lake. It offers water sports, walking trails, and diverse wildlife habitats for visitors to explore and enjoy.

Name: Sallowsprings (Hay Meadow)
Description: Sallowsprings is a small woodland in Stibbington, Cambridgeshire. It features diverse native trees and ancient hedgerows, managed to create varied habitats for local wildlife. The reserve supports a range of species.

Name: Salmon Pastures (Industrial Riverside Habitat)
Description: Salmon Pastures is a small, linear nature reserve, once a densely developed industrial site. It now provides a vital riverside habitat along the River Don in Sheffield, featuring woodlands, reedbeds, and wildflower meadows, supporting diverse wildlife.

Name: Salt Lake Quarry (Wild Ingleborough)
Description: Salt Lake Quarry Nature Reserve, a former limestone quarry near Ingleborough, offers grassland, scrub, and woodland habitats. It supports diverse wildflowers, insects, and birds, alongside significant geological features.

Name: Saltbox Hill
Description: Saltbox Hill is a mosaic of chalk grassland, ancient woodland, and scrub on a steep hillside in southeast London. It hosts orchids, butterflies, and diverse bird species. Managed for biodiversity, it supports a rich ecosystem.

Name: Saltburn Gill
Description: Saltburn Gill is an ancient semi-natural woodland valley, a Site of Special Scientific Interest near Saltburn-by-the-Sea. It supports diverse wildlife, including breeding birds and rich ground flora, with a stream flowing to the coast. The Tees Valley Wildlife Trust manages the reserve.

Name: Saltburn Gill (Ancient Woodland SSSI)
Description: Saltburn Gill is a steep-sided ancient woodland SSSI in North Yorkshire, leading to Saltburn-by-the-Sea. It features diverse flora, including uncommon species, and a range of birdlife. The Gill forms an important part of the Cleveland Way National Trail.

Name: Salthill Quarry (Geological SSSI)
Description: Salthill Quarry, a Geological SSSI in Clitheroe, showcases Carboniferous limestone beds formed 340 million years ago. It is rich in marine fossils, including brachiopods and corals, offering insights into ancient marine ecosystems.

Name: Salthill Quarry LNR
Description: Salthill Quarry, a former limestone quarry, is a Local Nature Reserve. This site features rare Carboniferous limestone pavements and supports diverse wildflowers and insects, including orchids and butterflies. It is managed for wildlife and educational purposes.

Name: Salthouse Marshes
Description: Salthouse Marshes, a Norfolk Wildlife Trust reserve, is located on the North Norfolk coast. It comprises shingle, saltmarsh, and freshwater pools, supporting diverse bird species including avocets, lapwings, and waders. It is a key habitat for coastal wildlife and offers birdwatching.

Name: Salthouse Marshes (Pools & Grazing)
Description: Salthouse Marshes is part of Norfolk Wildlife Trust's Cley and Salthouse reserve. It features grazing marsh and saline lagoons, supporting a diverse range of resident and migratory birds. The area is recognised for its importance as a Ramsar site and SSSI.

Name: Saltwells Nature Reserve (Ancient Woodland Hub)
Description: Saltwells Local Nature Reserve is 140 hectares of ancient woodland, grassland, and wetlands in Dudley, West Midlands. It features diverse habitats, including a geological SSSI, and is home to a variety of wildlife. A sculpture trail and visitor centre are present.

Name: Samphire Hoe (Restored Coastal Land)
Description: Samphire Hoe is 30 hectares of restored coastal land between Dover and Folkestone, created from Channel Tunnel excavations. It opened in 1997 and is now an internationally recognised nature reserve, a haven for diverse wildlife, and offers recreational and educational opportunities.

Name: Samson (Uninhabited Heritage Core)
Description: Samson is the largest uninhabited island of Scilly, comprising two hills joined by a sandy isthmus. Managed by Isles of Scilly Wildlife Trust in partnership, it shows clear evidence of prehistoric and post-medieval human activity. The island supports important breeding seabirds and rare plants.

Name: Sand Point & Middle Hope
Description: Sand Point and Middle Hope is a coastal headland on the Bristol Channel. This unprotected site offers walks, diverse wildlife, and geological interest. Maintained by the National Trust, it provides public access with views across to South Wales.

Name: Sandilands (New Coastal Reserve)
Description: Sandilands is a coastal reserve in Lincolnshire, managed by the National Trust. It features dunes, saltmarsh, and freshwater scrapes, providing habitats for natterjack toads and migratory birds. The site is undergoing ecological restoration and offers visitor paths.

Name: Sandilands Pit
Description: Sandilands Pit, a former sand and gravel quarry, is now a wetland nature reserve featuring open water, reedbeds, and wet woodland. It supports diverse wildlife, including migratory birds, dragonflies, and amphibians, with tranquil paths.

Name: Sandlings Forests (Aldeburgh/Snape blocks)
Description: Sandlings Forests include areas near Aldeburgh and Snape, forming part of the larger Sandlings Special Protection Area. These coniferous and broadleaf woodlands support rare species like nightjar and woodlark. Visitors can enjoy walking and cycling trails.

Name: Sandlings Heaths and Forest
Description: The Sandlings Heaths and Forests form a unique landscape of ancient heaths and commercial forestry along the Suffolk coast. This area provides vital habitats for rare species such as Nightjar, Woodlark, and the Silver-studded Blue butterfly.

Name: Sandown Meadows
Description: Sandown Meadows Nature Reserve on the Isle of Wight features wet grassland, ditches, fen, and reedbed within the Eastern Yar river floodplain. It supports water voles, great pampas grasshoppers, various birds, dragonflies, and beetles. The site is part of a wider wetland network.

Name: Sandown Meadows Nature Reserve (IOW)
Description: Sandown Meadows Nature Reserve is a freshwater grazing marsh on the Isle of Wight. It supports wetland birds like snipe and lapwing, alongside dragonflies and butterflies, including the rare marsh fritillary. Cattle graze the meadows for habitat management.

Name: Sandscale Haws NNR (Dune Slacks)
Description: Sandscale Haws NNR is a dynamic dune system near Barrow-in-Furness, Cumbria. It protects a unique landscape of dune slacks, saltmarsh, and intertidal sands, supporting rare natterjack toads and diverse plant life.

Name: Sandwell Valley (Connectivity Core)
Description: Sandwell Valley Country Park, a 660-acre Green Flag award-winner in the West Midlands, features two lakes, a golf course, and nature trails. It provides access to RSPB Sandwell Valley, Forge Mill Farm, and Sandwell Park Farm, offering various activities.

Name: Sandwich and Pegwell Bay (National Nature Reserve)
Description: Sandwich & Pegwell Bay NNR is a mosaic of coastal habitats including mudflats, saltmarsh, shingle, dunes, and lagoons. An internationally important wildlife site, it supports diverse species and is a National Nature Reserve in Kent.

Name: Sandylay
Description: Sandylay is a compact woodland and heathland reserve in south Essex. It features ancient woodland, primarily oak and hornbeam, alongside open heathland. The reserve supports a variety of flora and fauna, including rare invertebrates. It is managed by Essex Wildlife Trust for biodiversity conservation.

Name: Sandylay and Moat Woods
Description: Sandylay and Moat Woods is an ancient woodland in Essex, featuring diverse habitats like ponds and a moated homestead site. It supports dormice, great crested newts, and woodland birds. The site is managed to maintain its ecological and historical significance.

Name: Santon Downham (Thetford Forest)
Description: Santon Downham is a village situated within Thetford Forest in Suffolk. It offers direct access to walks along the River Little Ouse, features a village hall, and a historic church. The surrounding forest provides numerous trails for walking and cycling activities.

Name: Sapperton Valley (River Frome Corridor)
Description: Sapperton Valley forms part of the River Frome corridor, featuring ancient woodland, grassland, and the river itself, creating diverse habitats. The valley supports rich flora and fauna, including water voles, kingfishers, and various wildflowers. A former railway line forms a key path through the reserve.

Name: Sawbridgeworth Marsh
Description: Sawbridgeworth Marsh is a mosaic of wildflower meadows, scrub, and wet woodland beside the River Stort. The site supports diverse birdlife, including kingfishers and warblers, and various invertebrates. Grazing cattle maintain the species-rich grasslands. It is managed by Essex Wildlife Trust.

Name: Sayes Court (Pocket Park)
Description: Sayes Court is a historic garden site in Deptford, South East London, once home to John Evelyn. It features community gardens, orchards, and open green space, developed and maintained by local volunteers.

Name: Scalby Beck Nature Reserve (2026 Launch)
Description: Scalby Beck Nature Reserve is a 24-hectare site near Scarborough, North Yorkshire, launching in 2026. This project aims to enhance biodiversity, improve water quality, and provide flood protection along Scalby Beck. It offers public access.

Name: Scarning Fen (Rare Mosses)
Description: Scarning Fen is a small, species-rich valley fen in Norfolk, boasting diverse wetland habitats. It supports a variety of rare mosses, sedges, and wildflowers, alongside a rich insect life, including dragonflies. The reserve is managed for biodiversity.

Name: Scatwell
Description: Scatwell is a small woodland reserve near South Tawton, Dartmoor National Park. Managed by Devon Wildlife Trust, it features ancient oak and ash trees. The site supports diverse mosses, lichens, fungi, and various bird species. A stream flows through its damp environment.

Name: Scolt Head Island (NWT/NT Joint)
Description: Scolt Head Island is a dynamic barrier island on the north Norfolk coast, forming part of an unspoilt estuarine system. It features saltmarshes, sand dunes, and shingle ridges, providing vital habitats for diverse birdlife, including breeding terns and waders.

Name: Scolt Head Island (Partner)
Description: Scolt Head Island is a dynamic barrier island off the Norfolk coast, formed by sand and shingle. It features saltmarsh, sand dunes, and mudflats, supporting diverse birdlife including terns and waders. It is a National Nature Reserve.

Name: Scoska Wood (Rare NT Limestone Ash Wood)
Description: Scoska Wood is a limestone ash wood located near Semer Water in the Yorkshire Dales. A Site of Special Scientific Interest, it hosts rare flora and fauna, including ancient woodland indicator species.

Name: Scotney Castle Estate (Woodland & River Bewl)
Description: Scotney Castle is a historic country house with a medieval moated castle and gardens in Kent. The estate features extensive woodlands and the River Bewl within its wider landscape. Visitors explore picturesque grounds, varied walking routes, and local wildlife.

Name: Scotton Common
Description: Scotton Common is an acid grassland and heathland site in Lincolnshire, designated as a Site of Special Scientific Interest. It is surrounded by conifer plantations. Rare species like nightjar, woodlark, tree pipit, and adder are present, alongside diverse flora. The reserve is managed through grazing.

Name: Sculthorpe Moor (Hawk and Owl Trust)
Description: Sculthorpe Moor is a Norfolk nature reserve managed by the Hawk and Owl Trust. It features wetlands, woodlands, and reedbeds, supporting diverse bird species and wildlife. Accessible boardwalks and hides provide visitor exploration opportunities year-round.

Name: Scutchditch Wood
Description: Scutchditch Wood is a small, semi-ancient woodland in north Herefordshire. It features an array of ancient oak and hornbeam standards, with a ground flora of dog's mercury, wood anemone, and yellow archangel. Its mature trees host important lichen communities.

Name: Seaford Head (Chalk Grassland & Coast)
Description: Seaford Head is a Sussex Wildlife Trust nature reserve on the East Sussex coast, east of Seaford. It features chalk grassland, cliff habitats, and views of the Seven Sisters. Diverse wildlife, including fulmars, kittiwakes, peregrine falcons, and rare plants, inhabit the site. Footpaths provide public access.

Name: Seaforth Nature Reserve
Description: Seaforth Nature Reserve is an internationally important site for migratory birds on the Mersey Estuary. It provides vital habitat and feeding grounds near the Port of Liverpool. The site is a key location for birdwatching and conservation efforts.

Name: Seale Chalk Pit
Description: Seale Chalk Pit is a former chalk quarry now a nature reserve in Surrey. It features steep chalk faces and open grassland, providing a unique habitat. The pit supports diverse plant and insect species, including rare orchids and invertebrates.

Name: Seasalter Levels (Wetland Restoration)
Description: Seasalter Levels is a 130-hectare site near Whitstable, restored to wetland mosaic habitats. It provides key areas for breeding and wintering waterbirds, enhancing local biodiversity. The project also improves flood resilience and water quality in the area.

Name: Seata Quarry
Description: Seata Quarry is a small, species-rich limestone grassland nature reserve located in Nidderdale AONB, North Yorkshire. The site features diverse wildflowers and insects, including rare species, and is actively managed for biodiversity.

Name: Seatoller (Atlantic Oakwood)
Description: Seatoller, a hamlet in Borrowdale, hosts ancient Atlantic oakwoods clinging to steep valley sides in the Lake District. These temperate rainforests are a vital habitat, supporting numerous rare mosses, lichens, and ferns. The National Trust manages this important site.

Name: Seaton Cliffs
Description: Seaton Cliffs is a 6-hectare coastal reserve in East Lothian, Scotland. It features dramatic cliffs, pebble beaches, and coastal grassland. The site supports breeding seabirds in summer, and rare plants like the Scottish primrose thrive here.

Name: Sedge Fen (Ancient Undrained Fen)
Description: Sedge Fen is an ancient undrained fen, part of Wicken Fen National Nature Reserve in Cambridgeshire. It represents a rare glimpse into past fenland landscapes. This site supports a diverse mosaic of habitats and contributes to carbon capture.

Name: Selborne Common (Gilbert White's Woods)
Description: Ancient woodlands, wildflower meadows and the Zig-Zag path create a diverse landscape at Selborne Common. Managed by the National Trust, this site offers views across Selborne village and the surrounding countryside, reflecting Gilbert White's 18th-century observations. Public access is maintained.

Name: Selworthy Beacon & North Hill
Description: Selworthy Beacon and North Hill are high points within the Holnicote Estate, Somerset. The area offers expansive views over the Bristol Channel and Exmoor National Park. A network of footpaths and bridleways allows visitors to explore diverse habitats and observe wildlife such as red deer.

Name: Semer Water (Glacial Lake)
Description: Semer Water is North Yorkshire's second-largest natural lake, a relic from the last ice age. The reserve supports wintering wildfowl and rare aquatic plants. It features reedbeds, wet grassland, and diverse marginal vegetation, forming a rich mosaic of habitats.

Name: Seven Acres (Bolton/LWT Partner)
Description: Seven Acres is a linear woodland and meadow site following Astley Brook, forming a green corridor on Bolton's edge. It provides valuable wildlife habitat and walking opportunities. Managed by Lancashire Wildlife Trust and Bolton Council, volunteers aid its upkeep.

Name: Seven Barrows
Description: Seven Barrows is a tranquil nature reserve near Lambourn, Berkshire, featuring ancient Bronze Age barrows and chalk grassland. It supports wildflowers like clustered bellflower and pyramidal orchid, alongside various butterflies and moths, offering peaceful walks.

Name: Seven Sisters Country Park
Description: Seven Sisters Country Park is a 280-hectare nature reserve in East Sussex, featuring iconic chalk cliffs, river valley, and grassland. It offers walking, birdwatching, cycling, and canoeing. A visitor centre provides information about the park.

Name: Sevenoaks Wildlife Reserve
Description: Sevenoaks Wildlife Reserve, established 1956, is Kent Wildlife Trust's oldest site. It features open water, reedbeds, and woodlands, supporting diverse bird species and otters. Visitors use footpaths and hides.

Name: Severn Farm Pond (Urban Wildlife)
Description: Severn Farm Pond is an urban wildlife haven in Welshpool, featuring a large pond, grassland, scrub, and mature trees. The site supports diverse amphibians, insects, and birdlife, providing a valuable green space for local residents.

Name: Sewardstone Marsh
Description: Sewardstone Marsh is a large marshland area in the Lee Valley Regional Park, known for its diverse birdlife and wetland habitats. It features walking trails and provides important breeding grounds for various waterfowl and migratory species within a protected nature reserve.

Name: Sewell Cutting
Description: Sewell Cutting, near Dunstable, is an abandoned railway cutting since 1989. The chalk grassland supports many wildflowers like pyramidal and common spotted orchids, plus insects such as green hairstreak butterflies. Scrub areas add diverse habitats.

Name: Shabden Park
Description: Shabden Park is a 108-hectare nature reserve featuring ancient woodland, chalk grassland, and acid heathland, home to rare butterflies and birds. It offers walking routes through varied habitats, providing panoramic views across the Surrey countryside.

Name: Shadwell Wood (Oxlip Stronghold)
Description: Shadwell Wood, a Site of Special Scientific Interest near Saffron Walden, is an ancient woodland. It features hornbeam and hazel coppice with mature oak standards, and hosts one of the UK's largest oxlip colonies. The site is important for diverse flora.

Name: Shaptor Woods
Description: Shaptor Woods near Bovey Tracey has a mix of ancient semi-natural woodland, conifer plantation and regenerating broadleaf. The site is notable for its birdlife and is home to dormice. It features walking routes and is part of the East Dartmoor Woods and Heaths SSSI.

Name: Shapwick Heath NNR (Natural England Core)
Description: Shapwick Heath NNR, part of the Somerset Levels and Moors, is a diverse wetland mosaic. Habitats include reedbeds, fens, open water, grassland, and woodland. It supports otters, bitterns, and marsh harriers. The site holds Ramsar and SAC status.

Name: Shapwick Moor (Hawk & Owl Trust Partner)
Description: Shapwick Moor Nature Reserve, owned by the Hawk and Owl Trust, provides habitats for wintering and breeding wading birds, otters, owls, and raptors. It forms part of the Avalon Marshes landscape in Somerset.

Name: Sharnbrook Summit
Description: Sharnbrook Summit is a nature reserve in an old railway cutting on the former Midland main line. It features chalk grassland habitat, supporting various wildflowers and butterfly species, including common blue and marbled white. The site offers views over the Great Ouse Valley.

Name: Sharnbrook Summit and Wymington Meadow
Description: Sharnbrook Summit and Wymington Meadow is a former railway cutting and embankment, now managed for wildflowers and grassland butterflies. The reserve features species-rich chalk grassland and supports plants like cowslips, pyramid orchids, and common spotted orchids. Birds and small mammals also inhabit the area.

Name: Sharpenhoe (Chalk Escarpment)
Description: Sharpenhoe Chalk Escarpment is a distinctive landscape feature in the Chiltern Hills, offering panoramic views across Bedfordshire. The site includes woodlands and rare chalk grassland, supporting diverse wildlife. It is managed for nature conservation.

Name: Sharpham Moor
Description: Sharpham Moor is a lowland wet grassland and fenland nature reserve in Somerset, managed by Somerset Wildlife Trust. It protects rare plants like marsh fern and fen violet, alongside diverse invertebrates and bird species.

Name: Shaugh Bridge Woods
Description: Shaugh Bridge Woods is a diverse ancient woodland site near Plymouth, forming part of the wider Plym Valley Woods. It features various tree species, a river, and supports local wildlife, popular for walking and nature enjoyment.

Name: Shaw’s Corner (Wilderness Garden)
Description: Shaw's Corner, the former home of Irish playwright George Bernard Shaw in Hertfordshire, includes a Wilderness Garden. Visitors explore the preserved rooms and tranquil garden paths, gaining insight into Shaw's life and work. The site offers a historical and natural experience.

Name: Sheepleas
Description: Sheepleas is an ancient woodland and grassland site near East Horsley, Surrey. It is a mosaic of chalk grassland, scrub, and woodland, home to diverse butterflies and wildflowers. Managed for conservation.

Name: Sheepleas (North Downs Flagship)
Description: Sheepleas is a significant chalk downland nature reserve in Surrey. It features ancient woodlands, flower-rich grasslands, and scrub habitats. The site supports a diverse range of wildflowers, insects, and butterflies, providing important biodiversity within the North Downs.

Name: Shepperlands Farm
Description: Shepperlands Farm nature reserve is a tranquil woodland and wetland site near Wargrave, Berkshire. It features ancient woodland, coppiced hazel, ponds, and marshland, supporting diverse wildlife including wildflowers and butterflies. The site offers peaceful walking trails.

Name: Shepperlands Farm (Finchampstead)
Description: Shepperlands Farm is a tranquil oasis of ancient woodland, wet meadows and a pond. It supports a variety of wildlife, including dragonflies, bats, and rare plants. The reserve offers peaceful walks along maintained paths. Managed by BBOWT for conservation.

Name: Sherbourne Valley Project
Description: The Sherbourne Valley Project revitalises the River Sherbourne and its valley in Coventry, improving water quality, creating new habitats, and reconnecting communities. It focuses on a 12km stretch, restoring wet woodland, reedbed, and grasslands, boosting biodiversity and reducing flood risk.

Name: Sherburn Willows
Description: Sherburn Willows is a Yorkshire Wildlife Trust nature reserve in Sherburn-in-Elmet. This site provides a quiet haven for wildlife, supporting willow tit, brown hares, water voles, and grass snakes. It also attracts various dragonflies and butterflies.

Name: Shere Woodlands (Silent Pool area)
Description: Shere Woodlands comprises three ancient woodlands near Guildford, Surrey. It features ancient oak and hazel coppice, supporting diverse ground flora like bluebells. Rare birds such as woodcock and nightjar breed within the reserve.

Name: Sheringham Park (Coastal Woodland)
Description: Sheringham Park, near Sheringham, Norfolk, is a Humphry Repton-designed landscape park. It offers panoramic views of the North Norfolk coast. The park features diverse rhododendrons and azaleas, with waymarked trails through woodland and parkland.

Name: Sheringham Park (Nature Trails)
Description: Sheringham Park is a landscape park and garden near Sheringham, Norfolk, England. Managed by the National Trust, it features extensive tree and shrub collections, designed by Humphry Repton. Visitors can enjoy miles of footpaths, with diverse wildlife and panoramic views of the coast.

Name: Sheringham Park (Repton Landscape)
Description: Sheringham Park is a landscape park and garden in Norfolk, England, designed by Humphry Repton in 1812. It features expansive views of the North Norfolk coast, mature woodland, and a significant collection of rhododendrons and azaleas. Visitors can explore waymarked trails and enjoy the diverse plant life.

Name: Sherwood Forest National Nature Reserve
Description: Sherwood Forest National Nature Reserve spans over 450 hectares of woodland and heathland in Nottinghamshire. It protects England's largest collection of ancient oak trees, including the Major Oak, and supports diverse wildlife. The site is a significant cultural landscape linked to Robin Hood legends.

Name: Sherwood Forest Trust (Landscape Recovery)
Description: The Sherwood Forest Trust is a charity conserving, enhancing, and promoting the natural, cultural, and historic heritage of Nottinghamshire's Sherwood Forest area. It facilitates landscape-scale conservation, community engagement, and sustainable development across the ancient landscape.

Name: Sherwood Heath SSSI (County Council)
Description: Sherwood Heath SSSI is an ancient woodland and heathland near Clipstone, Nottinghamshire. It is managed by Nottinghamshire County Council. The site is a Site of Special Scientific Interest due to its rare acid grassland, heathland, and wetland habitats, which support diverse wildlife.

Name: Sherwood Pines Forest Park
Description: Sherwood Pines Forest Park is Nottinghamshire's largest publicly accessible forest, offering extensive cycle and walking trails. It features a visitor centre, play areas, Go Ape, and Segway adventures. The diverse woodland is home to a variety of wildlife.

Name: Shetas
Description: Shetas is a small ancient semi-natural woodland in West Dorset, bequeathed to Dorset Wildlife Trust in 1993. The site includes a valley with a stream and hazel coppice. It provides a habitat for various plants and insects and is managed for nature conservation.

Name: Shibdon Pond
Description: Shibdon Pond is a significant wetland in Tyne and Wear, vital for migratory birds and dragonflies. It features a mosaic of habitats including reedbed, scrub, and grassland. Over 180 bird species and 15 dragonfly species have been recorded here.

Name: Shipley Country Park (Derbyshire CC)
Description: Shipley Country Park spans 700 acres of parkland, lakes, and woodland near Derby and Nottingham. It offers walking, cycling, and horse riding trails. Facilities include a visitor centre, shop, craft units, and a children's play area.

Name: Shorne Woods Country Park
Description: Shorne Woods Country Park is a Green Flag award-winning site in Kent, featuring ancient woodland, lakes, and open spaces. It supports diverse wildlife habitats and offers visitor facilities, including a café and play areas, alongside educational activities.

Name: Short Wood and Southwick Wood
Description: Short Wood and Southwick Wood are ancient woodlands near Oundle, Northamptonshire. These reserves feature mature oak and ash trees, diverse ground flora, and support a variety of bird species and invertebrates. They are managed for biodiversity.

Name: Shrubhurst Wood
Description: Shrubhurst Wood is a small ancient woodland, part of the Arden Forest, featuring diverse broadleaved trees like oak, ash, and hazel. Its ground flora includes bluebells, wood anemone, and yellow archangel, supporting woodland birds and invertebrates. A stream runs through the site.

Name: Shugborough Estate (900-acre Parkland)
Description: Shugborough Estate features a 900-acre parkland, formal gardens, and a working Georgian farm in Staffordshire. The historic mansion and collections are managed by Staffordshire County Council, while the National Trust cares for the wider estate. Visitors explore history and nature.

Name: Shut Heath Wood
Description: Shut Heath Wood is an ancient woodland nature reserve in north Essex, near Great Totham. It features a mix of broadleaf trees like oak, hornbeam, and hazel, with diverse ground flora including primroses, bluebells, and wood anemones. Managed for wildlife conservation.

Name: Shut Heath Wood (Bluebell Wood)
Description: Shut Heath Wood, near Great Totham, is an ancient woodland renowned for its spring bluebells. The reserve features mature oak and hornbeam trees, providing a habitat for diverse woodland flora and bird species. It offers a tranquil setting for wildlife conservation.

Name: Shutpaddock (Ancient Woodland)
Description: Shutpaddock is a small ancient woodland nature reserve in Hampshire, managed by Hampshire and Isle of Wight Wildlife Trust. It features mature trees, diverse flora, and supports various bird species. The site exemplifies semi-natural woodland.

Name: Shutts Copse
Description: Shutts Copse is an ancient woodland nature reserve in Hampshire, bordering Fareham. It features mature oak, ash, hazel, and field maple trees, alongside a rich ground flora with bluebells and wild garlic. The site is managed for biodiversity and conservation.

Name: Siccaridge Wood (Ancient Beech & Dormice)
Description: Siccaridge Wood is an ancient beech woodland in the Stroud Valleys, providing habitat for nationally important populations of hazel dormice. The site features steep slopes and deep valleys, supporting diverse flora, including various fungi and orchids.

Name: Sideland (Spring Flower Woodland)
Description: Sideland, managed by Radnorshire Wildlife Trust, protects tranquil woodland and meadows in the Usk Valley. Famous for carpets of spring flowers like bluebells, it provides vital habitat for diverse wildlife. Well-maintained walking trails are available.

Name: Sidmouth & Salcombe Hill
Description: Sidmouth and Salcombe Hill span a dramatic East Devon coastline section, part of the Jurassic Coast. Visitors enjoy clifftop walks with far-reaching views across Lyme Bay and the Area of Outstanding Natural Beauty. The dynamic landscape features coastal heathland, woodland, and grassland, subject to ongoing erosion. Explore Sidmouth Esplanade.

Name: Sidneys Paddock
Description: Sidneys Paddock is a wildflower meadow and small wetland managed by Nottinghamshire Wildlife Trust near Retford. It provides a habitat for various insects, birds, and plant species including ragged robin and marsh marigold, supporting local biodiversity.

Name: Silent Valley (Ancient Beech Wood)
Description: Silent Valley is an ancient woodland near Cwmbran, Torfaen, home to mature beech trees and diverse ground flora including bluebells and wild garlic. The reserve supports various bird species and invertebrates, with a stream flowing through its valley floor.

Name: Silk Stream (Action for Silk Stream)
Description: The Action for Silk Stream project restores the Silk Stream and its tributaries in Barnet and Harrow. It improves water quality, enhances biodiversity, increases flood resilience, and creates engaging public green spaces. The project is delivered by Thames21.

Name: Silk Wood - Forest of Dean
Description: Silk Wood is a naturalistic woodland within Westonbirt Arboretum, managed for conservation and research. It features diverse native and exotic trees, rich flora, and wildlife habitats, offering public access.

Name: Silverdale & Arnside (NT holdings)
Description: The National Trust protects diverse landscapes across Arnside and Silverdale, an Area of Outstanding Natural Beauty. This includes coastal marshes, limestone hills, ancient woodlands, and rich wildflower meadows, supporting a variety of wildlife.

Name: Silverhill Wood (Former Colliery)
Description: Silverhill Wood, near Teversal and Sutton in Ashfield, is a former colliery site transformed into a thriving woodland. It features a conical spoil heap, now Nottinghamshire's highest point, offering panoramic views. The site provides various walking trails and is home to diverse wildlife and plants.

Name: Silverines Meadow
Description: Silverines Meadow is a vibrant wildflower meadow near Scopwick, Lincolnshire. Managed by the Lincolnshire Wildlife Trust, this site supports a diverse range of plant species, including common spotted orchid and cowslip, attracting various insects and birds throughout the year.

Name: Silvermeade
Description: Silvermeade is a green space in Tottenham, part of Lee Valley Regional Park. Adjacent to the River Lee Navigation, it offers walks and wildlife spotting, including dragonflies, herons, and water voles. It features a modern children's play area, ideal for families and nature discovery.

Name: Simpson's Saltings
Description: Simpson's Saltings is a coastal nature reserve near Ramsholt in Suffolk. It features saltmarsh, mudflats, and a shingle beach, supporting various bird species including avocets and Brent geese. The site is important for waders and wildfowl, especially during migration.

Name: Sissinghurst Castle Estate (Nature Trails)
Description: Sissinghurst Castle Estate offers diverse nature trails across its wider landscape of woodlands, wetlands, and farmland. Visitors explore footpaths through ancient woods and along waterways, observing local wildlife. The estate is a key part of the Kent countryside.

Name: Sissinghurst Castle Garden (Lakes & Estate)
Description: Sissinghurst Castle Garden in Kent, England, features romantic, intricate gardens designed by Vita Sackville-West and Harold Nicolson. The estate includes a medieval tower, farm buildings, and a renowned plant collection, offering diverse garden rooms and woodland paths.

Name: Sizergh Estate (Wetland & Wood)
Description: Sizergh Estate, near Kendal, Cumbria, combines a medieval house with extensive gardens, orchards, wetlands, and woodlands. Managed by the National Trust, it is home to diverse wildlife and offers various walking routes. Conservation work focuses on habitat restoration.

Name: Skiddaw Forest (High Fell Project)
Description: Skiddaw Forest is a remote upland area in the northern Lake District, managed by Cumbria Wildlife Trust.
